Title: Freelance Data Science Engineer (Python & SQL)
Description:
Please submit your CV in English and indicate your level of English proficiency.

Mindrift connects specialists with project-based AI opportunities for leading tech companies, focused on testing, evaluating, and improving AI systems. Participation is project-based, not permanent employment.

What This Opportunity Involves

While each project involves unique tasks, contributors may:


Design original computational data science problems that simulate real-world analytical workflows across industries (telecom, finance, government, e-commerce, healthcare)
Create problems requiring Python programming to solve (using Pandas, Numpy, Scipy, Sklearn, Statsmodels, Matplotlib, Seaborn)
Ensure problems are computationally intensive and cannot be solved manually within reasonable timeframes (days/weeks)
Develop problems requiring non-trivial reasoning chains in data processing, statistical analysis, feature engineering, predictive modeling, and insight extraction
Create deterministic problems with reproducible answers: avoid stochastic elements or require fixed random seeds for exact reproducibility
Base problems on real business challenges: customer analytics, risk assessment, fraud detection, forecasting, optimization, and operational efficiency
Design end-to-end problems spanning the complete data science pipeline (data ingestion → cleaning → EDA → modeling → validation → deployment considerations)
Incorporate big data processing scenarios requiring scalable computational approaches
Verify solutions using Python with standard data science libraries and statistical methods
Document problem statements clearly with realistic business contexts and provide verified correct answers


What We Look For

This opportunity is a good fit for Data Science specialists with an experience in python open to part-time, non-permanent projects. Ideally, contributors will have:


5+ years of hands-on data science experience with proven business impact
Portfolio of completed projects and publications showcasing real-world problem-solving
Expert Python programming for data science (pandas, numpy, scipy, scikit-learn, statsmodels)
Expert statistical analysis and machine learning - deep understanding of algorithms, methods, and their practical applications
Expert with SQL and database operations for data manipulation and analysis
Experience with GenAI technologies (LLMs, RAG, prompt engineering, vector databases)
Understanding of MLOps practices and model deployment workflows
Knowledge of modern frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch, LangChain)
Strong written English (C1+)


How It Works

Apply → Pass qualification(s) → Join a project → Complete tasks → Get paid

Project time expectations

For this project, tasks are estimated to require around 10-20 hours per week during active phases, based on project requirements. This is an estimate, not a guaranteed workload, and applies only while the project is active.

Compensation

On this project, contributors can earn up to $58 per hour equivalent, depending on their level and pace of contribution.

Compensation varies across projects depending on scope, complexity, and required expertise. Please note that other projects on the platform may offer different earning levels based on their requirements.

Title: Associate, Primary Research
Description:
Application deadline 10 June 2026




WHAT MAKES US A GREAT PLACE TO WORK

We are proud to be consistently recognized as one of the world’s best places to work. We are currently the top ranked consulting firm on Glassdoor’s Best Places to Work list and have earned the #1 overall spot a record seven times. Extraordinary teams are at the heart of our business strategy, but these don’t happen by chance. They require intentional focus on bringing together a broad set of backgrounds, cultures, experiences, perspectives, and skills in a supportive and inclusive work environment. We hire people with exceptional talent and create an environment in which every individual can thrive professionally and personally.




WHO YOU’LL WORK WITH


You’ll join our Research and Design experts within the AI, Insights & Solutions team. This team is part of Bain’s digital capabilities practice, which includes experts in analytics, engineering, product management, research and design. In this multidisciplinary environment, you'll leverage deep research expertise with business acumen to help clients tackle their most transformative challenges. You’ll work on integrated teams alongside our general consultants and clients to develop data-driven strategies and innovative solutions. Together, we create human-centric solutions that harness the power of data and artificial intelligence to drive competitive advantage for our clients. Our collaborative and supportive work environment fosters creativity and continuous learning, enabling us to consistently deliver exceptional results.

 

WHERE YOU’LL FIT WITHIN THE TEAM
 As a Associate Primary Researcher you will work closely with Bain project teams – across strategy, value proposition design, customer experience – as well as the Private Equity Group and clients. You’ll design and deliver high-quality primary research, combining quantitative and qualitative approaches to generate actionable insights that shape critical business decisions. 

You’ll also be part of a team dedicated to evolving our primary research capabilities, helping redefine how we generate insights through innovative approaches – including the use of human-AI collaboration to deliver faster, deeper, and more actionable results.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

Provide primary market research and user research expertise to Bain case teams and clients globally, although with more focus on Europe and the Middle East. As an Associate, Primary Research, you will support the full end-to-end research process – from designing the right approach (qualitative, quantitative or mixed methods), data collection and insight synthesis. Your work will translate research findings into clear, actionable insights that directly inform strategic recommendations and drive business impact.
Apply a range of research methods and analytical techniques to address key business questions – such as customer implications for strategy, customer acquisition and retention, new product development and pricing, customer experience design and engagement,  brand strategy and messaging, go-to-market strategy, among others. 
Train and coach AIS colleagues and consultants on primary research procedures.
Take ownership of the end-to-end research to insight process – from defining the research approach to delivering actionable insights. 
Stay at the forefront of emerging and cutting-edge research methodologies and tools
Develop relationships with external research vendors and interact with as needed at the project level
Codify, document and share best practice experience with Bain’s consulting team to enhance the value of primary research / user research
Leverage AI-enabled research tools and workflows to enhance research design, data synthesis, insight generation, and scalability of primary research delivery

 

ABOUT YOU

1-2 years of experience with primary market research or user research experience in a business, market research or social science environment 
Master’s Degree or PhD in market research, sociology, ethnography or related field is a plus
Expertise in quantitative research methodologies, knowledgeable with qualitative research methodologies
Experience in using statistics to analyze quantitative research data such as significant test, sample reweighting, regression and clustering, segmentation, conjoint analysis etc.
Champion the voice of customer / user
Strong interpersonal, communication and storytelling skills
Must thrive in a fast paced environment and be able to work independently 
Fluency in English is required, along with business-level proficiency in the local language of the office location.
Interest or experience in applying AI-enabled tools, automation, or generative AI technologies within research, insights, or analytics workflows

Title: (Senior) Data Analyst
Description:
As a (Senior) Data Analyst at Nelly, you'll transform fragmented health and finance data into scalable, trustworthy models, creating a single source of truth that powers company-wide strategic decisions. Your role goes beyond answering business questions: you'll own the core analytics logic, challenge requirements, and define how we measure business health.

About Nelly

At Nelly, we’re revolutionizing medical practice management with AI-driven automation and seamless digital workflows. Our innovative solutions - from best-in-class patient journeys, AI enabled patient relationship management and billing to state of the art payment solutions - eliminate painful workflows and improve financial outcomes making healthcare administration smarter, faster, and 100% digital.

To fuel our mission, we just raised €50M in our Series B, backed by top-tier investors like Cathay, Notion, Lakestar, GFC, b2venture, and Motive Partners. With this momentum, we’re on a mission to build Europe’s largest fintech in healthcare - bringing best-in-class financial and operational solutions to over 1 million medical practices serving millions of patients daily.

Join us now if you’re driven by impact, excited by fast-paced innovation, and ready to shape the future of healthcare!

Joining us, you will


Design, build, and maintain scalable data models using dbt and strong SQL, with a focus on robust data marts and a clean semantic layer
Own the BI layer (Looker/LookML) end-to-end, creating self-service Explores that give the entire company a single source of truth for analysis
Translate complex business logic into structured data models, including topics such as Customer Health Scores, NRR (cohort-based), CAC Payback, product unit economics, churn, provisions, and commercial KPIs.
Unify and harmonize data across systems such as Salesforce, Product Data, HubSpot, Jira, Intercom, Personio, and internal booking/factoring systems.
Develop end-to-end reporting solutions from requirement gathering and data validation to Looker dashboards and stakeholder-ready reports.


Build advanced analyses such as:


Sales funnel and GTM performance tracking
Commercial KPI automation
Cohort-based NRR, CAC payback and churn analytics
Revenue reconciliation (Signed vs. Actual Revenue)
Customer and usage benchmarks (including regional usage / booking patterns)


Ensuring data quality and cross-functional alignment:


Identify gaps in data collection and instrumentation before modeling begins, ensuring quality, consistency, and long-term scalability
Establish tests and validation logic in dbt to guarantee reliability and transparency across data models
Collaborate closely with Product, Commercial and Finance to deeply understand the processes generating the data
Support system alignment between tools to ensure consistent reporting logic across the organization


What You'll Bring


Strong hands-on experience with SQL, modular query design and dimensional data modeling (star schemas, fact/dimension design).
Production experience designing and maintaining scalable data models using modern transformation tools (e.g. dbt, SQLMesh, Metabase), including testing and documentation.
Experience developing SQL-based data marts and self-service explores BI-tool with modeling capabilities (e.g. Looker)
Solid understanding of revenue metrics (MRR, NRR, churn, cohort logic), SaaS KPIs, and GTM performance metrics.
Experience integrating and modeling data from tools such as Salesforce, HubSpot, CRM systems, and financial systems.
Strong stakeholder management skills and the ability to translate ambiguous business questions into structured analytical frameworks.
A proactive mindset - you question definitions, challenge inconsistencies, and improve processes instead of just reporting on them.
Fluency in SQL; Experience in dbt and Looker/LookML is a plus
Professional proficiency in English; German is a plus.


What We Offer


Competitive salary
Work in a great team of talented and highly motivated people
Dynamic environment, steep learning curve, and extensive development opportunities
Part of an exciting AI enabled FinTech startup in the German healthcare market
Responsibility, independent work, and high flexibility
Generous budget for learning and development
First-class work equipment and flexible working hours
Deutschlandticket or Swapfiets rental bike
Urban Sports Club membership


We are looking forward to receiving your application and welcoming you to the team.

Title: Research Analyst
HTML:
Research Analyst


Do you want to be part of a bold and ambitious team that helps organizations make better decisions with good data?


About The Role

Data Sciences is looking for a research analyst to assist in the development and execution of market research studies, online experiments and other research projects. This is an entry-level position for someone who has experience in R. The right fit is someone who is a highly motivated self-learner looking to grow and take on a challenging role at a fast-growing company. 


Key Responsibilities

To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform the following satisfactorily:

  • Analyzing and visualizing data to identify meaningful patterns and communicate findings through clear, accurate, and visually polished charts, tables, and other deliverables.
  • Online survey programming and fielding, including survey setup, testing, quotas, vendor coordination, quality checks, and troubleshooting.
  • Establishing research strategies and writing survey questionnaires that align with client objectives and support a clear analytical story, with attention to survey length and respondent experience.
  • Drafting and presenting research reports and memos that synthesize findings into clear, accurate, and actionable insights.
  • Identify opportunities to streamline workflows, strengthen QA, and solve problems through automation, AI tools, and API integrations.
  • Carry out other special projects as required.


Candidate Qualifications

Education/Experience:

  • Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field with experience in quantitative methods.
  • R programming capabilities: 2+ years intermediate experience, with practical experience using the tidyverse (especially dplyr and purrr), writing reusable and modular code, and developing or maintaining R packages.
  • Knowledge of version control systems (e.g. git).
  • Excellent analytical, communication and writing skills.
  • Ability to design clear, compelling, and visually polished data visualizations for technical and non-technical audiences.
  • Ability to work with code and follow coding best practices.
  • Understanding of statistical principles, particularly with respect to survey design and interpretation. 
  • Exceptionally strong organizational skills: detail-oriented, ability to manage several tasks at once and stick to deadlines, good at identifying priorities and managing expectations.
  • Flexibility: you don’t get flustered when new challenges are thrown your way.
  • Ability to work in a fast-paced environment and pivot/react quickly when needed.


Assets:

  • Experience in market and/or public opinion research.
  • Knowledge of other programming languages and tools (e.g., SQL, Python, JavaScript, Google Apps Script), additional analytics related software development experience is a plus.
  • Experience programming, launching, and monitoring surveys programmed in Qualtrics and/or similar survey platforms.
  • Familiarity with AWS-based data workflows, including storing, querying, transforming, and automating data using tools such as S3, Redshift, DynamoDB, and Lambda.
  • Experience safely leveraging LLMs into workflows, especially code assist tools (e.g., Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and willingness to integrate new technologies into your work.
  • Experience with web development tools like React, Next.js, and modern CSS. 
  • Experience related to politics or campaigning.
  • Experience with public speaking and presenting research to diverse audiences.
  • Bilingual in English and French.
  • We work globally! Knowledge of other languages is appreciated.


What We Offer

  • 100% employer-paid competitive health package, including Medical & Dental benefits.
  • Perkopolis: a free program with discounts and savings on entertainment, hotels, travel, shopping and health and wellness products. 
  • Hybrid work, both remote and in the office.
  • Our Head Office in Montreal always has a supply of snacks and coffee.
  • Many internal traditions to create an inclusive and fun environment. 
  • A MacBook computer on your first day. 
  • 3 weeks of vacation.
  • 5 personal days to use at your discretion.
  • A fast-paced work environment where every day is different.
  • The opportunity for professional development through funded courses, conferences and certifications.
  • A startup office space and the opportunity to join a growing, multidisciplinary team.


Knowledge of English is required for this position, as the incumbent will be required to communicate daily, both orally and in writing, with a predominantly English-speaking clientele outside of Quebec.



Analyste de recherche


Voulez-vous faire partie d'une équipe audacieuse et ambitieuse qui aide les organisations à prendre de meilleures décisions grâce à de bonnes données ?


Description du poste

Data Sciences recherche une ou un analyste de recherche pour participer au développement et à l’exécution d’études de marché, d’expériences en ligne et d’autres projets de recherche. Il s’agit d’un poste junior pour une personne qui a de l’expérience à programmer avec R. La personne idéale est autodidacte et très motivée. Elle cherche à se développer et à assumer un rôle stimulant au sein d’une entreprise en plein essor.


﻿Responsabilités

Pour accomplir ce travail avec succès, une personne doit être en mesure d'accomplir les tâches suivantes de manière satisfaisante :


  • Analyser et visualiser les données afin d’en dégager des tendances significatives et de communiquer les résultats au moyen de graphiques, de tableaux et d’autres livrables clairs, exacts et soignés sur le plan visuel;
  • Programmation et déploiement de sondages en ligne, incluant la configuration, les tests, les quotas, la coordination avec les fournisseurs, les contrôles qualité et le dépannage;
  • Établir des stratégies de recherche et rédiger des questionnaires de sondage qui s’harmonisent avec les objectifs des clients et soutiennent une histoire analytique claire, en portant attention à la longueur du sondage et à l’expérience du répondant;
  • Rédiger et présenter des rapports de recherche et des mémos qui synthétisent les résultats en analyses claires, exactes et exploitables;
  • Repérer des occasions de rationaliser les processus, de renforcer le contrôle qualité et de résoudre des problèmes au moyen de l’automatisation, des outils d’IA et des intégrations d’API;
  • Réaliser d'autres projets spéciaux en fonction des besoins.


Exigences

Formation et expérience:

  • Baccalauréat dans un domaine pertinent avec expérience en méthodes quantitatives;
  • Capacités de programmation R : 2 ans ou plus d’expérience de niveau intermédiaire, avec une expérience pratique du tidyverse (en particulier dplyr et purrr), de l’écriture de code réutilisable et modulaire, ainsi que du développement ou de la maintenance de packages R;
  • Connaissance des systèmes de contrôle de version (par exemple, git);
  • Excellentes capacités d'analyse, de communication et de rédaction;
  • Capacité à concevoir des visualisations de données claires, convaincantes et soignées sur le plan visuel, destinées à des publics techniques comme non techniques;
  • Capacité à travailler avec du code et à suivre les meilleures pratiques de codage;
  • Compréhension des principes statistiques, en particulier en ce qui concerne la conception et l'interprétation des enquêtes;
  • Sens de l’organisation exceptionnel — souci du détail, capacité à mener plusieurs tâches de front et à respecter les échéances, aptitude à cerner les priorités et à gérer les attentes;
  • Capacité d’adaptation : vous ne vous laissez pas déconcerter par les nouveaux défis qui se présentent à vous;
  • Capacité à travailler dans un environnement au rythme rapide et à pivoter ou réagir rapidement au besoin.


Atouts: 

  • Expérience en matière d'études de marché et/ou d'opinion publique;
  • Connaissance d’autres langages et outils de programmation (par exemple SQL, Python, JavaScript, Google Apps Script); toute expérience supplémentaire en développement logiciel lié à l’analytique est un atout;
  • Expérience de programmation, de lancement et de suivi de sondages programmés dans Qualtrics ou des plateformes de sondage similaires;
  • Familiarité avec les flux de données basés sur AWS, notamment le stockage, l’interrogation, la transformation et l’automatisation de données à l’aide d’outils comme S3, Redshift, DynamoDB et Lambda;
  • Expérience d’intégration sécuritaire de grands modèles de langage (LLM) dans des flux de travail, en particulier les outils d’assistance au code (par exemple Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), et volonté d’intégrer de nouvelles technologies à votre travail;
  • Expérience avec des outils de développement web tels que React, Next.js et CSS moderne;
  • Expérience dans le domaine de la politique ou des campagnes électorales;
  • Expérience de la prise de parole en public et de la présentation d'études à des publics divers;
  • Bilingue (anglais et français); 
  • Nous travaillons à l'échelle mondiale ! La connaissance d'autres langues est appréciée.


Ce que nous offrons

  • Un régime d'assurance médicale et dentaire compétitif, payé à 100 % par l'employeur;
  • Perkopolis: Un accès gratuit à une foule de rabais et d'économies sur les divertissements, les voyages, le magasinage et les produits de bien-être;
  • Mode de travail hybride offrant la flexibilité du télétravail et de la collaboration au bureau;
  • Des collations, du café et bien plus dans notre siège social de Montréal;
  • Une tonne d'activités et de traditions pour créer un environnement inclusif et divertissant;
  • Un ordinateur MacBook fourni dès ton arrivée;
  • 3 semaines de vacances par année;
  • 5 jours personnels à utiliser à ta discrétion;
  • Un environnement de travail dynamique où aucune journée ne se ressemble;
  • Des opportunités de croissance grâce au financement de cours, de conférences et de certifications;
  • Un bureau moderne avec une ambiance ‘’startup’’;
  • La chance de rejoindre une équipe multidisciplinaire en pleine croissance.


La connaissance de la langue anglaise est exigée pour ce poste, car les fonctions de la personne titulaire nécessiteront des communications orales et écrites quotidiennes avec une clientèle majoritairement anglophone située à l'extérieur du Québec.

Title: Senior Analyst, Labour Insights & Planning
Description:
As a global leader in micromobility, Lime is on a mission to build a future where transportation is shared, affordable and carbon-free. A Time Magazine 100 Most Influential Company, Lime has powered more than one billion rides in close to 30 countries across five continents, spurring a new generation of clean alternatives to car ownership. Learn more at li.me.

Lime is hiring a Senior Analyst to join our Global Operations team! In this role, you’ll be at the forefront of improving labor insights, reporting and financial planning across our operations. You’ll develop key metrics and tools to enhance real-time visibility of performance and drive targeted analysis that informs operational decisions and strategic opportunities. You’ll collaborate closely with Finance, Strategy & Planning, and Local Operations teams to help shape the future of Lime’s labour as we continue to scale.

The ideal candidate is highly analytical, has a strong understanding of financial principles, and is passionate about using data to solve complex problems. If you have 3+ years of experience in business analytics, strong data analysis & visualization skills, and a drive to influence outcomes through insight, we’d love to hear from you.

This is a remote position in the United Kingdom, Germany or France, with a preference for candidates to reside in London to maintain effective collaboration across teams.

This position requires occasional travel to support business operations, which is considered an essential function of the role. Lime will provide reasonable accommodations as needed.

What You’ll Do


Improve real-time visibility of labour efficiency and spend: Develop metrics and tooling to improve visibility of warehouse and street operations labour efficiency and performance
Build models and dashboards: Use data analysis and visualization tools (SQL, tableau, spreadsheet) to build models for labour forecasting and dashboards for local operations teams - ensuring robust logic, consistent and clearly-defined assumptions, intuitive user-interface
Monitor trends in performance: Monitor labour efficiency and vehicle availability vs plan, identifying root causes for any variance or new trends
Collaborate with partner teams to improve efficiency: Provide insight and targeted analysis to troubleshoot issues or highlight opportunities. Partner with Operations leadership and local teams to land recommendations and drive action through to resolution
Contribute to financial planning process: Collaborate with Finance, S&P, and local operations to support the labor aspect of Lime’s financial planning process
Drive continuous improvement in data analytics capabilities across global operations: Contribute to a culture of data-driven decision making and analytical excellence through peer collaboration and support


About You


Strong proficiency in data analytics, including advanced SQL, spreadsheet, and data visualization (e.g. Tableau)
Bachelor’s degree in Business, Mathematics, Finance, Engineering, or a related field, or equivalent industry experience
3+ years of experience in business analytics, where prior experience in financial planning, operations, tracking spend, or equivalent is highly preferred
Strong understanding of financial principles and how labor impacts operations as well as P&L
Curious mindset to spot trends in data, deep-dive root causes, and generate proposals for both tactical and strategic solutions
Clear verbal and written communicator, strong collaboration and influencing skills


Preferred Experience


Previous experience in a fast-paced scaling-up environment
Experience working closely with Finance or S&P stakeholders


If you want to make an impact, Lime is the place for you. Not sure if you meet all the qualifications? If this role excites you we encourage you to apply. Explore all opportunities on our career page.

Lime is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We believe different perspectives help us grow and achieve more. That’s why we’re dedicated to building and developing a team that reflects a wider range of backgrounds, abilities, identities, and experiences. If you require a reasonable accommodation during the application or hiring process, please email recruiting-operations@li.me for assistance.

Title: Research Analyst
Description:
Job Title

Research Analyst

Job Description Summary

The Research Analyst is responsible for research, data & analytics and reporting activities across the German market. This role is designed to support the organic growth of the German businesses by thought leadership and insight outputs to help generate leads and opportunities in the business.

The role will directly report to and work closely with the Research Team Lead, Germany to ensure consistency of client experience, processes and operational excellence across the EMEA region. The role will also cooperate with colleagues from local business services departments, as well as other central functions, mainly Marketing and Data & Analytics teams.

Job Description

Key Responsibilities:


Ensure real estate data collection and update of existing databases (office, industrial, retail, residential, hotel, investment etc.) to deliver regular market and economic updates, with specific focus areas determined by team structure, location, and other relevant factors.
Cooperate with other business services teams to gather, analyse, and utilize local and regional market insights, including competitive, client, and industry data, to produce market thought leadership.
Support business development activities, including presenting market updates to clients.
Undertake independent market analyses for ad-hoc reports for clients as defined by the business.
Support marketing and communication activities in line with the company content strategy defined by the Head of Business Development Services, Germany.
Contribute to C&W brand visibility through active participation in public events and social media.
Cooperate with C&W research teams in other countries and the wider EMEA team.


Knowledge & Experience


Strong analytical skills with expertise in data collection, statistical analysis, and market research
Ability to interpret data, identify trends, and assess market dynamics with accuracy
Excellent communication skills for translating complex data into clear reports, presentations, and visualizations
Creative problem-solving and effective collaboration in dynamic, deadline-driven environments
Proficiency in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, OneDrive, SharePoint); familiarity with Power BI, Power Query, and GIS tools (ArcGIS, QGIS)
Experience in professional services or real estate sector, aligned with role seniority
Degree in Economics, Geography, Real Estate, or related field preferred


What We Offer

Early assumption of responsibility, flat hierarchies and an international and modern working environment, attractive and performance-related remuneration, flexible working hours with the possibility of hybrid work scheme, 30 vacation days plus special leave days, further training and development opportunities, company pension scheme.

Please note, we do not consider applications per E-mail.

We foster a culture of inclusion that embraces the unique strengths, perspectives, and experiences of all our employees. We firmly believe that our diversity enhances our team's capabilities, leading to improved decision-making, innovation, and business outcomes.

Title: Data Analyst (Marketing Analytics, Bangkok Based, Relocation provided)
Description:
About Agoda

At Agoda, we bridge the world through travel. Our story began in 2005, when two lifelong friends and entrepreneurs, driven by their passion for travel, launched Agoda to make it easier for everyone to explore the world.

Today, we are part of Booking Holdings [NASDAQ: BKNG], with a diverse team of over 7,000 people from 90 countries, working together in offices around the globe. Every day, we connect people to destinations and experiences, with our great deals across our millions of hotels and holiday properties, flights, and experiences worldwide.

No two days are the same at Agoda. Data and technology are at the heart of our culture, fueling our curiosity and innovation. If you’re ready to begin your best journey and help build travel for the world, join us.

This role is based in Bangkok, Thailand, and is open to candidates worldwide. We offer a generous relocation package to ensure a smooth transition to living and working in Bangkok.

Get to Know our Team:

The Performance Marketing Team of Agoda is a world leader in online marketing. This department is highly data-driven and focused on developing at-scale marketing programs that improve the lifetime value of Agoda customers through measurable marketing programs and channels. The team is a blend of the best analysts, marketing strategists, and data scientists in the world. The marketing leadership at Agoda have deep experience in data science, product, strategy, and other marketing fields and have built an organization that thrives on data, creative ideas, and technology. The Performance Marketing Team also fosters a great learning environment. You will be able to learn and grow by working closely with experts from a variety of backgrounds from all over the world.

In this Role, you’ll get to:


Search: Experiment with text ads, bidding, and campaign structures on Google, Bing, Baidu, Naver, and other search engines. Adapt to new product features and roll out changes from successful tests.
Display: Test, analyze, and optimize campaigns on Facebook, Twitter, Instagram, and others.
Modeling: Analyze the vast amounts of data generated by experiments, develop models we can use for optimization, and build dashboards for account managers.


What you’ll Need to Succeed:


2–3 years of experience
Bachelor’s Degree or higher from top university in a quantitative subject (computer science, mathematics, economics, engineering, statistics or science)
Strong English communication
Solid business acumen
Strong statistical and analytical skills
Proficient in Excel and hands-on with data tools (SAS, R, SPSS, Python, VBA, SQL, Tableau)
Experience designing and analyzing A/B tests
Works independently on daily analytics tasks and provides business recommendations


It’s Great if you Have:


Experience in digital marketing
Academic research experience

Title: Senior Data Analyst (m/f/x)
Description:
Atolls is the world’s largest community-driven shopping platform, active in 20+ markets.

We help millions make smarter spending decisions across discovery, evaluation, and (re-)purchase by connecting people with the right brands and retailers.

Our 1,000+ team across 10 countries builds products used every day at global scale, where you’ll have real ownership and see your impact. Want to shape the destinations people rely on to shop with confidence? Keep reading.




About This Role:

We are looking for a Senior Data Analyst (Community & Product Forensics) to join our Community Data Team team in Berlin and surrounding area. At Atolls, your role will be instrumental in helping us lead our community intelligence and data integrity efforts. You will be our "Data Detective," responsible for performing deep-dive analytics on high-volume event data, ensuring its accuracy, and uncovering insights to drive community growth. As a key part of our team, you'll be directly involved in building and enhancing the digital destinations our users rely on throughout their shopping journeys.

Your contributions will help shape how millions of consumers interact with our platforms, guiding them to make smart, fair, and rewarding choices.




Our Benefits:

At Atolls, we believe in nurturing both your professional and personal growth. Here’s what you can expect:

A culture that values personal and professional development, with internal mobility opportunities.
A supportive and open-minded team that embraces diverse perspectives and innovative ideas.
32 days of paid vacation plus your birthday off, giving you the time you need to recharge.
A flexible hybrid working scheme to balance work and life.
Access to a learning budget and internal training to help you grow in your role.
Mental health coaching to support your well-being.
Regular global and local get-togethers to celebrate successes and build connections.
The possibility of taking a sabbatical after three years with the company.
A cloud-based company setup, providing flexibility and collaboration opportunities no matter where you are.
These are global benefits that apply to all employees, with additional local perks based on your location.




Responsibilities:

In this role, you will:

Technical Investigation & Forensics: Identify and resolve data discrepancies. If a metric looks "off," you'll use your knowledge of web/app technologies (inspecting network requests, tracking pixels, or API responses) to find the root cause.
Deep-Dive Community Analytics: Analyze complex member behaviors—retention cohorts, engagement velocity, and viral loops—to drive community growth.
Data Science & Modeling: Move beyond descriptive stats by building and deploying models (e.g., Churn Prediction, Sentiment Analysis, or Member Segmentation) using Python.
High-Performance SQL: Master our ClickHouse environment, writing optimized queries that leverage columnar storage to process billions of rows of interaction data.
Data Hygiene: Audit "dirty" data and build robust logic to handle bot activity, duplicate identities, and tracking gaps.




Your Profile:

We're looking for someone with experience in writing complex SQL, investigating web/app event data, and building predictive models in Python, who is dedicated to creating exceptional user experiences and driving innovation.

Expert SQL: Advanced mastery of SQL (CTEs, Window Functions, Optimization) specifically for columnar databases (ClickHouse experience is a massive plus).
Web/App Technical Literacy: Basic understanding of how web/app tracking works. You should be comfortable using tools to inspect network payloads and debug event triggers.
Analytical Intuition: A "sixth sense" for spotting data anomalies and the tenacity to investigate them until they are solved.




Nice-to-Haves:

Workflow Automation: Basic knowledge of Airflow for scheduling analytical scripts.
Data Science Toolkit: Proficiency in Python (Pandas, Scikit-learn) for statistical modeling and complex data manipulation.
Community Domain Knowledge: Experience analyzing developer communities, forums, or social platforms.




Our hiring process:

TA Call: Meet one of our Talent Experts and get to know Atolls better.
Technical Round: Focus on the technical aspects of the role (Through a Live Case), and meet your potential manager.
Final Round: Meet other Atollians 🏝️ It varies from 1 to 3 interviews
Some processes might slightly change according to needs




Ready to apply?

Be part of a destination where your work helps millions of people make better decisions every day. One focused application is all we need. If you truly fit more than one role, you’re welcome to apply to up to three. This helps us match you with the right opportunity faster.




We review every application with equal care and will reach out if your profile aligns. Apply now with your CV in English.

Portfolios, writing samples, or certifications may be requested based on the role.




At Atolls, we want to ensure that all employees can thrive in an inclusive environment. Our employment opportunities are open to every gender, race, religion, age, sexual orientation, ability, place of origin, or socioeconomic status. We remain committed to a culture of diversity, equity and belonging, where all employees are welcomed, respected, connected, and engaged.

HTML:
Atolls is the world’s largest community-driven shopping platform, active in 20+ markets.

We help millions make smarter spending decisions across discovery, evaluation, and (re-)purchase by connecting people with the right brands and retailers.

Our 1,000+ team across 10 countries builds products used every day at global scale, where you’ll have real ownership and see your impact. Want to shape the destinations people rely on to shop with confidence? Keep reading.


About This Role:

We are looking for a Senior Data Analyst (Community & Product Forensics) to join our Community Data Team team in Berlin and surrounding area. At Atolls, your role will be instrumental in helping us lead our community intelligence and data integrity efforts. You will be our "Data Detective," responsible for performing deep-dive analytics on high-volume event data, ensuring its accuracy, and uncovering insights to drive community growth. As a key part of our team, you'll be directly involved in building and enhancing the digital destinations our users rely on throughout their shopping journeys.

Your contributions will help shape how millions of consumers interact with our platforms, guiding them to make smart, fair, and rewarding choices.


Our Benefits:

At Atolls, we believe in nurturing both your professional and personal growth. Here’s what you can expect:

  • A culture that values personal and professional development, with internal mobility opportunities.
  • A supportive and open-minded team that embraces diverse perspectives and innovative ideas.
  • 32 days of paid vacation plus your birthday off, giving you the time you need to recharge.
  • A flexible hybrid working scheme to balance work and life.
  • Access to a learning budget and internal training to help you grow in your role.
  • Mental health coaching to support your well-being.
  • Regular global and local get-togethers to celebrate successes and build connections.
  • The possibility of taking a sabbatical after three years with the company.
  • A cloud-based company setup, providing flexibility and collaboration opportunities no matter where you are.
  • These are global benefits that apply to all employees, with additional local perks based on your location.


Responsibilities:

In this role, you will:

  • Technical Investigation & Forensics: Identify and resolve data discrepancies. If a metric looks "off," you'll use your knowledge of web/app technologies (inspecting network requests, tracking pixels, or API responses) to find the root cause.
  • Deep-Dive Community Analytics: Analyze complex member behaviors—retention cohorts, engagement velocity, and viral loops—to drive community growth.
  • Data Science & Modeling: Move beyond descriptive stats by building and deploying models (e.g., Churn Prediction, Sentiment Analysis, or Member Segmentation) using Python.
  • High-Performance SQL: Master our ClickHouse environment, writing optimized queries that leverage columnar storage to process billions of rows of interaction data.
  • Data Hygiene: Audit "dirty" data and build robust logic to handle bot activity, duplicate identities, and tracking gaps.


Your Profile:

We're looking for someone with experience in writing complex SQL, investigating web/app event data, and building predictive models in Python, who is dedicated to creating exceptional user experiences and driving innovation.

  • Expert SQL: Advanced mastery of SQL (CTEs, Window Functions, Optimization) specifically for columnar databases (ClickHouse experience is a massive plus).
  • Web/App Technical Literacy: Basic understanding of how web/app tracking works. You should be comfortable using tools to inspect network payloads and debug event triggers.
  • Analytical Intuition: A "sixth sense" for spotting data anomalies and the tenacity to investigate them until they are solved.


Nice-to-Haves:

  • Workflow Automation: Basic knowledge of Airflow for scheduling analytical scripts.
  • Data Science Toolkit: Proficiency in Python (Pandas, Scikit-learn) for statistical modeling and complex data manipulation.
  • Community Domain Knowledge: Experience analyzing developer communities, forums, or social platforms.


Our hiring process:

  • TA Call: Meet one of our Talent Experts and get to know Atolls better.
  • Technical Round: Focus on the technical aspects of the role (Through a Live Case), and meet your potential manager.
  • Final Round: Meet other Atollians 🏝️ It varies from 1 to 3 interviews
  • Some processes might slightly change according to needs


Ready to apply?

Be part of a destination where your work helps millions of people make better decisions every day. One focused application is all we need. If you truly fit more than one role, you’re welcome to apply to up to three. This helps us match you with the right opportunity faster.


We review every application with equal care and will reach out if your profile aligns. Apply now with your CV in English.

  • Portfolios, writing samples, or certifications may be requested based on the role.


At Atolls, we want to ensure that all employees can thrive in an inclusive environment. Our employment opportunities are open to every gender, race, religion, age, sexual orientation, ability, place of origin, or socioeconomic status. We remain committed to a culture of diversity, equity and belonging, where all employees are welcomed, respected, connected, and engaged.

Title: Data Analyst Global Finance Technology & Data
Description:
Als Data Analyst (w/m/d) in unserem Team für Global Finance Technology & Data gestaltest du die Datengrundlage, die unser modernes Finanzreporting, unsere Planung und Analysen vorantreibt. An der Schnittstelle von Finanzen, Daten und Technologie verantwortest du zentrale Elemente unserer Finanzdaten- und Reporting-Landschaft. Du konzipierst, implementierst und betreust skalierbare Datenmodelle und Reporting-Lösungen auf Kernplattformen wie BigQuery, dbt, Looker und SAP Datasphere. Deine Arbeit ist ein entscheidender Beitrag zu unserer strategischen Transformation: Du steigerst die Automatisierung, stärkst die Datenqualität und formst eine zukunftsfähige Finanzarchitektur für unser globales Unternehmen.




Das sind deine Aufgaben

Konzeption & Entwicklung: Du konzipierst, entwickelst und pflegst Finanzdatenmodelle, Reporting-Strukturen und analytische Layer für GuV, Cashflow, Bilanz und verwandte Finanzanwendungen.
Datenpipelines: Du baust Datenpipelines und Transformationen über BigQuery, dbt, Looker, Datasphere, SAC und angebundene Finanzsysteme hinweg auf, optimierst und betreust sie.
Datenintegrität: Du pflegst und entwickelst Finanzhierarchien, Reporting-Logiken und Stammdatenstrukturen weiter, um ein konsistentes und verlässliches Reporting zu gewährleisten.
Automatisierung: Du treibst die Automatisierung von Datenerfassung, Abgleich und Reporting im Finanzbereich voran, um manuelle Prozesse zu reduzieren und die Effizienz zu steigern.
Strategische Projekte: Du unterstützt die Implementierung strategischer Finanztechnologie-Initiativen, wie die Einführung von SAP Datasphere, die COPA-Optimierung und die Modernisierung unserer Reporting-Architektur.
Governance: Du gewährleistest eine hohe Datenqualität sowie effektive Governance- und Berechtigungskonzepte über alle Systeme und Reporting-Umgebungen hinweg.
Schnittstellenmanagement: In enger Zusammenarbeit mit Stakeholdern aus Finanzen, Daten-Teams und Technologie übersetzt du Geschäftsanforderungen in skalierbare technische Lösungen.
Standardisierung: Du wirkst an der Standardisierung und Harmonisierung von Finanzdaten mit und unterstützt die Transformation weg von veralteten BW-Strukturen.




Das bringst du mit

Technische Expertise: Du besitzt starke SQL-Kenntnisse und fundierte Erfahrung in Datenmodellierung, Data Warehousing und Analytics Engineering. Erfahrung mit Cloud-Plattformen und Tools wie BigQuery, dbt, Looker oder SAP Datasphere ist von großem Vorteil.
Finanz-Know-how: Du hast ein fundiertes Verständnis von Finanzreporting-Strukturen wie GuV, Cashflow, Bilanz sowie von Forecast- und Budgetprozessen.
Datenarchitektur: Du bringst nachgewiesene Erfahrung im Design und in der Betreuung von Datenpipelines, semantischen Modellen und Reporting-Lösungen mit.
Analytische Fähigkeiten: Dich zeichnen exzellente analytische und problemlösende Fähigkeiten aus, gepaart mit einem hohen Qualitätsanspruch und Liebe zum Detail.
Schnittstellenkompetenz: Du bist in der Lage, komplexe Anforderungen aus dem Finanzbereich souverän in robuste technische und datengestützte Lösungen zu übersetzen.
Teamfähigkeit: Du arbeitest effektiv in funktionsübergreifenden Teams und kommunizierst sicher mit Stakeholdern aus Business und Technologie.
Mindset: Eine ausgeprägte Bereitschaft zur kontinuierlichen Weiterbildung und ein starkes Interesse an moderner Finanztechnologie, Automatisierung und Data Governance runden dein Profil ab.
Ausbildung: Ein Bachelor-Abschluss in Finanzen, Rechnungswesen, Informatik oder einem verwandten Bereich ist erforderlich; ein Master-Abschluss wird bevorzugt.
Sprachen: Deine Englischkenntnisse sind verhandlungssicher. Gute Deutschkenntnisse sind wünschenswert.
Zusätzliche Pluspunkte: Kenntnisse in Python sowie Erfahrung mit SAP-Finanzumgebungen und deren Stammdatenstrukturen.

Title: Analytics Engineer (Power BI Specialist)
Description:
Company overview:

Blue Orange Digital is a boutique data & AI consultancy that delivers enterprise-grade results. We design and build modern data platforms, analytics, and ML/AI Agent solutions for mid‑market and enterprise clients across Private Equity, Financial Services, Healthcare, and Retail.

Our teams work with technologies like Databricks, Snowflake, dbt, and the broader Microsoft ecosystem to turn messy, real-world data into trustworthy, actionable insight.

We’re a builder‑led, client‑first culture that prizes ownership, clear communication, and shipping high‑impact work.

Note: Please submit your resume in English, as all application materials must be in English for review and consideration.

Position Overview

Blue Orange Digital is seeking a Senior Analytics Engineer and Power BI Specialist to own the analytics and visualization layer of a 26‑week enterprise data warehouse implementation in the global commodities trading and supply chain industry. You will join full‑time from Week 6 through Week 22, designing and building Fabric Semantic Models and production Power BI reports that translate Gold‑layer data products into operational dashboards for trading, risk, and finance stakeholders — including Trader P&L, Global Position, Risk Metrics (VaR), and Financial Consolidation.

This role bridges the engineering team and business users, requiring deep Power BI expertise and the communication skills to work directly with trading and finance professionals.

Responsibilities


Design and build Microsoft Fabric Semantic Models (Power BI datasets) on top of Gold‑layer Lakehouse and Warehouse data products
Define DAX measures, hierarchies, relationships, and semantic model structures
Develop production Power BI dashboards for Trader P&L, Global Position, VaR, and Financial Consolidation
Work directly with trading, risk, and finance stakeholders to translate reporting requirements into semantic model design
Optimize DirectQuery and Import mode performance for complex financial and trading datasets
Define naming conventions, metric hierarchies, and semantic governance standards for the Fabric workspace
Collaborate with Data Engineers to ensure Gold‑layer outputs are analytics‑ready
Support self‑service analytics enablement for client analysts
Contribute to UAT, report validation, and business sign‑off on accuracy and performance


Requirements


7+ years in analytics engineering, BI development, or data analytics with production‑grade Power BI delivery
Deep hands‑on expertise with Power BI (semantic models, DAX, report design, workspace management, RLS)
Experience with Microsoft Fabric Power BI integration or SSAS/AAS as a semantic layer
Strong SQL for analytical queries and metric logic
Proven ability to engage finance, trading, or risk stakeholders and translate requirements into technical specifications
Bachelor’s degree in a quantitative, technical, or analytical field (or equivalent experience)


Preferred Qualifications


Exposure to commodity trading, financial risk, or P&L reporting environments
Microsoft Power BI certification (PL‑300 or PL‑400)
Hands‑on experience with DAX optimization and performance tuning for large tabular models
Familiarity with Fabric Lakehouse/Warehouse as semantic model sources (DP‑600 or DP‑700 exposure a plus)


Salary: $ 5,500 - 7,200 [ USD ] monthly

Background checks may be required for certain positions/projects.

Blue Orange Digital is an equal-opportunity employer.

Title: Big Data Analyst
Description:
YOUR FUTURE, ON DEMAND




The ideal blend of stability and flexibility. A genuinely human employer that cares for people and the planet. True autonomy to shape what comes next, for us and you. This is the perfect platform to take your career where you want.




Back in 2005, we pioneered Europe’s first TV streaming service. Today, we’re the world’s first certified climate neutral TV streaming provider. Several million users across three countries. Over 230 colleagues of 47 nationalities. We’re the grown-up start-up, helping to shape a better future since day one. That hasn’t changed, even if the world has. We’re friendly, international, inclusive – and our people say we’re reliable and transparent, too. It’s why they trust us and believe in what we do.




THE ROLE




We are looking for a Data Analyst to join Zattoo's BI Team following an internal transition. This is not a typical dashboard-producer role. We want someone who combines deep technical craft — writing production-grade SQL, building DBT models, orchestrating pipelines — with the commercial instinct to turn raw data into decisions. We are also actively exploring how AI and LLM tooling can augment the way we work, and we want someone genuinely curious about that space.




This role sits at the intersection of Data Engineering and Business Analysis. You will own analytical products end-to-end: from schema design and DBT model authorship, through to stakeholder presentations and strategic recommendations - without needing to hand off at either end.




THE TEAM




You'll join a compact BI team of four analysts, reporting to the Head of Business Intelligence. We operate across Zattoo's DE, CH, and AT markets, supporting product, commercial, and executive stakeholders. We process 6 TB of event data daily and are mid-way through a migration from a legacy Vertica/Jenkins stack to a modern GCP architecture (BigQuery, Airflow, dbt). There is real scope to shape how we do things - this is not a maintenance role.


WHAT YOU’LL DO



Own analytical domains end-to-end: Design and maintain DBT models, write production SQL in BigQuery, and deliver finished insight - not raw data - to stakeholders.


Proactively generate business insight: Go beyond answering questions. Identify trends, anomalies, and opportunities autonomously and bring them to the relevant team before they ask.


Build and maintain Tableau dashboards: Design decision-oriented dashboards tuned for product, commercial, and executive audiences - including external-facing B2B analytics.


Collaborate on the data stack: Work closely with Data Engineers on Airflow pipelines, schema evolution, data quality and governance. You should be comfortable reading and contributing to pipeline code.


Partner with stakeholders: Translate ambiguous business questions into analytical frameworks. Run working sessions, present findings, and advocate for data-informed decisions at all levels.


Support A/B testing and experimentation: Design test setups, validate statistical significance, and communicate results as clear product recommendations.


Champion data quality: Own data dictionaries, documentation, and DBT tests for your domains. Raise quality issues proactively.



WHAT YOU’LL BRING



4+ years of experience as a Data or Analytics Engineer in a digital or tech product company


Expert-level SQL - you write complex, optimised queries without looking things up


Hands-on experience with BigQuery as a primary data warehouse


Practical experience with DBT - writing models, tests, documentation, and understanding lineage


Experience building and scheduling data pipelines with Apache Airflow


Strong Tableau skills - you build dashboards that drive decisions, not just display numbers


Strong communication: you can present analytical findings to a CFO and pair-program with an engineer on the same day


Familiarity with Mixpanel, mParticle, or similar product analytics tools


Comfort working in ambiguity - you can scope and prioritise your own analytical work



Nice to Have



Experience with ClickHouse


Python for data manipulation or automation (pandas, scripts, notebooks)


Background in OTT, media, or subscription businesses


Experience with A/B testing platforms (Optimizely, etc.)



AI & Emerging Tools — Openness to Learn



Hands-on exposure to LLM APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, etc.) — e.g. building classification, tagging, or summarisation pipelines on top of text data


Familiarity with agentic frameworks or workflow automation tools (LangChain, n8n, Airflow with LLM steps, or similar)


Regular use of AI-assisted development tools such as Cursor or GitHub Copilot in day-to-day coding


Curiosity about applying AI to BI workflows: auto-commentary on KPI movements, semantic search over data catalogs, or similar





WHY JOIN US?




At Zattoo, you’re always on firm ground with real freedom to explore. You’ll have the support and opportunity you need to go far, while you’re helping us create a sustainable and entertainment-filled future for all.


WHAT WE OFFER YOU



Reward: strong market competitive salary


Recharge: 30 days vacation plus public holidays


Regulate: trust based flexible working hours and the option for remote work worldwide for up to 8 weeks per year



Plus:



We’re sharing: Free access to the best streaming experience on the market


We're developing: Your own development budget, German classes and Zattoo all-company hack days


**We’re inclusive: ‘**Zattooies’ are a fun, engaged, and diverse team made up of 47 nationalities


We’re healthy: Health checks, mental wellbeing support, team sports and more!


We’re responsible: We are carbon neutral, an active employee Green team, discounted BVG tickets and free bike rentals


We’re sociable: A calendar of events with fireside chats, lunches, in-house bar, BBQ’s, monthly socials, summer party and an annual off-site event





Zattoo enables our users to view indiscriminately and we reflect that in our team too. Regardless of race, gender, sexual orientation, religion, national origin, age, disability or any other characteristic protected by law we encourage everyone to apply and will give everyone equal consideration. We are an equal opportunities employer — join us and help build a diverse team dedicated to revolutionising the way you watch TV.




We are committed to ensuring fair, open, and equitable hiring practices and are happy to answer any questions you may have about us or the role throughout the whole process. In line with the EU Pay Transparency Directive, we share the applicable compensation range with you before your first interview.

Title: Working Student Operations & Analytics (x/f/m)
Description:
Gemeinsam gestalten wir die Gesundheitsversorgung der Zukunft

Doctolib sucht Dich ab sofort als Werkstudent:in im Bereich Operations & Analytics für unseren Standort in Berlin.  

Das LOE-Team steht für operative Exzellenz quer durch unsere kundenseitigen Bereiche – von Deployment über Customer Onboarding und Customer Success bis hin zu Support Excellence. Wir stellen sicher, dass alle Teams mit sauberen Daten, verlässlichen KPI-Reportings und reibungslosen CRM-Prozessen arbeiten können. So tragen wir dazu bei, dass Tausende Gesundheitseinrichtungen in Deutschland täglich die bestmögliche Unterstützung von Doctolib erhalten – und leisten damit unseren direkten Beitrag zu unserer Mission: den Zugang zur Gesundheitsversorgung für alle zu verbessern.

Deine Aufgaben

Als Werkstudent:in unterstützt Du uns in den folgenden Aufgabenbereichen:

Wöchentliche Datenexporte aus Salesforce, Metabase & Google Sheets
Manuelle KPI-Updates in Qobra (unserem Tool zur Bonusberechnung)
Pflege von Abwesenheits- und Headcount-Tracking-Tabellen
Aktualisierung von KPI-Präsentationen für die Teams Customer Onboarding & Deployment
Erstellung wiederkehrender Salesforce-Cases (KYC, Preiserhöhung, Churn)
Unterstützung bei der Bonus-Validierung und der Vorbereitung von Performance Reviews
Formatierung & Re-Import von Daten zwischen verschiedenen Systemen (z. B. Typeform → Salesforce, Google Sheets → Salesforce)
Ad-hoc-Unterstützung bei Reporting-Anfragen und Analytics-Tickets
Beantwortung von Datenfragen quer durch alle Teams (KPI-Definitionen, Berechnungen)
Während Deiner Tätigkeit bei uns wirst Du lernen:
relevanten Business-Intelligence- und CRM-Tools (Salesforce, Metabase, Qobra) in einem schnell wachsenden HealthTech-Unternehmen
Ein tiefes Verständnis für datengetriebene Operations-Prozesse entwickeln und verstehen, wie KPI-Systeme in einer internationalen Organisation funktionieren
Cross-funktionale Zusammenarbeit in einem dynamischen Umfeld erleben und ein breites Netzwerk innerhalb von Doctolib aufbauen
Dein Profil

Du bist der festen Überzeugung, dass Du genau das mitbringst, was es für die Position braucht? Dann ermutigen wir Dich, Dich zu bewerben. Selbst wenn Du nicht jede Anforderung erfüllst, bist Du vielleicht genau die/der richtige zukünftige Doctoliber:in für diese oder andere Stellen!

Um Teil unseres Teams zu werden, bringst Du Folgendes mit:
Du bist in Vollzeit an einer Hochschule immatrikuliert und studierst im Bereich BWL, Wirtschaftsinformatik, Data Science, Statistik oder einem vergleichbaren Studiengang (ab dem 3. Semester)
Du hast sehr gute Kenntnisse in Google Sheets oder Excel (Formeln, strukturierte Daten) und bist sicher im Umgang mit strukturierten Datensätzen
Du arbeitest strukturiert, präzise und zuverlässig – auch bei wiederkehrenden Aufgaben
Du beherrschst die Datenformatierung, -bereinigung sowie den Import/Export zwischen verschiedenen Systemen sicher
Du kommunizierst klar auf Englisch (working level) und informierst Dein Team proaktiv bei Problemen oder Unklarheiten
Besonders interessant sind für uns außerdem:
Erste Erfahrungen mit Salesforce (Exporte, Case-Erstellung, grundlegende Navigation)
Kenntnisse in Metabase oder einem ähnlichen BI-Tool
Deutschkenntnisse von Vorteil
Was wir Dir bieten
Ein strukturiertes und individuelles Onboarding im Rahmen unserer Doctolib Academy sowie ein breites Angebot interner Trainingsprogramme
Ein von Doctolib gesponsertes Deutschlandticket oder die Übernahme Deines Semestertickets
28 Urlaubstage im Jahr (anteilig auf Deine Arbeitszeiten berechnet)
Ein flexibles Arbeitsmodell, das hybrides und Office-basiertes Arbeiten ermöglicht
Regelmäßige Frühstücksangebote im Büro sowie Bezuschussungen zu Deinem Lebensmitteleinkauf
Ein gefördertes Sportangebot mit unserem Partner Urban Sports Club
Der Interviewprozess
Interview mit einer Deiner Managerin
Case Study mit Deinen Teammitgliedern
Offer
Job Details
Tätigkeit als Werkstudent:in (20 Std./Woche)
Tätigkeitsdauer: 3 Monate
Arbeitsort: Berlin
Gehalt: 15 €/Std.
Startdatum: zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt
Arbeitsmodus: Hybrid (2-3 Tage Office/Woche)



Wir verfolgen die Vision, die Gesundheit aller zu verbessern. Dies spiegelt sich auch im Recruiting wider. Wir evaluieren Bewerbungen ausschließlich auf der Grundlage von Qualifikation und Motivation. Diskriminierung wird bei uns nicht toleriert.

Je diverser unser Team und die Ideen sind, desto mehr wird unser Produkt die Gesundheit unserer Nutzer:innen verbessern können. Deshalb laden wir dich ein, dich bei uns zu bewerben, unabhängig von Geschlecht, Religion, Alter, sexueller Orientierung, ethnischer Herkunft oder Behinderung. 

Um Chancengleichheit zu gewährleisten, kannst Du in deiner Bewerbung auf Angaben wie Fotos, Alter, etc. verzichten. Lass uns bitte wissen, welche Vorkehrungen wir treffen können, um Dich bestmöglich zu unterstützen. Wir freuen uns, mit Dir das Gesundheitswesen zu digitalisieren!

Title: Data Analyst (m/w/d) – Industrial & Automotive Consulting
Description:
Beschreibung

Du willst im Bereich Data Science & Analytics dort arbeiten, wo es wirklich zählt – an komplexen Industrieprojekten mit direktem Einfluss auf Entscheidungen auf Management-Ebene?

Bei DRIVE Consulting arbeitest du an anspruchsvollen Fragestellungen führender Industrieunternehmen. Du unterstützt dabei, datengetriebene Entscheidungen in Transformations- und Beratungsprojekten umzusetzen. Unsere Analysen und Dashboards fließen direkt in das Reporting und die Entscheidungsgrundlagen unserer Kunden ein.

In dieser Rolle arbeitest du hands-on an Analytics-Lösungen und unterstützt bei der Entwicklung von Dashboards, der Analyse komplexer Daten sowie beim Aufbau von Datenstrukturen und -pipelines. Dabei begleitest du Projekte von der Anforderungsanalyse über die Konzeption bis zur Umsetzung und entwickelst deine fachlichen und methodischen Fähigkeiten kontinuierlich weiter.

Deine Aufgaben


Du arbeitest an Analytics-Workstreams in Kundenprojekten und übernimmst Verantwortung für eigene Aufgaben und Teilprojekte
Gemeinsam mit deinem Team entwickelst und betreibst du Datenpipelines zwischen Cloud-Quellen und Analytics- bzw. Dashboard-Tools und stellst deren Qualität, Automatisierung und Performance sicher (insbesondere über SQL)
Du konzipierst und entwickelst Dashboards mit Fokus auf Usability und klare Entscheidungsunterstützung für das Management
Du analysierst komplexe und heterogene Datenstrukturen, bereitest Daten auf und führst verschiedene Datenquellen effizient zusammen
Du führst Ad-hoc-Analysen und explorative Auswertungen durch und bereitest Ergebnisse adressatengerecht auf
Du arbeitest eng im Projektteam und mit relevanten Stakeholdern zusammen und treibst die Umsetzung datengetriebener Lösungen aktiv voran
Du unterstützt bei Architekturfragen, Priorisierungen und der Weiterentwicklung von Analytics-Lösungen
Du bringst eigene Ideen ein und entwickelst dich fachlich und methodisch kontinuierlich weiter


Dein Profil


Du hast erste Berufserfahrung (ca. 1–3 Jahre) im Bereich Data Analytics, Data Science, Data Engineering oder einem vergleichbaren datengetriebenen Umfeld
Du verfügst über gute Kenntnisse in Python und bist in der Lage, komplexe Datenstrukturen eigenständig zu analysieren und aufzubereiten
Du hast erste Erfahrung im Aufbau von Datenpipelines und/oder im Dashboarding gesammelt
Du bringst Erfahrung im Umgang mit BI-Tools (z. B. AWS QuickSight, Grafana, Tableau, etc.) mit
Du arbeitest analytisch und strukturiert und hast Freude daran, komplexe Fragestellungen zu lösen
Du denkst dich schnell in neue Themen ein und entwickelst dich fachlich kontinuierlich weiter
Idealerweise hast du erste Berührungspunkte mit Prozessen in der Automobilindustrie oder im industriellen Umfeld
Du arbeitest gerne im Team, kommunizierst klar und trittst auch im Austausch mit Kunden sicher auf
Du bist bereit, regelmäßig vor Ort bei unseren Kunden zu arbeiten


Unser Angebot an dich

Bei DRIVE Consulting triffst du auf ein junges, wachsendes Team, dessen Herz für technische Beratungsprojekte in der Industrie schlägt – insbesondere im Automotive-Umfeld.

Dich erwartet ein Umfeld mit hohem fachlichen Anspruch, schneller Lernkurve und individueller Weiterentwicklung. Du arbeitest eng mit erfahrenen Kolleg:innen zusammen, übernimmst früh Verantwortung für eigene Themen und entwickelst deine fachlichen und methodischen Fähigkeiten kontinuierlich weiter.

Offenheit, Vertrauen und Teamspirit prägen unsere Zusammenarbeit – in einem motivierten Team mit klarer Umsetzungsorientierung.

Außerdem Erwarten Dich


Individuelles Coaching und persönliches Buddy-Programm für deinen Einstieg und deine kontinuierliche Weiterentwicklung
Dienstwagen oder Jobrad bzw. Jobticket – flexibel nach deinen Bedürfnissen
Täglicher Lunch-Zuschuss (6 €)
EGYM Wellpass für Fitness- und Gesundheitsangebote
Attraktive Mitarbeiterrabatte über Corporate Benefits
Zuschuss zur betrieblichen Altersvorsorge
Obst, Kaffee und Erfrischungsgetränke im Office
Regelmäßige Teamevents zur Stärkung des Teamzusammenhalts
Monatliches Happy Friday Frühstück
100 € Spendenbudget zur freien Verfügung
Sabbatical-Optionen


Über uns

DRIVE Consulting GmbH ist eine Unternehmensberatung mit Hauptsitz in Aachen sowie Standorten in München, Berlin, Peking und Shanghai. Unser Herz schlägt für technische Beratungsprojekte mit Fokus auf die Automobil- und Industriebranche. Zu unseren Kunden zählen sowohl deutsche Premium OEMs als auch junge, aufstrebende Automobilhersteller sowie große und mittelständische Industrieunternehmen.

Unser Name steht für unseren klaren Fokus auf die Mobilitätsbranche und repräsentiert gleichzeitig unseren Spirit – denn wir sind ein Team, in dem jede*r Einzelne DRIVE hat.

Wir stehen unseren Kunden als Wegbereiter ins Unbekannte und als Katalysator für Innovationen zur Seite. Denn wir sind überzeugt, dass man nur mit dem Mut, neue Wege einzuschlagen, einzigartige Erfolgsgeschichten schreiben kann.

Wir schlagen Brücken zwischen kleinen Ideenschmieden und großen Konzernen sowie zwischen Management und Technologieexpert*innen.

Durch den engen und partnerschaftlichen Austausch mit unseren Kunden entwickeln wir maßgeschneiderte und individuelle Lösungen. Ein entscheidender Erfolgsfaktor ist dabei unser einzigartiges Partnernetzwerk aus Industrie und Forschung. Dank unserer Nähe zu namhaften Universitäten wie der RWTH Aachen verbinden wir Wissenschaft und Praxis.

Begeisterung wird bei uns großgeschrieben und bestimmt unseren Arbeitsalltag – denn sie ist der beste Antrieb.

Title: Senior Data Analyst, Customer Care Analytics
Description:
Change the way the world travels

Join the GetYourGuide journey to connect people with unforgettable travel experiences around the world. Millions look to us for unique activities they can trust, and it’s all powered by our commitment to make every single journey extraordinary - including yours.

Ready to unlock your potential with a community of fellow explorers? Find your next role at our Berlin HQ or one of our local offices around the globe, from New York to Bangkok. Head to getyourguide.careers to take the first step.

Team mission

In this analytics role, you will be the key analytical partner to Customer Care — the team that supports travelers when things don’t go as planned and protects trust in the GetYourGuide experience. You will work closely with Care leadership, Product, and the broader Analytics organization to ensure Care’s biggest decisions are grounded in rigorous measurement and clear trade-offs.

Care is inherently cross-domain: it spans Traveler (On-Trip), Supplier, and Partner Operations. You’ll be embedded enough to build context and influence, while staying anchored in Analytics to uphold standards, peer review, and alignment with company-wide metrics.

Your mission


Own the analytical problems that matter most for Care — define and drive the strategic Care analytics backlog and turn it into recommendations that shape priorities, policies, and investments.
Build the measurement frameworks Care relies on — create and evolve metrics, models, and dashboards that make efficiency, friction, and outcomes measurable and self-serve.
Deliver deep-dive modeling that unlocks step-change decisions — quantify drivers and trade-offs behind contacts, refunds, CSAT, and channel performance, including LTV-aware policy logic.
Be Care's analytical voice across the company for Travelers, Suppliers and Partners — turn what Care learns from travelers into insights Product, Marketing, and Operations can act on, aligned with On-Trip and Destination growth strategy.
Champion hypothesis-driven decision-making — raise the bar on rigor, evaluation, and impact measurement so Care decisions are consistently defensible and outcome-driven.


Your toolkit


5+ years of relevant experience interpreting data in an analytics or a data science role
Production-grade SQL and strong working knowledge of Python (e.g., notebooks, modeling, analysis workflows).
Experience with experimentation (statistics, assumptions, and evaluation).
Strong statistical background and proficiency with advanced statistical methods (e.g. causal inference methods, difference-in-differences, regression analysis, propensity score matching)
Strong stakeholder management and the ability to operate with senior leaders in a high-context, ambiguous environment.
Proven ability to turn messy questions into structured analysis, crisp narratives, and decisions with measurable business impact.
Strong customer mindset: you understand user behavior and the “why” behind the numbers.
Excellent communication skills — you can influence without authority and align teams around shared metrics and priorities.
Experience with data visualization and reporting tools (e.g., Looker, Jupyter Notebooks).


Extras that give you an edge


Experience working in Customer Care within a marketplace.
Familiarity with ETL/orchestration tools (e.g., Airflow) and/or big data tooling (e.g., Spark, Hive).
Experience working in agile product teams and cross-functional mission setups.
Exposure to LLM APIs (OpenAI, Gemini, Anthropic), especially for operational tooling or workflow augmentation.
Experience mentoring or managing analysts.

Annual personal growth budget and mentorship programs for continuous learning and development
Work from anywhere in the world for 30 days per year
A hybrid working approach with three days of in office collaboration (Mon, Tue, Thur) and two days of optional at home focus time.
Opportunities to collaborate and socialize with team members through quarterly team events and yearly company-wide events
Monthly transportation and fitness budget

Change the way the world travels

Join the GetYourGuide journey to connect people with unforgettable travel experiences around the world. Millions look to us for unique activities they can trust, and it’s all powered by our commitment to make every single journey extraordinary - including yours.

Ready to unlock your potential with a community of fellow explorers? Find your next role at our Berlin HQ or one of our local offices around the globe, from New York to Bangkok. Head to getyourguide.careers to take the first step.

Team mission

In this analytics role, you will be the key analytical partner to Customer Care — the team that supports travelers when things don’t go as planned and protects trust in the GetYourGuide experience. You will work closely with Care leadership, Product, and the broader Analytics organization to ensure Care’s biggest decisions are grounded in rigorous measurement and clear trade-offs.

Care is inherently cross-domain: it spans Traveler (On-Trip), Supplier, and Partner Operations. You’ll be embedded enough to build context and influence, while staying anchored in Analytics to uphold standards, peer review, and alignment with company-wide metrics.

Your mission

  • Own the analytical problems that matter most for Care — define and drive the strategic Care analytics backlog and turn it into recommendations that shape priorities, policies, and investments.
  • Build the measurement frameworks Care relies on — create and evolve metrics, models, and dashboards that make efficiency, friction, and outcomes measurable and self-serve.
  • Deliver deep-dive modeling that unlocks step-change decisions — quantify drivers and trade-offs behind contacts, refunds, CSAT, and channel performance, including LTV-aware policy logic.
  • Be Care's analytical voice across the company for Travelers, Suppliers and Partners — turn what Care learns from travelers into insights Product, Marketing, and Operations can act on, aligned with On-Trip and Destination growth strategy.
  • Champion hypothesis-driven decision-making — raise the bar on rigor, evaluation, and impact measurement so Care decisions are consistently defensible and outcome-driven.

Your toolkit

  • 5+ years of relevant experience interpreting data in an analytics or a data science role
  • Production-grade SQL and strong working knowledge of Python (e.g., notebooks, modeling, analysis workflows).
  • Experience with experimentation (statistics, assumptions, and evaluation).
  • Strong statistical background and proficiency with advanced statistical methods (e.g. causal inference methods, difference-in-differences, regression analysis, propensity score matching)
  • Strong stakeholder management and the ability to operate with senior leaders in a high-context, ambiguous environment.
  • Proven ability to turn messy questions into structured analysis, crisp narratives, and decisions with measurable business impact.
  • Strong customer mindset: you understand user behavior and the “why” behind the numbers.
  • Excellent communication skills — you can influence without authority and align teams around shared metrics and priorities.
  • Experience with data visualization and reporting tools (e.g., Looker, Jupyter Notebooks).

  • Experience working in agile product teams and cross-functional mission setups.

  • Experience mentoring or managing analysts.
