Title: (Jr.) Operations Systems Analyst (m/f/d)
Description:
About HOLY


HOLY is the soft drink revolution: With our powdered drinks HOLY Energy®, HOLY Hydration®, HOLY Iced Tea®, and HOLY Syrup Bottle® you get amazing taste across 50+ flavors while maintaining a good conscience—for your body & the environment.
Over 2M loyal customers: Started in a shared apartment by three best friends, we have scaled to Europe's fastest growing consumer brand—and our cult-like community, the HOLY Squad, loves it: 4.6 stars on reviews.io speak for themselves.
From D2C to Global Omnichannel: In less than four years, we've profitably scaled HOLY to more than €180M D2C revenue in DACH, FR, and the UK. Now scaling our retail business, we're on an exciting transition to becoming a true omnichannel rocketship.
We are still just getting started: Backed by the world's best consumer investors, we aim to revolutionize the soft drink industry and build a generational love brand: tastier, healthier & more sustainable. Our team of 170+ people aims high (Coca-Cola, we're coming for you!).


About The Position

The Operations Systems Analyst is the guardian of HOLY's data backbone — ensuring our ERP operates as the single source of truth that supply chain, finance, and analytics teams depend on every day. This role sits at the intersection of operations, data, and systems: investigating discrepancies, enforcing data standards, and building automated checks that prevent issues before they reach downstream teams.

As HOLY scales, this person will evolve from reactive troubleshooting into proactive data governance, making them a critical enabler of operational reliability across the business.

Responsibilities:


Data Integrity Ownership & Discrepancy Resolution: Lead the investigation and resolution of data discrepancies across Odoo ERP, WMS, and PIM — whether originating from user input, API syncs, or EDI integrations. Ensure that inventory quantities, product records, and transactional data remain consistent so operations, supply chain, and finance teams can execute on numbers they trust.
Master Data Standards & User Enablement: Enforce and maintain data entry standards across all ERP users — catching miscoded products, incorrect quantities, and improperly configured records before they cascade into reporting errors or blocked integrations. Onboard new users to correct data practices and drive targeted corrections when standards are breached.
Cross-System Reconciliation & Monitoring: Build and own regular reconciliation checks between Odoo, WMS, and PIM, developing automated monitoring where possible to proactively surface mismatches on inventory, shipments, and product data. Monitor API and EDI integration flows for sync failures or data mismatches and coordinate resolution with the relevant technical teams.
Pattern Recognition & Systemic Improvement: Track and document recurring data issues and system errors, identifying underlying patterns that signal root-cause problems rather than one-off incidents. Collaborate with technical and operational stakeholders to fix systemic causes and reduce the volume of repeated reactive fixes.
Process Design, Adherence & AI-Driven Automation: Document operational processes clearly, identify where they're being bypassed or degraded, and drive the behavioral and system changes needed to close gaps. Use AI tools to build automated adherence checks and monitoring solutions that scale process compliance without manual overhead.


About You


ERP & data fluency: You have 2–4 years of hands-on experience working with ERP systems (Odoo strongly preferred; NetSuite or Business Central also relevant) and understand how supply chain, finance, and analytics teams interdependencies
Detail-obsessed: You catch discrepancies others miss — whether it's a mismatched inventory quantity, an API response that doesn't add up, or a product record configured just slightly wrong.
Structured diagnostic thinker: You approach problems systematically — separating symptoms from root causes, documenting findings clearly, and knowing when to escalate versus resolve independently. You'd rather fix the pattern than patch the same issue twice.
Technically self-sufficient: You can navigate ERP, WMS, and PIM systems independently — using logs, documentation, hands-on testing, and AI tools to get to root cause without waiting for someone to point you in the right direction. SQL proficiency is a strong plus.
Clear cross-functional communicator: You're comfortable working with procurement, logistics, and accounting teams — translating technical findings into language that non-technical stakeholders can act on, and driving resolution without unnecessary back-and-forth.
AI & automation mindset: You actively look for opportunities to replace manual monitoring and repeated fixes with automated checks and AI-assisted workflows. You see tooling as leverage, not a nice-to-have.


Our Offering

In today's job market, A-players have many options to choose from. As part of our offer, we prioritise providing a productive working environment and opportunities for growth. In addition to competitive compensation, including company shares, you can expect:


A successful and growing company: Join a company that has been growing profitable and quickly for the last 36+ months and have raised a series B, while many other e-commerce companies are struggling.
Huge impact and career growth: Enjoy huge impact and hence explosive career growth, build your own market, and shape HOLY’s internationalisation in a top market
Awesome team: Join an awesome team of colleagues who are ambitious, funny, down-to-earth, and authentic. Get to know us and see for yourself!
Top product: Marketing is important, but a good product is essential. Our differentiated soft drinks have won over customers. Check out our Trustpilot reviews or contact us for free samples.
Future of e-commerce: Be part of the future of e-commerce and help set new standards in many areas. Work with the best minds in the industry.
Startup culture: Join a startup culture that values flat hierarchies, fast pace, and personal development. With us, you can make a real impact.
Perfect setup: Enjoy a perfect setup that includes all the requirements of a modern employer, such as a central office in Berlin Prenzlauerberg, flexible working hours, and much more.
How we support your growth: flexible hours, 28 vacation days, 30 work-from-abroad days, €1,500 learning budget, Urban Sports Club membership, 63€ public transport subsidy, and biannual 360° feedback tied to your compensation.


Find out more about HOLY and our team on our careers page.

The Process

As we are experiencing rapid growth and have ambitious plans, the optimal start date for the position is today. Nonetheless, we are prepared to patiently await the arrival of the ideal candidate, recognizing that quality ultimately yields the best results.


Getting to know you: Eric, our Talent Acquisition Manager will have a 30 minute screening interview where we will talk to you more about HOLY and the position and see if our expectations match.
Hiring Manager interview: Sofiia, our Sr. Ops Project Manager will dive a bit deeper into your experience and details of the role with you.
Case study & on-site interview: As soon as you have successfully completed the first interviews, you will receive a case study. In a second meeting at our office, the team will discuss your case study with you and answer questions about our workspace, the HOLY Temple.
Reference calls: At this stage, we’d like to connect with 1–2 former colleagues or managers who have worked closely with you. These conversations help us better understand your working style and strengths from those who know you professionally.
Interview with Frederick: As a strong cultural fit is important to us, you will have a final meeting with Frederick, one of our HOLY Founders. This gives both you and us the opportunity for a final check.
Job offer and decision: If everything goes well, you will receive an offer and we are ready to embark you this journey together with you!


Typically his process should take around 2 weeks.

Any questions or concerns?

If you have any questions or feedback, please send an email to jobs@weareholy.com.

Saarbrücker Str. 37

10405, Berlin

Title: Sr. Digital Analyst – Tesla Energy EMEA (m/w/d)
Description:
What To Expect
Please submit your CV in English in order to be considered for the role.

Data is deeply embedded in the product, engineering, quality, and customer experience culture at Tesla. This role will collaborate across Demand Generation, Marketing, Web Development, Software, and executive teams to optimize the Web and Digital Customer Experience for Tesla Energy for all of Europe.

This position requires an in-depth understanding of digital marketing best practices, web UI/UX, web traffic data, web marketing channels, first through third party data, and data analysis.

Web Digital Platforms Analyst at Tesla supports campaign planning, site optimization, campaign performance, and data activation.

What You'll Do


Lead web analytics for Tesla Energy in Europe, optimizing customer conversion and interaction
Design Tests and Audit performance in paid media campaigns (Google, Meta, X, and others)
Design and conduct A/B tests on web pages to evaluate performance and drive improvements
Analyze web traffic data to recommend UX enhancements and ensure tagging/tracking compliance
Combine marketing channel data with CRM insights for audience sizing and campaign optimization
Deploy dashboards and conduct impact analysis of business process changes on customer experience
Understand multi-channel marketing campaigns and develop explanatory performance models



What You'll Bring


Relevant enterprise-level experience in web analytics, digital marketing, or data science
Business-level communication skills in German and English (Italian a plus)
Expertise in BigQuery, GA4 funnels, A/B test configuration, and Google Tag Manager
Proficiency in statistical tools (significance tests, regression, time series analysis)
Strong ability to use BI tools (Looker Studio, Tableau, PowerBI) and write SQL
Experience blending web data with CRM/databases across multiple systems
Bonus: Familiarity with UX design, Design Thinking, and data taxonomy (DMP/CDP), Optimizely

Title: Senior Data Analyst, Real Estate & Motors (d/f/m)
Description:
Job Description

Kleinanzeigen is the leading online classifieds market in Germany. Users buy and sell on Germany's No. 1 for classifieds mainly second-hand. In this way they make an active contribution to more sustainability. On average, more than 55 million ads are available in numerous categories – from children's supplies to electronics and real estate. Kleinanzeigen also offers small and medium-sized businesses the opportunity to present their services online. Around 35 million users per month make Kleinanzeigen one of the most widely used websites in Germany. Kleinanzeigen was launched in September 2009 as eBay Kleinanzeigen. Since June 2021, the company has been part of Adevinta, a global provider of online classified ads. In May 2023, the name was changed to Kleinanzeigen.

The Job

As a Data Analyst in the Real Estate & Motors Analytics team, you help shape data-driven decisions across Product, Business, and Sales functions. You turn complex data into actionable insights, support experimentation and forecasting initiatives, and contribute to improving our products and user experience. Working closely with cross-functional stakeholders in a collaborative Data and Analytics setup, you help teams identify impactful opportunities and drive informed decision-making through analytics and clear communication. This is a hybrid role, our office is in Berlin-Charlottenburg. You report to the Analytics Lead, Real Estate.

We're looking for someone to join us on a limited 1-year contract.

Job requirements

Who You Are


You bring strong SQL skills and hands-on experience in product and business analytics, experimentation, and KPI analysis.
You communicate effectively with technical and non-technical stakeholders and build strong cross-functional relationships.
You demonstrate an ownership mindset, strong prioritization skills, and adaptability in dynamic environments.
You have experience with BI and visualization tools such as Tableau and understand tracking concepts and data quality validation.
You bring skills to adapt automation and AI where it creates value.
Nice to have: exposure to forecasting, Python, and modern analytics tooling such as DBT.


Job responsibilities

What You’ll Do


Partner with Product, Business, and Sales stakeholders to deliver actionable insights and support data-driven decision making.
Own analytics topics, including tracking definition, funnel analysis, KPI monitoring, and performance reporting for product, business and sales.
Support A/B testing initiatives through experiment setup, analysis, and measurement of feature performance and business impact.
Build and maintain dashboards, reports, and analytical deep dives for the Real Estate & Motors domain. To “get the data ready”, you will contribute to the semantic layer and data model.
Collaborate closely with Analysts, Data Engineers, and Product Managers in a cross-functional DnA environment.
Contribute to forecasting, planning, and operational analytics initiatives to support business growth and prioritization.
Utilize state-of-the-art AI tools to perform your work efficiently by balancing manual effort with automation.


Job benefits

At Kleinanzeigen, we care about our people — their wellbeing, growth, and life beyond work. Our benefits are designed to support you holistically, giving you flexibility and opportunities to thrive.

✨ Flexibility


Flexible working time (hybrid)
20 days “work from anywhere”
Paid additional parental leave for both parents
Extra paid days off for special occasions (e.g. your wedding or a move)


❤️‍🩹 Wellbeing & Support


Confidential support & coaching for you and your loved ones
Annual company sports events
Pension and life insurance


📚 Growth & Development


Personal development budget
Learning opportunities to grow your skills and career


🛍️ Perks & Everyday benefits


Corporate benefits and exclusive discounts
An attractive referral program
Transportation allowance
Free shipping on Kleinanzeigen Direktkauf


Kleinanzeigen is an equal opportunity employer that values diversity and inclusion. We welcome people of all backgrounds, races, religions, colours, national origins, gender identities, sexual orientation, ages, marital status, or disability.

If this role sparks your interest but you’re not sure you meet every single requirement, we’d still love to hear from you anyway.

  • Collaborate closely with Analysts, Data Engineers, and Product Managers in a cross-functional DnA environment.
  • Contribute to forecasting, planning, and operational analytics initiatives to support business growth and prioritization.
  • Utilize state-of-the-art AI tools to perform your work efficiently by balancing manual effort with automation.

Job benefits

At Kleinanzeigen, we care about our people — their wellbeing, growth, and life beyond work. Our benefits are designed to support you holistically, giving you flexibility and opportunities to thrive.

✨ Flexibility

  • Flexible working time (hybrid)
  • 20 days “work from anywhere”
  • Paid additional parental leave for both parents
  • Extra paid days off for special occasions (e.g. your wedding or a move)

❤️‍🩹 Wellbeing & Support

  • Confidential support & coaching for you and your loved ones
  • Annual company sports events
  • Pension and life insurance

📚 Growth & Development

  • Personal development budget
  • Learning opportunities to grow your skills and career

🛍️ Perks & Everyday benefits

  • Corporate benefits and exclusive discounts
  • An attractive referral program
  • Transportation allowance
  • Free shipping on Kleinanzeigen Direktkauf

Kleinanzeigen is an equal opportunity employer that values diversity and inclusion. We welcome people of all backgrounds, races, religions, colours, national origins, gender identities, sexual orientation, ages, marital status, or disability.

If this role sparks your interest but you’re not sure you meet every single requirement, we’d still love to hear from you anyway.

Title: Werkstudent Business Intelligence (m/w/d)
Description:
About MYNE

Wir sind MYNE Homes - der cleverste Weg in die eigenen Traum-Ferienimmobilie. Mit unserem innovativen Co-Ownership Angebot geben wir mehr Menschen die Möglichkeit, sich ihren Traum vom eigenen Ferienhaus zu erfüllen. Gegründet von Serial Entrepreneurs und unterstützt von einigen der renommiertesten Business Angels in Europa (Gründer von Auto1, Smava und Numa) wachsen wir schnell und suchen dich um uns zu unterstützen das nächste Proptech Unicorn aufzubauen!

Als Werkstudent Business Intelligence (m/w/d) unterstützt Du die Super-User unseres Marketingteams und sammelst dabei wertvolle Erfahrungen in einem dynamischen Unternehmensumfeld. Diese Position bietet Dir eine hervorragende Gelegenheit, praxisnahe Fähigkeiten in der Datenanalyse, Business Intelligence und im Bereich Marketing Analytics zu entwickeln.

Deine Aufgaben


Du wirst ab Tag eins vollwertiges Mitglied des Business Intelligence-Teams mit Zugang zum Data Warehouse und allen relevanten Tools.
Du unterstützt die Super-User im Marketingteam bei Datenanalysen, Reportings und wöchentlichen Insights.
Du lernst neue Tools und Technologien kennen und bleibst nah an aktuellen Business-Entwicklungen.
Du übernimmst mit wachsender Erfahrung mehr Verantwortung, z. B. bei Code Reviews.


Dein Profil


Du studierst Wirtschaftswissenschaften, (Wirtschafts-)Informatik oder einen vergleichbaren Studiengang.
Du bist an einer Berliner Hochschule eingeschrieben und hast noch mindestens 12 Monate bis zur Abschlussarbeit.
Du bist 16–20 Stunden pro Woche verfügbar, idealerweise an 2–3 festen Wochentagen.
Du lernst schnell und passt Dich gut an ein dynamisches Umfeld an.
idealerweise beherrschst du G-Sheets und Excel, inklusive Funktionen wie VLOOKUP und Pivot-Tabellen.
Du sprichst fließend Englisch und sehr gut Deutsch.


Kein Muss Aber Von Vorteil


Du hast erste Erfahrungen mit SQL, dbt und/oder version control tools wie Git.
Du kennst Dich mit Performance-Marketing-Kanälen wie Google Ads, Meta o. ä. aus.
Du hast bereits in schnelllebigen Umfeldern wie Startups, Beratung, Investment Banking oder Private Equity Praktika gearbeitet.


Warum wir?


Die Chance eines der führenden Proptech-Startups aktiv mit aufzubauen und zu gestalten.
Du arbeitest direkt mit erfahrenen Gründern und hochkarätigen Investoren aus der Technologie- und Immobilienwelt zusammen.
Verantwortungsvolle und abwechslungsreiche Aufgaben mit Freiraum für eigene Ideen.
Motiviertes und ergebnisorientiertes Team, flache Hierarchien und kurze Entscheidungswege.
Kompetitives Gehalt und die üblichen Startup Benefits.
Und natürlich: Aufenthalte in unseren Traum-Ferienimmobilien!


Über uns

Unsere Mission

Wir sind MYNE Homes - der cleverste Weg in die eigene Traum-Ferienimmobilie. Mit unserem innovativen Co-Ownership Angebot geben wir mehr Menschen die Möglichkeit, sich ihren Traum vom eigenen Ferienhaus zu erfüllen. Gegründet von Serial Entrepreneurs und unterstützt von einigen der renommiertesten Business Angels in Europa (Gründer von Auto1, Smava und Numa), wachsen wir schnell und suchen dich, um gemeinsam das nächste Proptech Unicorn aufzubauen!

Was treibt uns an

Wir wollen mehr Menschen die Möglichkeit geben, sich ihren Traum von der eigenen Ferienimmobilie zu erfüllen.

Title: Werkstudent:in Data Science
Description:
Über nerou




Die Datenanalyse-Software von nerou unterstützt Betreiber von Kläranlagen bei täglichen Entscheidungen in der Anlagensteuerung. In Kläranlagen müssen komplexe Entscheidungen getroffen werden, um die Anlage auf sich täglich ändernde Einflüsse einzustellen. Alle relevanten Daten werden von Machine Learning Algorithmen analysiert und ausgewertet. Die gewonnen Erkenntnisse werden in Form von Entscheidungshilfen an den Kunden weitergeleitet. Dies reduziert nicht nur Kosten für die Betreiber, sondern schont so auch Ressourcen und Umwelt. Zur Verstärkung unseres Data Science-Bereichs suchen wir nun Dich!







Deine Tätigkeiten



Du extrahierst und validierst große Datenbestände auf Grundlage verschiedener Fragestellungen
Du analysierst Datensätze mit geeigneten statistischen Methoden
Du ergänzt unser motiviertes Team mit Machine Learning- und Data Science-Expertise






Deine Erfahrung



Du studierst (Wirtschafts-)Informatik, Mathematik oder etwas vergleichbares
Du hast Erfahrung im Umgang mit Data Science und Machine Learning
Du hast grundliegende Kenntnisse mit Python
Du bist in der Lage, deine Arbeit selbstständig zu strukturieren






Was wir dir bieten




Eine Menge Spaß, versprochen! Du wirst als Werkstudent Teil eines jungen Start-ups und kannst dich mit deinen eigenen Ideen austoben. Du sammelst wertvolle Erfahrungen und hast dabei die Möglichkeit, eigene Projekte zu verwirklichen. Hierbei erhältst du zudem wertvolle Einblicke in das Thema Unternehmensgründung und Start-up. Bei uns kannst du dir deine Arbeitszeit frei einteilen und hast die Wahl zwischen Büro und Home Office - somit bist du auch neben deinem Studium flexibel.




Wenn es dir bei uns gefällt, freuen wir uns natürlich, wenn du lange bei uns bleibst - auch nach deinem Studium in einer Festanstellung! Interessiert? Dann ran an die Tasten! Sende uns deine Mail mit Anschreiben, Zeugnissen und Lebenslauf an jobs@nerou.de Wir freuen uns von dir zu hören und hoffen, dich schon bald in unserem Team begrüßen zu dürfen!

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
About Huzzle

At Huzzle, we connect exceptional talents with top opportunities at leading companies across the UK, US, Canada, Europe, and Australia. Our clients include startups, digital agencies, and tech platforms in industries such as SaaS, MarTech, FinTech, and AI.




Unlike an outsourcing agency, we place you directly with a client where you're hired in-house as a valued member of their team.




Job Type: Full-time

Location: On-site in Berlin, Germany




About the Company

Our client is an ambitious, venture-backed startup building an AI-powered SAP consultant designed to transform how enterprise systems are implemented and optimized. Already post-revenue and working with DAX 40 and Fortune 500 companies. Their mission is to encode and compound consulting knowledge across deployments, creating a system that becomes smarter with every customer engagement.




Job Summary

We are hiring an AI Data Engineer to design and build the foundational data layer that powers a next-generation AI system for SAP consulting. This role is critical in structuring complex enterprise data—capturing configurations, decisions, and relationships across deployments—and transforming it into scalable, queryable knowledge that improves model accuracy over time.

You will work at the intersection of data engineering and AI, ensuring that structured insights flow seamlessly into machine learning systems and continuously evolve with each customer interaction.




Key Responsibilities

Design and build a scalable data layer that maps relationships across SAP projects, including configurations, outcomes, and domain patterns
Own the metadata architecture, including data labeling, linking, versioning, and queryability
Develop robust data pipelines to ingest SAP telemetry and consulting insights into structured datasets
Collaborate closely with AI/ML teams to provide high-quality signals for model training and inference
Continuously refine and improve data systems with each customer deployment
Ensure high standards of data integrity, consistency, and usability across systems
Architect systems with a long-term, scalable mindset rather than short-term fixes




Requirements

Strong background in data engineering, including pipeline development, schema design, and data modeling
Experience working with complex, semi-structured enterprise data
Knowledge of graph-based systems (knowledge graphs, graph databases) or relational modeling for complex domains
Systems-level thinking with a focus on architecture and scalability
Proven ability to maintain high standards for data quality and reliability
Willingness to work on-site in Berlin (mandatory requirement)




Benefits

💰 Competitive salary aligned with top-tier European startup benchmarks

🌍 Work on-site in Berlin at a fast-growing, venture-backed AI company

📈 High-growth environment with strong funding and enterprise traction

🤝 Collaborate with elite engineers, AI researchers, and enterprise clients

🧠 Exposure to cutting-edge AI, data systems, and enterprise-scale challenges

Show more

Title: Data Engineer ll, Program Team (Tech Foundations)
Description:
As the world’s pioneering local delivery platform, our mission is to deliver an amazing experience, fast, easy, and to your door. We operate in over 70+ countries worldwide, powered by tech, designed by people. As one of Europe’s largest tech platforms, headquartered in Berlin, Germany. Delivery Hero has been listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange since 2017 and is part of the MDAX stock market index. We enable creative minds to deliver solutions that create impact within our ecosystem. We move fast, take action and adapt. No matter where you're from or what you believe in, we build, we deliver, we lead. We are Delivery Hero.

Job Description

We are on the lookout for a Data Engineer ll to join the Program team, known as the Catalyst for Agentic Transformation within Tech Foundations on our journey to always deliver amazing experiences.

As a member of the Tech Foundation Team, you’ll support Delivery Hero’s rapid innovation and growth. You’ll work on foundational systems that enable faster feature development, security, and reliability across our global engineering community. Every enhancement you make will contribute to our teams' ability to build, scale, and deliver quality features—ultimately impacting millions of users worldwide.

The Program Execution team is the centralized hub that connects our specialized agentic efforts with the broader organization. While engineering teams build the systems, this team ensures they are integrated, governed, and adopted group-wide. Their mission is to orchestrate a unified strategy, moving Delivery Hero from fragmented AI experimentation to a cohesive, high-velocity AI-native ecosystem.

This team is the essential glue between technical innovation and business impact. By centralizing vendor relations and compliance, they ensure that a breakthrough in one area (like automated coding) or a win in another (like non-tech process automation) becomes a standard for all.

As the team that ties everything together, they own the Northstar of 100% daily AI-usage across both engineering and enterprise functions. They bridge the gap between having a tool and using a tool by turning technical capabilities into daily habits for everyone at delivery hero.

What You Will Do


Impact Analysis & Reporting: Setting up a group-wide framework to track if our AI investment is working. Analyzing productivity data across all focus areas and turning it into clear ROI stories for leadership.
Advocacy & Evangelism: Acting as the voice of the program. Running roadshows and workshops to get both tech and non-tech teams excited, while addressing cultural concerns regarding how AI changes the nature of work.
Upskilling & Training: Building the curriculum with learning and development teams to help the entire workforce move past the initial learning curve. Creating train-the-trainer programs to empower local AI champions in every department.
Cross-Brand Orchestration: Connecting decentralized brands and verticals to ensure knowledge sharing and a unified AI experience across the entire Delivery Hero group.


Qualifications

Success in this team requires a blend of data-driven analysis and a passion for people-centric change management across diverse departments.

Analytical Expertise


Data Storytelling: The ability to take raw usage data and turn it into a compelling narrative about productivity and business value.
Metric Definition: Understanding how AI influences business processes and delivery cycles, and defining the KPIs to prove it.


Advocacy & Soft Skills


High-Energy Evangelism: A genuine passion for AI that is contagious. You must be comfortable presenting to large groups—from engineers to business leads—and handling skepticism with empathy.
Change Management: Experience helping large organizations adopt new habits. You understand that cultural shifts are the hardest part of any technological rollout.


Strategic Mindset


Problem-Solving: The ability to navigate administrative or technical blockers (legal, security, or regional hurdles) to keep the program’s momentum high.
Collaborative Diplomacy: Building strong relationships across different brands to drive a central vision without stepping on local autonomy.


Additional Information

Who We Are

As the world’s pioneering local delivery platform, our mission is to deliver an amazing experience, fast, easy, and to your door. We operate in around 70 countries worldwide, powered by tech, designed by people. As one of Europe’s largest tech platforms, headquartered in Berlin, Germany. Delivery Hero has been listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange since 2017 and is part of the MDAX stock market index. We enable creative minds to deliver solutions that create impact within our ecosystem. We move fast, take action and adapt. No matter where you're from or what you believe in, we build, we deliver, we lead. We are Delivery Hero.

Ensuring you and all our Heroes are looked after, happy, and healthy is always on the menu. Because if you’re in good shape, then we’re in good shape.


Make the most of our hybrid working model and join the team for face-to-face connection and collaboration in our beautiful Berlin campus 2 days a week
We offer 27 days holiday with an extra day on 2nd and 3rd year of service
We will support you in developing yourself and your career growth opportunities: 1.000 € Educational Budget, Language Courses, Parental Support and access to the Udemy Business platform to explore a variety of online courses.
Get moving and release those wonderful, mind-boosting endorphins: Health Checkups, Meditation, Gym & Bicycle Subsidy
Cash. Dough. Cheddar. Whatever you call it, we’ll help you with it: Employee Share Purchase Plan, Sabbatical Bank, Public Transportation Ticket Discount, Life & Accident Insurance, Corporate Pension Plan
The power of getting together over some food is unrivaled. Here are a few ways to help you do that. All the yum: Digital Meal Vouchers, Food Vouchers, Corporate Discounts. Courses.
Wondering what relocating to Berlin is like? In this article, we’ve put together 10 things you should know about moving to Berlin and how Delivery Hero can support you. You can also visit our relocation hub and check out more information about moving to Berlin.
Ready to prepare for your interview? Check out the list of the 5 most common interview questions and answers created in collaboration with our recruiters.


Ready to join our team? If you’re excited to grow, collaborate and be part of the world’s leading delivery platform, we’d love to hear from you. Apply today!

We believe diversity and inclusion are key to creating not only an exciting product, but also an amazing customer and employee experience. Fostering this starts with hiring - therefore we do not discriminate on the basis of racial identities, religious beliefs, color, national origin, gender identities or expressions, sexual orientations, age, marital or disability statuses, or any other aspect that makes you, you.

We encourage you to let us know if you need any accommodations or specific accessibility support to ensure a smooth interview experience—just let us know with an email to our Inclusion Officer at inclusion@deliveryhero.com.

Severely disabled applicants with equal qualifications will be given preferential consideration.

You're welcome to share your pronouns (he/she/they) right from the start so we can address you respectfully from our first contact.

Ready to join our team? If you’re excited to grow, collaborate and be part of the world’s leading delivery platform, we’d love to hear from you. Apply today!

We believe diversity and inclusion are key to creating not only an exciting product, but also an amazing customer and employee experience. We encourage you to let us know if you need any accommodations or specific accessibility support to ensure a smooth interview experience—just let us know with an email to our Inclusion Officer at inclusion@deliveryhero.com.

Title: Senior Analyst, In-Field Repair Quality
Description:
As a global leader in micromobility, Lime is on a mission to build a future where transportation is shared, affordable and carbon-free. A Time Magazine 100 Most Influential Company, Lime has powered more than one billion rides in close to 30 countries across five continents, spurring a new generation of clean alternatives to car ownership. Learn more at li.me.

Lime is hiring a Senior Analyst to join our Global Operations team. In this role, you'll be at the forefront of improving in-field repair quality performance— developing key metrics, building tools that drive real-time visibility, and delivering targeted analysis that informs operational decisions and strategic opportunities.

You'll serve as the functional bridge between Local Operations and our Product organization, translating operational needs into structured data requirements and tooling improvements. You'll collaborate closely with Local Operations, Product, Data Science, and Hardware teams to help shape the future of Lime's in-field repair quality across all of our operations as we continue to scale.

The ideal candidate is highly analytical, technically strong, and passionate about using data to solve complex problems. You're equally comfortable partnering with local operations teams and product or engineering stakeholders — and you know how to both structure a process and build a dashboard. If you have 3+ years of experience in business analytics or operations, strong data analysis and visualization skills, and a drive to influence outcomes through insight, we'd love to hear from you.

This is a hybrid role open to candidates located in London, Paris, or Berlin. The role requires occasional travel (~10%) to Lime warehouses or other locations for team meetings.

What You’ll Do


Improve visibility into in-field repair quality: Define and maintain metrics to track repair performance, effectiveness, compliance, efficiency, and ROI — and build tooling to surface these insights in real time across operations
Build models and dashboards: Use SQL, Tableau, and spreadsheets to develop repair volume forecasting models, quality compliance dashboards, and performance reports for local operations teams — with robust logic, clearly defined assumptions, and intuitive interfaces
Monitor trends and diagnose performance gaps: Track repair efficiency and quality compliance over time, identify root causes behind variances or emerging trends, and recommend actionable solutions at both tactical and strategic levels
Turn operational complexity into structured clarity: Bridge Local Operations and Product/Tech by converting messy, real-world business needs into crisp specs, user stories, data requirements, and SOPs that teams can actually execute against
Formalize and improve operational processes: Map and document quality workflows, surface inefficiencies, and partner with Local Operations to define and roll out improved standard operating procedures
Collaborate with partner teams to improve efficiency: Provide insight and targeted analysis to troubleshoot issues or highlight opportunities. Partner with Operations leadership and local teams to land recommendations and drive action through to resolution
Drive continuous improvement in data analytics capabilities across global operations: Contribute to a culture of data-driven decision making and analytical excellence through peer collaboration, knowledge sharing, and support


About You


Extremely strong proficiency in data analytics, including advanced SQL, spreadsheet modeling, and data visualization (e.g. Tableau or equivalent).
Bachelor's degree in Business, Mathematics, Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field, or equivalent industry experience
3+ years of experience in business analytics or operations analytics, with demonstrated ability to build reports and dashboards used by operational stakeholders
Experience translating business needs into functional data and reporting requirements, ideally working closely with a Product or Engineering team
Ability to structure and document operational processes, comfortable with ambiguity and working from first principles when standard frameworks don't exist
Curious mindset to spot trends in data, deep-dive root causes, and generate proposals for both tactical and strategic solutions
Clear verbal and written communicator, with strong collaboration and influencing skills across both field teams and technical stakeholders


Preferred Experience


Python, Google Apps Script, or other automation/scripting capabilities are a strong plus
Previous experience in a fast-paced, scaling operations environment (gig economy, last-mile, field services, or similar)
Experience working closely with Product or Engineering teams on tooling/product improvements


If you want to make an impact, Lime is the place for you. Not sure if you meet all the qualifications? If this role excites you we encourage you to apply. Explore all opportunities on our career page.

Lime is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We believe different perspectives help us grow and achieve more. That’s why we’re dedicated to building and developing a team that reflects a wider range of backgrounds, abilities, identities, and experiences. If you require a reasonable accommodation during the application or hiring process, please email recruiting-operations@li.me for assistance.

Title: Initiative Application
Description:
Empower every employee.

Our mission is to be the world's most used AI employee experience platform by changing the way frontline employees work.

Flip is the leading AI-powered employee experience platform for frontline workers. We're transforming how the people who keep the world running — in retail, manufacturing, and logistics — do their jobs. One app. One touch. Everything they need.

Our mission: Connect every employee to everything they need in one touch.

You appreciate flat hierarchies, short decision-making paths and live the hands-on mentality? You want to actively shape our journey instead of just being part of it and want to help shape Flip's vision?

Then we should get to know each other!

Apply on your own initiative and let us know in which area you would like to contribute your talents.

Let our employees convince you: https://www.kununu.com/de/flip-app/kommentare

What we offer you


Work mode: We're remote-first, giving you flexibility to work from home. At the same time, we deeply value the power of in-person collaboration. Depending on the role, you'll join occasional team events, workshops, or meetings in our Berlin or Stuttgart offices - always with plenty of notice. The exact balance will be discussed during your interview.
Work-Life-Balance: We don't want you to grow roots to your desk chair. That's why we cover the costs of your E-Gym-Wellpass membership and offer job bike leasing.
Celebrating success: Expect highly motivated and committed people in a relaxed working atmosphere.
Be part of something bigger: You actively shape Flip in your role. Along the way, you are an enabler of the rapid growth process of a young tech company and grow towards your goals, fun is guaranteed.
Happy to be a Flipster: Stay tuned for regular team events and culture days that bring us together as Flipsters.
Working abroad: At Flip you can also work abroad in the European Union. Let's talk about remote work in the interview.


At Flip, everyone is welcome - no matter what gender you identify as or how old you are. Sexual identity, origin, religion, world view and disabilities do not influence your potential job at Flip. The most important thing is that YOU fit in!

Title: Data Engineer (Mid-level) (m/w/d)
Description:
Company Description Haensel AMS—Advanced Mathematical Solutions—is a technology and data analytics boutique specializing in innovative machine learning and big data solutions. The company leverages advanced mathematical methods to design tailored data-driven strategies that address the specific needs of each client. Working closely with partners across industries, Haensel AMS turns complex data into actionable insights and measurable value. Team members collaborate in an environment that combines technical excellence, analytical rigor, and practical business impact. This setting offers professionals the opportunity to work on challenging projects with modern technologies and methods.




Role Description As a Mid-level Data Engineer at Haensel AMS, you will design, build, and maintain data pipelines that support advanced analytics and machine learning use cases. In close collaboration with data scientists and analysts, you will develop scalable ETL processes, integrate data from multiple sources, and ensure data is clean, reliable, and well-structured. You will contribute to data modeling and data warehouse design, optimize performance, and monitor data quality in production environments. Your work will include documenting data flows, automating workflows, and supporting stakeholders with data access and tooling. This is a full-time, on-site role based in Berlin, Germany.




Qualifications

Strong data engineering skills, including experience with Data Engineering concepts and building robust data pipelines.
Proficiency in Data Modeling and designing scalable data architectures to support analytics and reporting needs.
Hands-on experience with Extract Transform Load (ETL) processes and tools to integrate and transform data from diverse sources.
Knowledge of Data Warehousing concepts and implementation, including schema design and performance optimization.
Ability to apply Data Analytics techniques to validate, profile, and interpret data for business and technical stakeholders.
Proficiency in at least one programming language commonly used in data engineering (e.g., Python, Scala, or Java) and SQL.
Experience with modern data platforms or cloud ecosystems (e.g., AWS, GCP, or Azure) and related services is beneficial.
Familiarity with workflow orchestration, version control, and CI/CD practices for data solutions is an advantage.
Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience.
Strong problem-solving skills, attention to detail, and the ability to work collaboratively in a cross-functional, inclusive team.

Title: Web Analyst (m/f/d) - Marketing & Data
Description:
We are home24, one of the leading home & living e-commerce platforms in continental Europe. We utilize data to provide personalized recommendations, tips, and inspiration for every home. We are looking for a Web Analyst (m/f/d) to join our team. In this role, you will support our marketing teams by ensuring data quality, maintaining our tracking setup, and delivering actionable insights through data visualization.

Your Responsibilities


You drive the implementation and maintenance of tracking concepts in GA4 and sGTM, refining event structures and debugging discrepancies to ensure delivery of high-quality data e.g. for attribution modeling and the design and evaluation of A/B tests
You evaluate the performance of marketing campaigns across various channels
You don't just deliver numbers, but interpret data to help the team understand trends and optimize ROI
You build and maintain interactive dashboards (e.g., in Tableau or Looker) to monitor key marketing metrics, translating business requirements into clear visualizations
You look beyond standard KPIs to investigate the "why" behind the data, conducting ad-hoc deep dives to uncover the root causes of performance shifts and user behavior
You act as a contact person for analytical questions regarding GA4, helping stakeholders understand their data usage needs
You monitor the data flow to ensure accuracy and support the team in identifying and fixing bugs in the tracking landscape


Your Profile


You have professional experience in web analytics, data analysis, or a related field, ideally within an e-commerce environment
You have hands-on experience with Google Analytics 4 (GA4) and (server-side) Google Tag Manager (sGTM)
You are proficient in SQL, capable of merging and transforming large-scale datasets from diverse sources. Proficiency in Python is a strong plus for building scalable data solutions
You have a basic understanding of HTML, CSS, and JavaScript to comprehend how tracking is implemented
Your mindset combines natural curiosity for new trends with a sharp eye for detail, ensuring your end-to-end thinking always aligns with the bigger picture
You have excellent English skills and can communicate your findings in a clear, structured way
You thrive in cross-functional collaboration, acting as a bridge between marketing, data, and tech teams


What We Offer You

Your impact


The chance to make an impact in a highly motivated team of professionals
An open-minded culture ready to incorporate new and creative ideas


Your growth


Extensive learning opportunities from our home24 academy and external partners
Room for growth and development within your role through regular feedback rounds


Your path


Hybrid working model: you have the flexibility to work from home on Wednesdays and Fridays in our standard working mode. We are in the office together Mondays, Tuesdays and Thursdays
The possibility to work up to two months per year within Germany or from an EU country
Up to 30 days paid vacation each year to unwind and recharge
City mobility with a subsidized public transportation ticket and our eco-friendly bike leasing program #ridinghome


Your second home


International campus in the heart of Berlin featuring drive-in bicycle garage, outdoor space and lounge areas, fitness facilities, parent and multi-faith rooms close to the Berlin S-Bahn train station Landsberger Allee
Company benefits including a pension scheme, 30 % off the home24 assortment, and employee discounts at partner organizations
You want to learn more about our home24 company culture and what it is like to be a homie? Follow @teamhome24 on Instagram


If you want to become part of a thrilling success story – we are looking forward to your application. Please send us your application together with your salary expectations and the earliest possible starting date via our career site.

Your home is surely filled with furniture of varying materials and styles. The same is true for home24 as an employer. We celebrate the diversity of the people who work with us. Be who you are and feel welcome to #makeyourselfathome24

You can find all job offers on our career site !

home24 SE | Otto-Ostrowski-Straße 3 | 10249 Berlin | Contact: Nadine Scharf

We are home24, the leading home & living e-commerce platform in continental Europe. We are currently active in seven countries. Over 1,000 Homies work together towards a common goal: to create happy homes for everyone and every budget.

How can we reach this goal? With an assortment of over 250,000 pieces of furniture and accessories from more than 1,000 suppliers, we offer the perfect addition to any living space combined with the comfort of being able to shop from anywhere.

What makes us proud to be Homies? We are pioneers in our market who seamlessly combine technology and creativity. We utilize data to provide personalized recommendations, tips and inspiration for every home.

At home24, we strive to deliver a superior assortment of products and the best service. To do this, we need fresh ideas, 100% motivation, and dedication to our company mission. We always have a space at our table for service-minded individuals who are passionate about digitization and modern living.

Title: Data Engineer (m/w/d)
Description:
Berlin
Vollzeit
sofort
IT
Berufserfahrene


Als Berliner Traditionsunternehmen gehört die Iden Gruppe zu den führenden Handelshäusern für Schreib- und Spielwaren sowie Festbedarf. Die Geschäftsfelder sind vielfältig und zukunftsorientiert. Mit seinen Kernkompetenzen agiert das Handelshaus als Kooperationspartner des stationären Handels, Systemdienstleister der Filialisten und zuverlässiger Dienstleister der führenden E-Commerce-Plattformen. Die Grundlagen des Erfolges liegen in der kontinuierlichen Entwicklung des Unternehmens auf dem Fundament seiner Werte Tradition, Unabhängigkeit und Dynamik. Mit ihren vier leistungsstarken Standorten bietet die Iden Gruppe eine sichere Basis für die vielfältigen Transformationsprozesse der Zukunft.

Ab sofort suchen wir Sie in Vollzeit als Data Engineer (m/w/d) für unsere Zentrale am Berliner Südkreuz.

Was Sie Erwartet


Entwicklung, Betreuung und Optimierung von ETL Prozessen
Aufbau und Weiterentwicklung unseres Datalakes sowie der zugrunde liegenden Datenarchitektur
Anbindung und Integration verschiedener Datenquellen (z. B. ERP-, CRM-, Shop-, Logistik- oder Dritt-Systeme)
Erstellung und Optimierung komplexer SQL-Abfragen, Views und Datenmodelle
Sicherstellung von Datenqualität, Datenkonsistenz und Performance
Bereitstellung strukturierter Datenmodelle für nachgelagerte Reporting- und BI-Lösungen, insbesondere Power BI
Zusammenarbeit mit Fachbereichen zur Analyse von Anforderungen und Umsetzung datengetriebener Lösungen
Dokumentation von Datenflüssen, Prozessen und Strukturen


Was Sie Mitbringen Sollten


Abgeschlossenes Studium im Bereich Informatik, Wirtschaftsinformatik, Data Science oder vergleichbare Qualifikation
Erfahrung im Bereich Data Engineering, Datenbanken und Business Intelligence
Sehr gute Kenntnisse in SQL und relationalen Datenbanken
Erfahrung mit Datalake- oder Data-Warehouse-Strukturen
Verständnis für Datenmodellierung und Datenintegration
Idealerweise Erfahrung mit Business Central und Power BI
Verständnis für betriebswirtschaftliche Prozesse
Strukturierte, analytische und lösungsorientierte Arbeitsweise, sowie ausgeprägte Teamfähigkeit
Deutschkenntnisse auf Niveau C1


Was Wir Bieten

Neben einem sicheren Arbeitsplatz in einem dynamischen, zukunftsorientierten Unternehmen mit Tradition bieten wir Ihnen:

Präsenztätigkeit in Gleitzeit mit Kernzeit

30 Tage Urlaub pro Jahr

Steuerfreie Benefit-Karte bis zu 50 € mtl.

Jubiläumsgratifikationen

Urlaubsgeld

Exklusive Mitarbeiterrabatte in unseren Abholmärkten

Bezuschusstes Deutschlandticket für 40 €

Bezuschusste Fitnessstudiomitgliedschaft bei FitX

Gute Verkehrsanbindung (5 Gehminuten vom Südkreuz)

Kostenfreie Parkplätze

Haben wir Ihr Interesse geweckt?

Dann freuen wir uns auf Ihre aussagekräftigen Bewerbungsunterlagen unter Angabe Ihrer Gehaltsvorstellung sowie des frühestmöglichen Eintrittsdatums.

Alina Langhammer Personalreferentin - Iden Gruppe

Für Rückfragen: karriere@iden.de

Als Berliner Traditionsunternehmen gehört die Iden Gruppe zu den führenden Handelshäusern für Schreib- und Spielwaren sowie Festbedarf. Die Geschäftsfelder sind vielfältig und zukunftsorientiert. Mit seinen Kernkompetenzen agiert das Handelshaus als Kooperationspartner des stationären Handels, Systemdienstleister der Filialisten und zuverlässiger Dienstleister der führenden E-Commerce-Plattformen. Die Grundlagen des Erfolges liegen in der kontinuierlichen Entwicklung des Unternehmens auf dem Fundament seiner Werte Tradition, Unabhängigkeit und Dynamik. Mit ihren vier leistungsstarken Standorten bietet die Iden Gruppe eine sichere Basis für die vielfältigen Transformationsprozesse der Zukunft.

Ab sofort suchen wir Sie in Vollzeit als Data Engineer (m/w/d) für unsere Zentrale am Berliner Südkreuz.

Was Sie Erwartet

  • Entwicklung, Betreuung und Optimierung von ETL Prozessen
  • Aufbau und Weiterentwicklung unseres Datalakes sowie der zugrunde liegenden Datenarchitektur
  • Anbindung und Integration verschiedener Datenquellen (z. B. ERP-, CRM-, Shop-, Logistik- oder Dritt-Systeme)
  • Erstellung und Optimierung komplexer SQL-Abfragen, Views und Datenmodelle
  • Sicherstellung von Datenqualität, Datenkonsistenz und Performance
  • Bereitstellung strukturierter Datenmodelle für nachgelagerte Reporting- und BI-Lösungen, insbesondere Power BI
  • Zusammenarbeit mit Fachbereichen zur Analyse von Anforderungen und Umsetzung datengetriebener Lösungen
  • Dokumentation von Datenflüssen, Prozessen und Strukturen

Was Sie Mitbringen Sollten

  • Abgeschlossenes Studium im Bereich Informatik, Wirtschaftsinformatik, Data Science oder vergleichbare Qualifikation
  • Erfahrung im Bereich Data Engineering, Datenbanken und Business Intelligence
  • Sehr gute Kenntnisse in SQL und relationalen Datenbanken
  • Erfahrung mit Datalake- oder Data-Warehouse-Strukturen
  • Verständnis für Datenmodellierung und Datenintegration
  • Idealerweise Erfahrung mit Business Central und Power BI
  • Verständnis für betriebswirtschaftliche Prozesse
  • Strukturierte, analytische und lösungsorientierte Arbeitsweise, sowie ausgeprägte Teamfähigkeit
  • Deutschkenntnisse auf Niveau C1

Was Wir Bieten

Neben einem sicheren Arbeitsplatz in einem dynamischen, zukunftsorientierten Unternehmen mit Tradition bieten wir Ihnen:

Präsenztätigkeit in Gleitzeit mit Kernzeit

30 Tage Urlaub pro Jahr

Steuerfreie Benefit-Karte bis zu 50 € mtl.

Jubiläumsgratifikationen

Urlaubsgeld

Exklusive Mitarbeiterrabatte in unseren Abholmärkten

Bezuschusstes Deutschlandticket für 40 €

Bezuschusste Fitnessstudiomitgliedschaft bei FitX

Gute Verkehrsanbindung (5 Gehminuten vom Südkreuz)

Kostenfreie Parkplätze

Haben wir Ihr Interesse geweckt?

Dann freuen wir uns auf Ihre aussagekräftigen Bewerbungsunterlagen unter Angabe Ihrer Gehaltsvorstellung sowie des frühestmöglichen Eintrittsdatums.

Title: Junior Data Engineer (m/f/d) - Martech
Description:
We are home24, the leading home & living e-commerce platform in continental Europe. We are currently active in seven countries. Over 1,000 Homies work together towards a common goal: to create happy homes for everyone and every budget.

How can we reach this goal? With an assortment of over 250,000 pieces of furniture and accessories from more than 1,000 suppliers, we offer the perfect addition to any living space combined with the comfort of being able to shop from anywhere.

What makes us proud to be Homies? We are pioneers in our market who seamlessly combine technology and creativity. We utilize data to provide personalized recommendations, tips and inspiration for every home.

At home24, we strive to deliver a superior assortment of products and the best service. To do this, we need fresh ideas, 100% motivation, and dedication to our company mission. We always have a space at our table for service-minded individuals who are passionate about digitization and modern living.

Our Marketing Technology (“Martech”) team is creating data-driven solutions that revolutionize and facilitate how Marketing & CRM operate enabling real-time personalization, campaign performance optimization, and marketing attribution. This involves data feeds, business insights, customer analytics, and machine learning models, along with other data products pushing marketing performance and automation.

For our headquarter in Berlin Prenzlauer-Berg we are looking to have you on-board as our new

Junior Data Engineer (m/f/d) - Martech

Your Responsibilities


You contribute to the design, implementation, and maintenance of batch and streaming data pipelines
You work with the product manager and other data engineers to enhance our AWS-based data platform adopting functional programming best practices and utilizing Apache Spark
You support data scientists and marketing stakeholders in operationalizing and scaling machine learning models that leverage our data
You build analytical workflows (e.g. customized user experiences, personalisation, automated A/B testing) using SQL, Scala, and Python
You contribute to data quality validation, and monitoring solutions to ensure reliability and trust in our data products
You take part in implementing POCs that bring data and business users closer


Your Profile

If you're interested in the role, but don't meet every requirement, we still encourage you to apply. We value a willingness to learn and grow with us. Tell us why you’re the right person in your application.


You hold a university degree in Computer Engineering, Computer Science, or a similar field
You have solid programming skills in Python and a JVM-based language like Scala
You are fluent in SQL, and familiarity with Apache Spark is a plus
You are familiar with concepts of modern data lake architecture and the fundamentals of Data Engineering
You have experience with AWS or any other cloud provider (preferred)
You are fluent in English


What We Offer You

Your impact


You will work closely with experienced data engineers and receive mentorship while gradually taking ownership of components within our data platform and Martech ecosystem
You will contribute to a modern technology stack and help drive openness to innovations


Your path


Hybrid working model: you have the flexibility to work from home on Wednesdays and Fridays in our standard working mode. We are in the office together Mondays, Tuesdays and Thursdays
The possibility to work up to two months per year within Germany or from an EU country
Up to 30 days paid vacation each year to unwind and recharge
City mobility with a subsidized public transportation ticket and our eco-friendly bike leasing program #ridinghome


Your second home


International campus in the heart of Berlin featuring drive-in bicycle garage, outdoor space and lounge areas, fitness facilities, parent and multi-faith rooms close to the Berlin S-Bahn train station Landsberger Allee
Company benefits including a pension scheme, 30 % off the home24 assortment, and employee discounts at partner organizations
You want to learn more about our home24 company culture and what it is like to be a homie? Follow @teamhome24 on Instagram


If you want to become part of a thrilling success story – we are looking forward to your application. Please send us your application together with your salary expectations and the earliest possible starting date via our career site.

Your home is surely filled with furniture of varying materials and styles. The same is true for home24 as an employer. We celebrate the diversity of the people who work with us. Be who you are and feel welcome to #makeyourselfathome24

You can find all job offers on our career site !

home24 SE | Otto-Ostrowski-Straße 3 | 10249 Berlin | Contact: Nadine Scharf

Title: Data Analyst (m/w/d)
Description:
Ihre guten Ideen finden endlich Gehör! Wir sind der Motor für eine schnelle und pragmatische Umsetzung zum Wohle unserer Patient:innen.

Zur Verstärkung unseres Teams Business Intelligence der Helios Kliniken suchen wir bundesweit zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt Sie als

Data Analyst (m/w/d)

in Vollzeit mit Dienstsitz in Berlin und der Möglichkeit zum mobilen arbeiten.

Das erwartet Sie



Spannende und abwechslungsreiche Aufgaben in der Analyse, Aufbereitung und Visualisierung komplexer Daten aus dem Gesundheitswesen
Entwicklung und Pflege von Datenmodellen, Machine-Learning-Modellen sowie automatisierten Auswertungen zur Unterstützung datenbasierter Entscheidungen
Visualisierung von Daten, um Trends, Muster und Optimierungspotenziale zu identifizieren
Enge Zusammenarbeit mit Entscheidungsträgern zur Erarbeitung datenbasierter Handlungsempfehlungen und strategischer Empfehlungen
Mitwirkung bei der Definition und Weiterentwicklung von KPIs und Reporting-Standards



Das bringen Sie mit



Abgeschlossenes Studium im Bereich Wirtschaftsinformatik, Informatik, Statistik, Mathematik, BWL oder eine vergleichbare Qualifikation
Sehr gute Kenntnisse in Power BI, insbesondere im Umgang mit DAX und Power Query zur Erstellung komplexer Auswertungen und Dashboards
Fundierte Kenntnisse in Python und SQL für Datenanalysen, Automatisierungen und Datenmodellierung (z.B. mit Pandas, NumPy, SQL Server, dbt, etc.)
Stark ausgeprägte analytisch-konzeptionelle Fähigkeiten zur strukturierten Analyse großer Datenmengen und zur Ableitung relevanter Erkenntnisse sowie ein Grundverständnis betriebswirtschaftlicher Zusammenhänge, idealerweise im klinischen Kontext
Sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift (mind. C1) sind Voraussetzung, genau wie eine hohe Eigeninitiative, Motivation und ein hohes Maß an Qualitätsbewusstsein
Kenntnisse im Bereich Medizincontrolling, Gesundheitswesen oder Krankenhaus-Controlling sind von Vorteil
Gesetzlich notwendiger Nachweis über Immunisierung gegen Masern



Freuen Sie sich auf



Ein kollegiales, offenes und hilfsbereites Team, das gemeinsam an innovativen Lösungen arbeitet
Ein modernes Arbeitskonzept mit der Möglichkeit zum mobilen Arbeiten und flexiblen Arbeitszeiten für eine gute Vereinbarkeit von Beruf und Privatleben
Ein hoher Grad an Selbstbestimmung und Eigenverantwortung mit Raum für eigene Ideen und Gestaltungsmöglichkeiten
Ein modernes, datengetriebenes Arbeitsumfeld mit kurzen Entscheidungswegen und klaren Hierarchien
Die Helios PlusCard: Eine durch Helios finanzierte Zusatzversicherung mit u. a. Wahlarztbehandlung bzw. Wahlärztinbehandlung im Falle eines Krankenhausaufenthalts in einer unserer Kliniken – bei Interesse für die ganze Familie
Rabatte für über 480 Topmarken und Präventionsprogramme, z.B. Vergünstigungen bei Sportstudios
Wir bieten Ihnen die Chance, umweltfreundlich zur Arbeit zu kommen – mit einem Arbeitgeberzuschuss zum Deutschlandticket



Jetzt liegt es nur noch an Ihnen!

Kontakt

Helios ist der führende Klinikträger in Europa. Die kollegiale und fachübergreifende Zusammenarbeit und die schnelle Umsetzung von Innovationen garantieren unseren Patient:innen eine bestmögliche Versorgung. Title: Data Engineer (Mid-level) (m/w/d)
Description:
Company Description Haensel AMS—Advanced Mathematical Solutions—is a technology and data analytics boutique specializing in innovative machine learning and big data solutions. The company leverages advanced mathematical methods to design tailored data-driven strategies that address the specific needs of each client. Working closely with partners across industries, Haensel AMS turns complex data into actionable insights and measurable value. Team members collaborate in an environment that combines technical excellence, analytical rigor, and practical business impact. This setting offers professionals the opportunity to work on challenging projects with modern technologies and methods.




Role Description As a Mid-level Data Engineer at Haensel AMS, you will design, build, and maintain data pipelines that support advanced analytics and machine learning use cases. In close collaboration with data scientists and analysts, you will develop scalable ETL processes, integrate data from multiple sources, and ensure data is clean, reliable, and well-structured. You will contribute to data modeling and data warehouse design, optimize performance, and monitor data quality in production environments. Your work will include documenting data flows, automating workflows, and supporting stakeholders with data access and tooling. This is a full-time, on-site role based in Berlin, Germany.




Qualifications

Strong data engineering skills, including experience with Data Engineering concepts and building robust data pipelines.
Proficiency in Data Modeling and designing scalable data architectures to support analytics and reporting needs.
Hands-on experience with Extract Transform Load (ETL) processes and tools to integrate and transform data from diverse sources.
Knowledge of Data Warehousing concepts and implementation, including schema design and performance optimization.
Ability to apply Data Analytics techniques to validate, profile, and interpret data for business and technical stakeholders.
Proficiency in at least one programming language commonly used in data engineering (e.g., Python, Scala, or Java) and SQL.
Experience with modern data platforms or cloud ecosystems (e.g., AWS, GCP, or Azure) and related services is beneficial.
Familiarity with workflow orchestration, version control, and CI/CD practices for data solutions is an advantage.
Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience.
Strong problem-solving skills, attention to detail, and the ability to work collaboratively in a cross-functional, inclusive team.

  • Hands-on experience with Extract Transform Load (ETL) processes and tools to integrate and transform data from diverse sources.

  • Ability to apply Data Analytics techniques to validate, profile, and interpret data for business and technical stakeholders.
  • Proficiency in at least one programming language commonly used in data engineering (e.g., Python, Scala, or Java) and SQL.
  • Experience with modern data platforms or cloud ecosystems (e.g., AWS, GCP, or Azure) and related services is beneficial.
  • Familiarity with workflow orchestration, version control, and CI/CD practices for data solutions is an advantage.
  • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience.
  • Strong problem-solving skills, attention to detail, and the ability to work collaboratively in a cross-functional, inclusive team.

Title: Associate Partner - AI & Data - Life Sciences
Description:
Title: Associate Partner

Type: Permanent contract

About Us

At Visium, we enable enterprise executives in defining their AI & Data strategy, execute large scale transformations and implement AI across operations, ensuring their organization becomes future-proof.

With expertise in strategy, architecture, cloud engineering, analytics, artificial intelligence and machine learning, we empower our clients to unleash and scale the power of their data.

We're on a mission to pioneer a bright future and build future-proof and ethical organizations . Join the curious, the ambitious, the doers, the good-hearted, the ones who build a world we're all in awe of - our Visumees.

Ready to become one?

Role

At Visium, you'll have the opportunity to take on an entrepreneurial role, contributing to the organization's growth and finding innovative ways to create meaningful impact for clients. Your mission will involve advising C-Level executives on their AI & Data strategies and guiding large-scale digital transformations. You'll act as a trusted advisor and a key source of strategic insight, overseeing and nurturing long-term client relationships while ensuring the highest quality of delivery.

As an Associate Partner you will be responsible for:


Creating new business opportunities and expanding existing ones. Rely on your extensive network and collaborate with our business development team to create new business opportunities in LifeScience
Engage with our client's C-level stakeholders to understand their Data and AI related challenges, developing solutions and support them in implementing and scaling them in a sustainable way
Lead and collaborate effectively within large cross-functional teams to review and analyze client requirements or challenges and provide leadership and direction to elevate our delivery capabilities.
Lead engagement teams in developing logical and insightful analyses; innovative & effective recommendations and high-quality deliverables


Depending on your previous experiences and appetite to grow, you will play an active role in practice development.

Requirements

What we are looking for


Director, Associate Partner/Partner level (or equivalent title) experience at a consulting firm
Data Science background and specialization across LifeScience & Healthcare are required.
Proven track record of developing new client relationships and generating business development opportunities that leads to successful client engagements
Demonstrated ability with the management of a portfolio of healthcare clients generating 7 figures in revenues in a consulting-like industry
Experience leading cross-functional teams and in-depth knowledge/defined involvement in helping frame, design and implement and scale solutions for challenging business situations
Exceptional intellectual and analytical curiosity, a creative visionary who can drive innovation
Ability to communicate complex ideas effectively - both verbally and in writing - in English. French and/or German are considered a plus.


Benefits

Benefits

What we offer


A competitive compensation package
A yearly education budget to steep your learning curve
A yearly sport budget because a fit body leads to a fit mind
A flexible working culture because your work-life balance matters to us
A position that enables you to have an impact on 1'000s of people, and the whole company's growth
An international, knowledgeable, and passionate team with a strong collaborative mindset


Check our LinkedIn and website to learn more about us & don't hesitate to contact us if you have any questions.

Title: IT - Helpdesk
Description:
Hallo! 👋 Du bist IT-affin, liebst es, Probleme direkt beim Anwender zu lösen und suchst einen Job mit echter Langzeitperspektive? Dann bist Du bei uns genau richtig!




Wir suchen einen engagierten IT-Supporter (all genders) für den direkten Einsatz bei unserem Kunden in der Region Berlin/Brandenburg.




Das Besondere an dieser Stelle: Wir bieten Dir ein extrem stabiles Projekt mit einer festen Laufzeit bis Februar 2026 und einer anschließenden Verlängerungsoption für 3 weitere Jahre, die als sehr sicher gilt! 🤝 Hier kannst Du wirklich ankommen und langfristig planen.


Aufgaben


Als zentraler Ansprechpartner (SPOC) bist Du das Gesicht der IT direkt beim Kunden.





👨‍💻 Ansprechpartner & Berater: Du bist die erste Anlaufstelle für alle IT-Themen und weist Anwender in neue Systeme, Laptops oder Mobilgeräte ein.


🖥️ Hardware-Management: Du kümmerst Dich um die Einrichtung, Konfiguration, Installation und den Austausch von IT-Arbeitsplätzen (Desktops, Notebooks, Drucker, Peripherie) sowie der Medientechnik in Konferenzräumen.


🔍 Troubleshooting: Als "Helping Hand" analysierst und behebst Du Hardware- und Softwarestörungen (MS Windows, Netzwerk etc.) direkt vor Ort.


📱 Mobile Devices: Du betreust mobile Geräte und übernimmst deren Einrichtung über MDM-Systeme (z.B. Intune).


📦 Rollouts & Logistik: Du unterstützt tatkräftig bei IT-Rollouts, IT-Umzügen und übernimmst die Lagerverwaltung (Ausgabe, Rücknahme) sowie die Pflege des Asset-Managements.


🎫 Ticket-Profi: Du bearbeitest, dokumentierst und verfolgst alle Anfragen strukturiert im Ticketsystem.



Qualifikation


Das bringst Du mit (Qualifikationen)




🎓 Deine Basis: Eine abgeschlossene Ausbildung im IT-Bereich (z.B. Fachinformatiker/in Systemintegration, IT-Systemkaufmann/-frau) ODER Du bist ein passionierter Quereinsteiger (all genders) mit nachweisbarer Berufserfahrung im IT-Support.





💡 Deine Erfahrung: Du konntest bereits wertvolle Erfahrung im direkten IT-User-Support (Vor-Ort-Service) sammeln.


🔧 Dein Know-how: Du hast gute Kenntnisse im Umgang mit Microsoft-Produkten (Active Directory, ENTRA ID, Berechtigungsvergabe) und bist routiniert im Umgang mit Ticketsystemen.


💻 Deine Skills: Troubleshooting von Desktops, Notebooks, mobilen Endgeräten und Medientechnik ist für Dich kein Problem.


⭐ Dein Plus: Du hast idealerweise bereits Erfahrung im Bereich Mobile Device Management (Intune)

Title: Data Engineer (m/w/d)
Description:
Berlin
Vollzeit
sofort
IT
Berufserfahrene


Als Berliner Traditionsunternehmen gehört die Iden Gruppe zu den führenden Handelshäusern für Schreib- und Spielwaren sowie Festbedarf. Die Geschäftsfelder sind vielfältig und zukunftsorientiert. Mit seinen Kernkompetenzen agiert das Handelshaus als Kooperationspartner des stationären Handels, Systemdienstleister der Filialisten und zuverlässiger Dienstleister der führenden E-Commerce-Plattformen. Die Grundlagen des Erfolges liegen in der kontinuierlichen Entwicklung des Unternehmens auf dem Fundament seiner Werte Tradition, Unabhängigkeit und Dynamik. Mit ihren vier leistungsstarken Standorten bietet die Iden Gruppe eine sichere Basis für die vielfältigen Transformationsprozesse der Zukunft.

Ab sofort suchen wir Sie in Vollzeit als Data Engineer (m/w/d) für unsere Zentrale am Berliner Südkreuz.

Was Sie Erwartet


Entwicklung, Betreuung und Optimierung von ETL Prozessen
Aufbau und Weiterentwicklung unseres Datalakes sowie der zugrunde liegenden Datenarchitektur
Anbindung und Integration verschiedener Datenquellen (z. B. ERP-, CRM-, Shop-, Logistik- oder Dritt-Systeme)
Erstellung und Optimierung komplexer SQL-Abfragen, Views und Datenmodelle
Sicherstellung von Datenqualität, Datenkonsistenz und Performance
Bereitstellung strukturierter Datenmodelle für nachgelagerte Reporting- und BI-Lösungen, insbesondere Power BI
Zusammenarbeit mit Fachbereichen zur Analyse von Anforderungen und Umsetzung datengetriebener Lösungen
Dokumentation von Datenflüssen, Prozessen und Strukturen


Was Sie Mitbringen Sollten


Abgeschlossenes Studium im Bereich Informatik, Wirtschaftsinformatik, Data Science oder vergleichbare Qualifikation
Erfahrung im Bereich Data Engineering, Datenbanken und Business Intelligence
Sehr gute Kenntnisse in SQL und relationalen Datenbanken
Erfahrung mit Datalake- oder Data-Warehouse-Strukturen
Verständnis für Datenmodellierung und Datenintegration
Idealerweise Erfahrung mit Business Central und Power BI
Verständnis für betriebswirtschaftliche Prozesse
Strukturierte, analytische und lösungsorientierte Arbeitsweise, sowie ausgeprägte Teamfähigkeit
Deutschkenntnisse auf Niveau C1


Was Wir Bieten

Neben einem sicheren Arbeitsplatz in einem dynamischen, zukunftsorientierten Unternehmen mit Tradition bieten wir Ihnen:

Präsenztätigkeit in Gleitzeit mit Kernzeit

30 Tage Urlaub pro Jahr

Steuerfreie Benefit-Karte bis zu 50 € mtl.

Jubiläumsgratifikationen

Urlaubsgeld

Exklusive Mitarbeiterrabatte in unseren Abholmärkten

Bezuschusstes Deutschlandticket für 40 €

Bezuschusste Fitnessstudiomitgliedschaft bei FitX

Gute Verkehrsanbindung (5 Gehminuten vom Südkreuz)

Kostenfreie Parkplätze

Haben wir Ihr Interesse geweckt?

Dann freuen wir uns auf Ihre aussagekräftigen Bewerbungsunterlagen unter Angabe Ihrer Gehaltsvorstellung sowie des frühestmöglichen Eintrittsdatums.

Alina Langhammer Personalreferentin - Iden Gruppe

Für Rückfragen: karriere@iden.de

Bitte beachten Sie, dass wir aus Gründen der Datenschutzkonformität keine Bewerbungen per E-Mail entgegennehmen.

Bewerben

Title: Finance Intern (m/w/d)
Description:
About The Role

Als Finance Intern bei LANCH wirst du direkt mit unserem Finance-Team zusammenarbeiten und tiefe Einblicke in die finanzielle Steuerung eines schnell wachsenden Startups erhalten. Dies ist eine ideale Position für Masterstudenten oder Absolventen mit Interesse an Finance, die eine steile Lernkurve suchen und praktische Erfahrung in einem dynamischen Umfeld sammeln möchten.

Deine Aufgaben:


Verantwortlich für den Rechnungseingang und Überprüfung der Ausgaben
Mitwirkung bei der Erstellung von Finanzberichten und -präsentationen
Analyse von Geschäftskennzahlen und Identifikation von Optimierungspotentialen
Mitarbeit bei Projekten zur Prozessverbesserung im Finanzbereich
Unterstützung bei der Vorbereitung von Investor Relations Materialien


About You

Dein Profil:


Student im Master-Studium oder kürzlich abgeschlossener Master in Wirtschaftswissenschaften, Finance, Controlling oder einem verwandten Fachgebiet
Starkes Interesse an Finance und der Startup-Welt
Analytisches Denkvermögen und Zahlenaffinität
Gute Kenntnisse in Excel und idealerweise erste Erfahrungen mit SQL
Selbstständige, proaktive Arbeitsweise und Teamgeist
Fließende Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse


Was wir bieten:


Steile Lernkurve und vielseitige Einblicke in die Finanzwelt eines wachstumsstarken Startups
Direkte Zusammenarbeit mit erfahrenen Finance-Experten und dem Management-Team
Möglichkeit, eigene Ideen einzubringen und umzusetzen
Flexible Arbeitszeiten und moderne Arbeitsumgebung im Herzen Berlins
Attraktive Vergütung und kostenlose Verpflegung mit unseren Produkten


Über uns

LANCH ist das wahrscheinlich am schnellsten wachsende Consumer‑Startup in Deutschland, gegründet von Serial Entrepreneurs und unterstützt von den besten Investoren Europas. Unsere Vision ist es, schon heute die Lebensmittelmarken von morgen zu schaffen, und zwar in den Bereichen Delivery, Store und CPG. Wir betreiben mehrere Ghost Kitchen Brands, expandieren derzeit stark auch international und treiben mit Loco Chicken gerade eine große Store‑Expansion voran. Unterstützt werden wir von HV Capital, Felix Capital und einer Reihe von Superstar‑Investoren wie den Machern von FlixBus, Gorillas, Flaschenpost, Glovo, Foodspring, Foodora, HelloFresh, Wolt, sowie den bekanntesten Stars unserer Zeit wie Knossi, Luciano, Mario Götze, André Schürrle und Trymacs.

5 Reasons to join LANCH:


Huge growth & even bigger growth ahead LANCH is the fastest growing consumer company in DACH ever and it’s still the very beginning. A journey like this brings massive career growth trajectory for you.
Work with experiences founders & C-Level Serial founders who have exited companies to Netflix, DeliveryHero, Bird and experienced C-Level.
Trusted by the best investors out there Raised oversubscribed pre-seed to Series A rounds in less than a year in the toughest venture market. Backed by Europe’s best investors such as HV Capital, Felix Capital, founders of FlixBus, Flaschenpost, Foodora, Foodspring, Glovo, Wolt or superstars like Mario Götze, Knossi, Luciano, André Schürrle & more.
Early enough to have huge impact but late enough to know the business works & scales fast Timing is everything: joining LANCH now is the perfect timing. We already know the business works and we have already fulfilled over 2.4 million orders. However, we are still small and you will have massive impact and career growth ahead.
Culture, fun, success - join a great team
We're growing incredibly fast - be prepared to learn a lot and take on responsibility.
An exceptionally good and experienced team. Here you can build your career.
Fair compensation and shares in LANCH (for permanent employees).
70+ team members after just 3 years and flat hierarchies.
Team offsites every 16 weeks to connect face-to-face and strengthen our relationships.
Full autonomy at every level.
