Title: Research Analyst
HTML:
Research Analyst


Do you want to be part of a bold and ambitious team that helps organizations make better decisions with good data?


About The Role

Data Sciences is looking for a research analyst to assist in the development and execution of market research studies, online experiments and other research projects. This is an entry-level position for someone who has experience in R. The right fit is someone who is a highly motivated self-learner looking to grow and take on a challenging role at a fast-growing company. 


Key Responsibilities

To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform the following satisfactorily:

  • Analyzing and visualizing data to identify meaningful patterns and communicate findings through clear, accurate, and visually polished charts, tables, and other deliverables.
  • Online survey programming and fielding, including survey setup, testing, quotas, vendor coordination, quality checks, and troubleshooting.
  • Establishing research strategies and writing survey questionnaires that align with client objectives and support a clear analytical story, with attention to survey length and respondent experience.
  • Drafting and presenting research reports and memos that synthesize findings into clear, accurate, and actionable insights.
  • Identify opportunities to streamline workflows, strengthen QA, and solve problems through automation, AI tools, and API integrations.
  • Carry out other special projects as required.


Candidate Qualifications

Education/Experience:

  • Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field with experience in quantitative methods.
  • R programming capabilities: 2+ years intermediate experience, with practical experience using the tidyverse (especially dplyr and purrr), writing reusable and modular code, and developing or maintaining R packages.
  • Knowledge of version control systems (e.g. git).
  • Excellent analytical, communication and writing skills.
  • Ability to design clear, compelling, and visually polished data visualizations for technical and non-technical audiences.
  • Ability to work with code and follow coding best practices.
  • Understanding of statistical principles, particularly with respect to survey design and interpretation. 
  • Exceptionally strong organizational skills: detail-oriented, ability to manage several tasks at once and stick to deadlines, good at identifying priorities and managing expectations.
  • Flexibility: you don’t get flustered when new challenges are thrown your way.
  • Ability to work in a fast-paced environment and pivot/react quickly when needed.


Assets:

  • Experience in market and/or public opinion research.
  • Knowledge of other programming languages and tools (e.g., SQL, Python, JavaScript, Google Apps Script), additional analytics related software development experience is a plus.
  • Experience programming, launching, and monitoring surveys programmed in Qualtrics and/or similar survey platforms.
  • Familiarity with AWS-based data workflows, including storing, querying, transforming, and automating data using tools such as S3, Redshift, DynamoDB, and Lambda.
  • Experience safely leveraging LLMs into workflows, especially code assist tools (e.g., Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and willingness to integrate new technologies into your work.
  • Experience with web development tools like React, Next.js, and modern CSS. 
  • Experience related to politics or campaigning.
  • Experience with public speaking and presenting research to diverse audiences.
  • Bilingual in English and French.
  • We work globally! Knowledge of other languages is appreciated.


What We Offer

  • 100% employer-paid competitive health package, including Medical & Dental benefits.
  • Perkopolis: a free program with discounts and savings on entertainment, hotels, travel, shopping and health and wellness products. 
  • Hybrid work, both remote and in the office.
  • Our Head Office in Montreal always has a supply of snacks and coffee.
  • Many internal traditions to create an inclusive and fun environment. 
  • A MacBook computer on your first day. 
  • 3 weeks of vacation.
  • 5 personal days to use at your discretion.
  • A fast-paced work environment where every day is different.
  • The opportunity for professional development through funded courses, conferences and certifications.
  • A startup office space and the opportunity to join a growing, multidisciplinary team.


Knowledge of English is required for this position, as the incumbent will be required to communicate daily, both orally and in writing, with a predominantly English-speaking clientele outside of Quebec.



Analyste de recherche


Voulez-vous faire partie d'une équipe audacieuse et ambitieuse qui aide les organisations à prendre de meilleures décisions grâce à de bonnes données ?


Description du poste

Data Sciences recherche une ou un analyste de recherche pour participer au développement et à l’exécution d’études de marché, d’expériences en ligne et d’autres projets de recherche. Il s’agit d’un poste junior pour une personne qui a de l’expérience à programmer avec R. La personne idéale est autodidacte et très motivée. Elle cherche à se développer et à assumer un rôle stimulant au sein d’une entreprise en plein essor.


﻿Responsabilités

Pour accomplir ce travail avec succès, une personne doit être en mesure d'accomplir les tâches suivantes de manière satisfaisante :


  • Analyser et visualiser les données afin d’en dégager des tendances significatives et de communiquer les résultats au moyen de graphiques, de tableaux et d’autres livrables clairs, exacts et soignés sur le plan visuel;
  • Programmation et déploiement de sondages en ligne, incluant la configuration, les tests, les quotas, la coordination avec les fournisseurs, les contrôles qualité et le dépannage;
  • Établir des stratégies de recherche et rédiger des questionnaires de sondage qui s’harmonisent avec les objectifs des clients et soutiennent une histoire analytique claire, en portant attention à la longueur du sondage et à l’expérience du répondant;
  • Rédiger et présenter des rapports de recherche et des mémos qui synthétisent les résultats en analyses claires, exactes et exploitables;
  • Repérer des occasions de rationaliser les processus, de renforcer le contrôle qualité et de résoudre des problèmes au moyen de l’automatisation, des outils d’IA et des intégrations d’API;
  • Réaliser d'autres projets spéciaux en fonction des besoins.


Exigences

Formation et expérience:

  • Baccalauréat dans un domaine pertinent avec expérience en méthodes quantitatives;
  • Capacités de programmation R : 2 ans ou plus d’expérience de niveau intermédiaire, avec une expérience pratique du tidyverse (en particulier dplyr et purrr), de l’écriture de code réutilisable et modulaire, ainsi que du développement ou de la maintenance de packages R;
  • Connaissance des systèmes de contrôle de version (par exemple, git);
  • Excellentes capacités d'analyse, de communication et de rédaction;
  • Capacité à concevoir des visualisations de données claires, convaincantes et soignées sur le plan visuel, destinées à des publics techniques comme non techniques;
  • Capacité à travailler avec du code et à suivre les meilleures pratiques de codage;
  • Compréhension des principes statistiques, en particulier en ce qui concerne la conception et l'interprétation des enquêtes;
  • Sens de l’organisation exceptionnel — souci du détail, capacité à mener plusieurs tâches de front et à respecter les échéances, aptitude à cerner les priorités et à gérer les attentes;
  • Capacité d’adaptation : vous ne vous laissez pas déconcerter par les nouveaux défis qui se présentent à vous;
  • Capacité à travailler dans un environnement au rythme rapide et à pivoter ou réagir rapidement au besoin.


Atouts: 

  • Expérience en matière d'études de marché et/ou d'opinion publique;
  • Connaissance d’autres langages et outils de programmation (par exemple SQL, Python, JavaScript, Google Apps Script); toute expérience supplémentaire en développement logiciel lié à l’analytique est un atout;
  • Expérience de programmation, de lancement et de suivi de sondages programmés dans Qualtrics ou des plateformes de sondage similaires;
  • Familiarité avec les flux de données basés sur AWS, notamment le stockage, l’interrogation, la transformation et l’automatisation de données à l’aide d’outils comme S3, Redshift, DynamoDB et Lambda;
  • Expérience d’intégration sécuritaire de grands modèles de langage (LLM) dans des flux de travail, en particulier les outils d’assistance au code (par exemple Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), et volonté d’intégrer de nouvelles technologies à votre travail;
  • Expérience avec des outils de développement web tels que React, Next.js et CSS moderne;
  • Expérience dans le domaine de la politique ou des campagnes électorales;
  • Expérience de la prise de parole en public et de la présentation d'études à des publics divers;
  • Bilingue (anglais et français); 
  • Nous travaillons à l'échelle mondiale ! La connaissance d'autres langues est appréciée.


Ce que nous offrons

  • Un régime d'assurance médicale et dentaire compétitif, payé à 100 % par l'employeur;
  • Perkopolis: Un accès gratuit à une foule de rabais et d'économies sur les divertissements, les voyages, le magasinage et les produits de bien-être;
  • Mode de travail hybride offrant la flexibilité du télétravail et de la collaboration au bureau;
  • Des collations, du café et bien plus dans notre siège social de Montréal;
  • Une tonne d'activités et de traditions pour créer un environnement inclusif et divertissant;
  • Un ordinateur MacBook fourni dès ton arrivée;
  • 3 semaines de vacances par année;
  • 5 jours personnels à utiliser à ta discrétion;
  • Un environnement de travail dynamique où aucune journée ne se ressemble;
  • Des opportunités de croissance grâce au financement de cours, de conférences et de certifications;
  • Un bureau moderne avec une ambiance ‘’startup’’;
  • La chance de rejoindre une équipe multidisciplinaire en pleine croissance.


La connaissance de la langue anglaise est exigée pour ce poste, car les fonctions de la personne titulaire nécessiteront des communications orales et écrites quotidiennes avec une clientèle majoritairement anglophone située à l'extérieur du Québec.

Title: NGS Data Analyst for Molecular Oncology
Description:
Alacris Theranostics GmbH is a leader in innovative molecular diagnostics, specialising in personalised oncology. Our cutting-edge solutions combine next-generation sequencing (NGS), advanced bioinformatics, and systems biology to transform scientific insights into enhanced cancer diagnostics and more precise treatment decisions. We collaborate closely with leading research institutions, hospitals, and pharmaceutical partners to drive meaningful and lasting improvements in patient outcomes.


Tasks


We are seeking an experienced Bioinformatician / Computational Biologist with a strong track record in next-generation sequencing (NGS) data analysis. The successful candidate will be responsible for processing and analysing NGS datasets - including RNA-Seq, whole-exome sequencing (WES), whole-genome sequencing (WGS), and single-cell sequencing - derived from tumour samples.


Requirements


The ideal candidate will demonstrate excellent programming skills in R and Python, along with proven expertise in NGS data analysis and a solid foundation in human molecular genetics. Knowledge of statistics, high-dimensional data analysis, and cancer genomics is highly desirable.




We are looking for a candidate with a genuine interest in precision oncology, a proactive approach to learning, and a commitment to developing new skills. Strong motivation, a collaborative mindset, and excellent communication skills are essential


Benefits


You will join a multidisciplinary team of bioinformaticians, molecular biologists, and software engineers dedicated to developing innovative analysis pipelines and diagnostic solutions in oncology.




We offer a rewarding and challenging role within a dynamic biotech company based in the vibrant city of Berlin. The position comes with a competitive salary and a stimulating working environment. The appointment is available immediately and will initially be for a two-year term, with the possibility of extension.




How to Apply? Interested candidates should provide a CV, publication list, details of previous research and work experience, and contact details of three referees as a single PDF file, citing reference ALA-NGSA-0426.




The closing date for applications is April 15, 2026.

Title: Staff Data Analyst, Marketing Analytics
Description:
Change the way the world travels

Join the GetYourGuide journey to connect people with unforgettable travel experiences around the world. Millions look to us for unique activities they can trust, and it’s all powered by our commitment to make every single journey extraordinary - including yours.

Ready to unlock your potential with a community of fellow explorers? Find your next role at our Berlin HQ or one of our local offices around the globe, from New York to Bangkok. Head to getyourguide.careers to take the first step.

Team mission

The Analytics department’s mission is to enable the Marketing organisation to take action based on data. We work on a variety of problems and datasets as members of cross-functional mission teams. We take ownership of the entire analytics funnel, from exploration through recommendations to impact measurement, and take pride in the insights we generate.

This specific position is within our Marketing Analytics team whose objective is to help our marketing scale our investments towards the most incremental and loyal customers. This is a very strategic position within the team as the Analyst will look at solving cross-channel problems in order to accelerate our growth. This means for example addressing the best strategy to grow our display and mid-funnel spend and understanding the behaviour of our repeat audiences.

Your mission


Develop quantitative analysis and statistical models to evaluate and steer marketing decisions
Propose and defend strategic recommendations to our top marketing leads
Setup and analyse marketing experiments to drive strategic decision making
Explore large datasets, give meaning to data and numbers, identify opportunities and issues, and present findings to stakeholders
Maintain and improve our marketing measurement tools (Attribution, experimentation framework, Customer lifetime value…)
Serve as a liaison between the marketing teams and the data engineering team
Mentor others in the skills needed for this role


Your toolkit


8+ years of relevant experience interpreting data in an analytics, data science or business intelligence role
Strong knowledge of SQL and Python for analysis (eg, pandas, scikit-learn, pyspark)
Ability to understand user behavior and meaning behind the numbers and a track record in sharing insights to business stakeholders
Prior exposure to digital and performance marketing or SEO, especially data-driven platforms such as Google Ads and Meta.
Experience with data visualization and reporting tools (e.g. Looker, Jupyter Notebooks, Databricks)
Desire to work in an international environment, with minimal direction and with highly engaged individuals
Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English


Extras that give you an edge


A track record in solving marketing and growth challenges and influencing decisions/driving business results
Experience in understanding and modelling marketing journeys
Experience with big data tools (e.g. Spark, Hive) is a plus


How We’ll Make Your Career Journey Extraordinary


Annual personal growth budget and mentorship programs for continuous learning and development
Work from anywhere in the world for 30 days per year
A hybrid working approach with three days of in office collaboration (Mon, Tue, Thur) and two days of optional at home focus time.
Opportunities to collaborate and socialize with team members through quarterly team events and yearly company-wide events
Monthly transportation and fitness budget
Discounts for you, your friends, and family on GetYourGuide activities
Language reimbursement program
Health and wellness benefits


And more…

How To Apply

Submit your CV/resume in English using the form below. For tips and insights into our hiring process and culture, check out ‘how we hire’ and ‘life at GetYourGuide’. If you have any further questions, please don’t hesitate to get in touch at jobs@getyourguide.com.

We’re an equal opportunities employer

Our commitment is that every qualified person will be evaluated according to their skills regardless of age, gender identity, ethnicity, sexual orientation, disability status, or religion. Please refrain from including your picture and age with your application.

Title: Wiss. Mitarbeiter*in (d/m/w) - 85 % Arbeitszeit
Description:
Fakultät VI - Planen Bauen Umwelt, Institut für Landschaftsarchitektur und Umweltplanung / FG Geoinformationsverarbeitung in der Umweltplanung

Wiss. Mitarbeiter*in (d/m/w) - 85 % Arbeitszeit

Über uns

Im Rahmen des internationalen Forschungsprojekts SAVE Water – GeoSpatial and Artificial Intelligence

Technologies: Innovative Approach for Water Resources Management entwickelt die Technische Universität Berlin datengetriebene Ansätze zur nachhaltigen Bewirtschaftung von Wasserressourcen unter den Bedingungen des Klimawandels. Das Projekt adressiert Herausforderungen wie Wasserknappheit, Verschmutzung und zunehmende Nutzungskonflikte durch den Einsatz von Geoinformationstechnologien, Fernerkundung und Künstlicher Intelligenz.

Ein Schwerpunkt der Stelle liegt auf der Untersuchung von Wasserstress in urbanen Bäumen durch die

Verknüpfung hochauflösender Zeitreihen aus einer multispektralen Phenocam mit Bodenfeuchte- und

baumphysiologischen Messdaten. Darüber hinaus werden Fernerkundungsdaten mit in-situ Messungen kombiniert, um Evapotranspiration über verschiedene räumliche Skalen hinweg abzuleiten.

Ihre Aufgaben

Wir suchen eine*n hoch motivierte*n Kandidat*in mit überdurchschnittlichen Qualifikationen, Begeisterung für Forschung und der Bereitschaft zur Mitarbeit in einem internationalen und interdisziplinären Projektkontext. Das Geoinformationslab für Umweltplanung der Technische Universität Berlin bietet daher eine Stelle als wiss. Mitarbeiter*in (d/m/w) im Bereich Fernerkundung und urbane Ökologie an.

Die Aufgaben dieser Stelle im Rahmen des Forschungsprojektes SAVE Water umfassen:


Auswertung von Zeitreihen multispektraler Kameraaufnahmen (Phenocam) urbaner Bäume
Verknüpfung und Vergleich von Zeitreihen aus Saftflussmessungen, Dendrometern und Bodenfeuchtesensoren
Analyse von Wasserstress und physiologischen Reaktionen urbaner Vegetation
Integration und Auswertung von Fernerkundungsdaten (z. B. Sentinel-2)
Kombination von Sentinel-2 Daten mit Eddy-Covariance (Eddy Flux) Messungen zur großflächigen Ableitung von Evapotranspiration
Entwicklung und Anwendung von Zeitreihenanalyseverfahren zur Identifikation von Stressindikatoren
Mitarbeit in internationalen Projektstrukturen sowie Unterstützung bei wissenschaftlichen Publikationen


Ihr Profil


Erfolgreich abgeschlossenes wissenschaftliches Hochschulstudium (Master, Diplom oder Äquivalent) in Umweltwissenschaften, Geoökologie, Ökologie, Physische Geographie oder einem verwandten Gebiet mit starkem Bezug zur Ökologie und Fernerkundung
Erfahrung in der Analyse von Fernerkundungsdaten
Erfahrung in urbaner Ökologie und pflanzenphysiologischen Fragestellungen
Kenntnisse in den Bereichen Programmierung (R und/oder Python) sowie Zeitreihenanalyse
Sehr gute Englischkenntnisse in der mündlichen und schriftlichen Kommunikation
Kenntnisse im Bereich Evapotranspiration, insbesondere in der Kombination von Fernerkundungs- und Eddy-Covariance-Daten von Vorteil
Erfahrungen im Upscaling von Punktmessungen auf größere räumliche Skalen (z. B. mit Sentinel-2) von Vorteil
Erfahrungen mit Saftflussmessungen, Dendrometern oder Bodenökologie von Vorteil
Begeisterung für die Arbeit in interdisziplinären und internationalen Teams von Vorteil


Hinweise zur Bewerbung

Ihre Bewerbung richten Sie bitte unter Angabe der Kennziffer und den üblichen Unterlagen per E-Mail an sekretariat@geoinformation.tu-berlin.de. Es werden nur einzelne pdf-Dateien akzeptiert, die alle relevanten Informationen (Motivationsschreiben, CV, Zeugnisse) enthalten.

Bei Fragen wenden Sie sich an Dr. Basem Aljoumani (basem.aljoumani.1@tu-berlin.de).

Mit der Abgabe einer Onlinebewerbung geben Sie als Bewerber*in Ihr Einverständnis, dass Ihre Daten elektronisch verarbeitet und gespeichert werden. Wir weisen darauf hin, dass bei ungeschützter Übersendung Ihrer Bewerbung auf elektronischem Wege keine Gewähr für die Sicherheit übermittelter persönlicher Daten übernommen werden kann. Datenschutzrechtliche Hinweise zur Verarbeitung Ihrer Daten gem. DSGVO finden Sie auf der Webseite der Personalabteilung: https://www.abt2-t.tu-berlin.de/menue/themen_a_z/datenschutzerklaerung/.

Zur Wahrung der Chancengleichheit zwischen Frauen und Männern sind Bewerbungen von Frauen mit der jeweiligen Qualifikation ausdrücklich erwünscht. Schwerbehinderte werden bei gleicher Eignung bevorzugt berücksichtigt. Die TU Berlin schätzt die Vielfalt ihrer Mitglieder und verfolgt die Ziele der Chancengleichheit. Bewerbungen von Menschen aller Nationalitäten und mit Migrationshintergrund sind herzlich willkommen.

Ihre Bewerbung richten Sie bitte unter Angabe der Kennziffer und den üblichen Unterlagen per E-Mail an sekretariat@geoinformation.tu-berlin.de. Es werden nur einzelne pdf-Dateien akzeptiert, die alle relevanten Informationen (Motivationsschreiben, CV, Zeugnisse) enthalten.

Bei Fragen wenden Sie sich an Dr. Basem Aljoumani (basem.aljoumani.1@tu-berlin.de).

Mit der Abgabe einer Onlinebewerbung geben Sie als Bewerber*in Ihr Einverständnis, dass Ihre Daten elektronisch verarbeitet und gespeichert werden. Wir weisen darauf hin, dass bei ungeschützter Übersendung Ihrer Bewerbung auf elektronischem Wege keine Gewähr für die Sicherheit übermittelter persönlicher Daten übernommen werden kann. Datenschutzrechtliche Hinweise zur Verarbeitung Ihrer Daten gem. DSGVO finden Sie auf der Webseite der Personalabteilung: https://www.abt2-t.tu-berlin.de/menue/themen_a_z/datenschutzerklaerung/.

Zur Wahrung der Chancengleichheit zwischen Frauen und Männern sind Bewerbungen von Frauen mit der jeweiligen Qualifikation ausdrücklich erwünscht. Schwerbehinderte werden bei gleicher Eignung bevorzugt berücksichtigt. Die TU Berlin schätzt die Vielfalt ihrer Mitglieder und verfolgt die Ziele der Chancengleichheit. Bewerbungen von Menschen aller Nationalitäten und mit Migrationshintergrund sind herzlich willkommen.

Title: Founder's Associate in Climate-Tech
Description:
Deine Mission



What matters most in this role

You establish yourself as the right hand of the CEO or COO, taking full ownership for major projects and being a 100% self starter
You adapt into new roles and overcome challenges quickly in a fast moving early stage climate-tech startup environment
You have to step out of your comfort zone to gain quick turnaround and delivering surpassing expectations

About the Job
Be the link between our founders and the teams. As a jack of all trades you are directly supporting the founders in executing strategic initiatives and driving business growth as well as managing day-to-day operations, project management, and strategic planning
You take full ownership of high-impact special projects that align with our strategic goals and vision
You prepare presentations, reports, and other communication materials for internal and external stakeholders
You identify and address operational challenges and opportunities for improvement
You contribute to the development and implementation of new ideas and strategies to drive growth
You will work closely with the founders and take on a variety of exciting tasks, ranging from strategic projects to daily operational tasks





Dein Profil



You have previous experience (at least 1-2 years) in a fast-paced start-up / scale-up environment, consulting, IB or VC role
You come with strong analytical and problem-solving skills as well as a proactive mindset
You have excellent written and verbal communication skills in English (German is a plus), with the ability to convey complex ideas concisely
You thrive in a dynamic, rapidly changing environment and are a self-starter with a high sense of ownership, strong work ethic, entrepreneurial mindset and the ability to work independently
You are passionate about contributing to our goal of energy-efficient buildings and a sustainable future





Warum wir?



Join one of Europe’s fastest growing companies, backed from global top tier investors.
Why Now VARM?
Buildings are the largest energy consumers in Europe, making them CO₂ monsters. They account for 40% of our energy usage, causing 36% of all EU CO₂ emissions. That's why the decarbonization of buildings is essential for a sustainable future.
Why Us?
At VARM, we are pushing insulation forward. We help our customers save up to 50% on their heating costs and decrease their CO₂ emissions. To scale insulation, we closed a a new financing Round with top-tier investors. Join our mission now to change an entire industry for good.
Our Goal?
Becoming Europe’s Insulation Champion and insulate 1 million houses over the next 10 years! Now is your chance to be part of our vision. Join us in building a market-leading company in the climate sector. Unf:ck the planet with us and actively contribute to slashing CO₂ emissions from buildings.

Our Benefits:


Competitive compensation package: We will offer you a mix of cash and stock options based on your appetite for upside
Be part of our core-team, building in a super-hot phase of an early-stage climate-tech startup
Make an impact at VARM: Shape our culture and the way we work and collaborate together. Nothing is set in stone – you have a direct impact, while we are growing and building together
Make an impact on the earth: Do you want to make a difference in the fight against climate change? Become part of the solution and actively shape the energy transition
Onsite first, but hybrid work keeps us flexible: We believe that in-person collaboration is an integral part of working together, but offer flexibility for remote working
Grow with us: We take your personal development seriously, which includes being mentored and challenged in regular founder feedback sessions
Start–up culture: We offer you hybrid work, training, team events, and a great office setup in the heart of Kreuzberg
Discounted fitness programs
Diversity & Inclusion: We value diversity and welcome people from all backgrounds





Über uns



Warum jetzt?Gebäude sind der größte Energieverbraucher in Europa. Sie verbrauchen 40 % unserer Energie und verursachen 36 % aller CO₂-Emissionen. Mehr als 85 % der heutigen Gebäude werden auch im Jahr 2050 noch stehen. Daher ist die Dekarbonisierung von Gebäuden ein wichtiger Bestandteil auf dem Weg zur Klimaneutralität.
Warum wir?Wir bei VARM glauben, dass es ein absoluter No-Brainer ist, Gebäude energieeffizienter zu machen. Wie wir das schaffen? Indem wir Wärmedämmung neu denken und unseren Kunden dabei helfen, bis zu 50 % ihrer Heizkosten und ihrer CO₂-Emissionen einzusparen.

Unser Ziel?Wir wollen Marktführer in Europa im Bereich Wärmedämmung werden und haben auf dem Weg dahin Großes vor. Hier ist deine Gelegenheit, mit uns dieses Ziel zu erreichen und Großes zu schaffen. Lass uns gemeinsam ein marktführendes und nachhaltiges Unternehmen aufbauen und einen positiven Beitrag fürs Klima leisten.

Title: Data & AI Consultant (m/w/d)
Description:
Was Dich in Deiner neuen Aufgabe erwartet


Du gestaltest den gesamten Prozess von der Anforderungsanalyse bis zum Roll-out individueller Datenmodelle und Datenstrategien und baust dabei kontinuierlich Dein fachliches und technologisches Know-how aus.
Du realisierst spannende Projekte mit modernen State-of-the-Art Data- und AI-Technologien.
Du gestaltest Lösungen entlang der gesamten Daten-Wertschöpfungskette - von der Cloud-Plattform über die Datenladestrecke bis hin zu BI-Reports und ML-Modellen.
Du wechselst flexibel zwischen inspirierenden Kundenworkshops und fokussierten Coding-Sessions.
Du arbeitest mit vielfältigen Kunden aus der DACH-Region zusammen und erhältst Einblicke in unterschiedliche Branchen.


Was wir uns von Dir wünschen


Du begeisterst Dich für aktuelle Entwicklungen im Bereich Data & AI und lernst schnell dazu.
Du trittst selbstbewusst und kundenorientiert auf - sowohl in Workshops als auch in Projekten - und überzeugst durch Kommunikationsstärke, Teamfähigkeit und Reisebereitschaft.
Du verfügst über Kenntnisse in modernen Datentechnologien wie Microsoft Fabric oder Databricks.
Du hast erste Erfahrung mit AI- und ML-Technologien, beispielsweise mit GPT-Modellen, Microsoft Foundry oder Databricks AI.
Du beherrschst Python; Kenntnisse in weiteren Programmiersprachen sowie Erfahrung mit Cloud-Plattformen wie Azure, AWS oder GCP sind ein Plus.
Du verfügst über sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse auf mindestens C1-Niveau.


Was wir Dir bieten


Eine Arbeitsumgebung mit viel Eigenverantwortung, Vertrauen und echter Mitgestaltung
Individuelle Weiterentwicklung durch Lernzeitbudgets und aktiven Wissenstransfer
Regelmäßige Team- und Sport-Events - vom digitalen Lunch bis zum jährlichen Retreat
Sinnstiftende Projekte, die unseren Kund:innen Transparenz und Entscheidungssicherheit bieten
Flexible Arbeitszeiten für eine ausgewogene Work-Life-Balance
Vielfältige Weiterbildungsangebote
Moderne Hardware und optimal ausgestattete Arbeitsplätze
Umfangreiche Sport- und Wellnessflatrate

Title: Investment Research Analyst
Description:
About The Job

Mercor connects elite creative and technical talent with leading AI research labs. Headquartered in San Francisco, our investors include Benchmark, General Catalyst, Peter Thiel, Adam D'Angelo, Larry Summers, and Jack Dorsey.

Position: Hedge Fund Expert

Type: Contract

Compensation: $200/hour

Location: Remote

Duration: Minimum four weeks

Commitment: 10+ hours/week

Role Responsibilities


Evaluate hedge fund strategies related to long/short equity to enhance AI model training.
Develop and review financial models to improve AI-generated financial content.
Conduct market sizing and TAM analysis to inform AI research teams.
Analyze investment research, summaries, and memos for AI model evaluation.
Predict and analyze earnings to refine AI model outputs.
Work independently and asynchronously to meet deadlines and improve AI model performance.



Qualifications

Must-Have


2+ years of experience in top hedge funds.
Experience in financial modeling, market sizing/TAM analysis, or investment research.



Start Date


October



Compensation & Legal


Hourly contractor
Paid weekly



Application Process (Takes 20–30 mins to complete)


Upload resume
AI interview based on your resume
Submit form



Resources & Support


For details about the interview process and platform information, please check: https://talent.docs.mercor.com/welcome
For any help or support, reach out to: support@mercor.com



PS: Our team reviews applications daily. Please complete your AI interview and application steps to be considered for this opportunity.

Title: Founders Associate
Description:
Als Founders Associate (m/w/d) arbeitest du eng mit unserem Gründerteam zusammen und bekommst einen einzigartigen Einblick in den Aufbau und die Weiterentwicklung eines Tech-Startups. Du übernimmst Verantwortung in strategischen und operativen Projekten und unterstützt bei vielfältigen Themen im Tagesgeschäft.


Strategische Zusammenarbeit: Enge Unterstützung der Gründer und des Leadership-Teams bei der Identifikation und Umsetzung von kommerziellen Initiativen, die das Wachstum des Unternehmens maßgeblich vorantreiben.
Projektverantwortung: Eigenverantwortliche Steuerung und Begleitung von strategisch relevanten Projekten mit Fokus auf Business Development, Sales Enablement und Prozessoptimierung.
Cross-funktionale Koordination: Du wirkst als zentrale Schnittstelle zwischen verschiedenen Teams (z.B. Sales, Product, Customer Success) und sorgst für eine zielgerichtete Abstimmung aller Aktivitäten.
Markt- und Wettbewerbsanalyse: Du beobachtest und analysierst relevante Markttrends sowie Kundenanforderungen und unterstützt so die datenbasierte Entscheidungsfindung.
Unterstützung von Wachstumsinitiativen: Du hilfst bei der Entwicklung und Optimierung von Go-to-Market Strategien und trägst zur Expansion in neue Märkte bei.
Vorbereitung von Präsentationen und Reportings: Du erstellst aussagekräftige Analysen, Entscheidungsvorlagen und Präsentationen für interne und externe Stakeholder.


Your profile


Nachgewiesene Erfahrung in einer ähnlichen Funktion, vorzugsweise in einem schnell wachsenden SaaS-Unternehmen oder gerne auch in der Managementberatung - plus wenn deine Aufgaben dort im kommerziellen Bereich lagen.
Du zeichnest dich durch strategisches Denken und die Fähigkeit aus, Projekte von der Konzeption bis zur Umsetzung zu begleiten.
Du bist kommunikativ stark und kannst funktionsübergreifende Teams motivieren und führen.
Du hast den Ehrgeiz, dich in einer dynamischen und schnell wachsenden Organisation weiterzuentwickeln, und möchtest langfristig Verantwortung übernehmen.
Verhandlungssichere Englischkenntnisse sind für dich selbstverständlich.
Du sprichst Deutsch auf muttersprachlichem Niveau.


Why us


Direkte Zusammenarbeit mit erfahrenen Gründern
Tiefer Einblick in den Aufbau und die Skalierung eines SaaS-Start-ups
Viel Verantwortung ab Tag 1
Flexible Arbeitszeiten & hybride Arbeitsweise
Offene, ehrliche und motivierte Teamkultur mit kurzen Entscheidungswegen
Aussicht auf Übernahme als Werkstudent:in oder in Festanstellung


Über uns

Cosuno – Wir revolutionieren die Baubranche durch Technologie.

Wir sind Cosuno – ein schnell wachsendes Tech-Startup, das die Baubranche mit unserer digitalen Plattform für Ausschreibungen und Beschaffungsprozesse effizienter und transparenter macht. Unser Ziel: die komplexesten Herausforderungen der Branche mit Innovation und Einfachheit zu lösen.

Warum Cosuno?

Künstliche Intelligenz ist der Schlüssel zu unserer Lösung: Sie analysiert Millionen von Preisdatenpunkten und hilft Bauunternehmen dabei, präzise und effiziente Angebote zu erstellen. Das spart unseren Kunden nicht nur Zeit, sondern hilft ihnen auch, unnötige Kosten zu vermeiden.

Aber für uns geht es nicht nur um Technologie – es geht um die Menschen, die sie vorantreiben. Bei Cosuno arbeitest du mit kreativen Köpfen zusammen, die die Baubranche neu gestalten. Wir glauben an Diversität, denn wir wissen, dass die besten Ideen aus unterschiedlichen Perspektiven entstehen. Ein integratives und exklusives Arbeitsumfeld ist für uns eine Selbstverständlichkeit.

Werde Teil unseres Teams – Gestalte die Zukunft der Baubranche.

Title: Data Engineer Berlin (m/w/d)
Description:
Schwarz Digits creates the technological foundation for digital sovereignty in Europe. As the IT and digital division of the Schwarz Group, we develop and manage the IT infrastructures for the retail divisions Lidl and Kaufland, as well as Schwarz Production and PreZero. At the same time, we operate as an independent provider in the external market to support companies across Europe in their digital transformation. We bundle our core services in the areas of Cloud, Cyber Security, Data & AI, Communication, and Workspace.

Join us and contribute to digital sovereignty in Europe. With us, you will work at the intersection of agility and security: You will benefit from fast decision-making processes, enjoy genuine creative freedom in your projects, and be able to build upon the stable foundation of the Schwarz Group.

Your Tasks


Develop and operate productive ETL pipelines for reports, analysis and machine learning and ensure their quality and sustainability
Use your software development skills and set new standards for development and deployment
Optimize the performance of data engineering solutions and guarantee their scalability
Ensure data quality and consistency through data validation and cleansing
Monitor and fix errors in the data infrastructure to minimize downtime
Implement data security and privacy measures
Work in interdisciplinary agile teams with data scientists, data engineers, product owners and departments


Your Profile


+4 years of industry experience as a Data Engineer
Experience developing large scale batch & streaming ETLs in spark, preferably in Databricks
Expertise developing in Python. Scala knowledge is a plus
Good command of Apache Airflow for workflow orchestration
Experience working in cloud environments, preferably GCP
Strong hands-on experience with modern, open-source table formats such as Apache Iceberg or Delta Lake to manage large-scale data lakehouse architectures
Proven track record managing data access controls, CLS, RLS and cataloging workflows. Experience with Unity Catalog is a plus
Hands-on experience working with containerized applications, preferably orchestrated in Kubernetes
Experience working with Google Pub/Sub is preferred
Knowledge of the software development cycle and its best practices (e.g. CI/CD, DevOps, testing)
Analytical skills, an eye for data quality, agile mindset, independence and hands-on mentality

At the same time, we operate as an independent provider in the external market to support companies across Europe in their digital transformation. We bundle our core services in the areas of Cloud, Cyber Security, Data & AI, Communication, and Workspace.

Join us and contribute to digital sovereignty in Europe. With us, you will work at the intersection of agility and security: You will benefit from fast decision-making processes, enjoy genuine creative freedom in your projects, and be able to build upon the stable foundation of the Schwarz Group.

Your Tasks

  • Develop and operate productive ETL pipelines for reports, analysis and machine learning and ensure their quality and sustainability
  • Use your software development skills and set new standards for development and deployment
  • Optimize the performance of data engineering solutions and guarantee their scalability
  • Ensure data quality and consistency through data validation and cleansing
  • Monitor and fix errors in the data infrastructure to minimize downtime
  • Implement data security and privacy measures
  • Work in interdisciplinary agile teams with data scientists, data engineers, product owners and departments

Your Profile

  • +4 years of industry experience as a Data Engineer
  • Experience developing large scale batch & streaming ETLs in spark, preferably in Databricks
  • Expertise developing in Python. Scala knowledge is a plus
  • Good command of Apache Airflow for workflow orchestration
  • Experience working in cloud environments, preferably GCP
  • Strong hands-on experience with modern, open-source table formats such as Apache Iceberg or Delta Lake to manage large-scale data lakehouse architectures
  • Proven track record managing data access controls, CLS, RLS and cataloging workflows. Experience with Unity Catalog is a plus
  • Hands-on experience working with containerized applications, preferably orchestrated in Kubernetes
  • Experience working with Google Pub/Sub is preferred
  • Knowledge of the software development cycle and its best practices (e.g. CI/CD, DevOps, testing)
  • Analytical skills, an eye for data quality, agile mindset, independence and hands-on mentality

Title: Founder's Associate (CTO Office)
Description:
About Us

Every 10 minutes, 18 million conversations happen between businesses and consumers around the world.

For decades, the consumer side of those conversations sucked: long queues, robotic menus, repeated explanations, and advice that is often unhelpful. Most people today still cannot simply say what their problem is in natural language when communicating with businesses.

AI changes that. For the first time, companies can have natural conversations with customers at massive scale. Every B2C company - from energy providers to telcos to insurers - will use AI agents to talk to their customers. The number of conversations businesses will handle is going to explode in the coming years.

At telli, we are solving the challenges that come with that shift. Today, leading B2C companies like Sky are already using telli to deploy thousands of voice agents to provide their customers with a new experience. But building a first voice agent is easy, getting it to drive real outcomes for customers and businesses is the hard part. telli helps companies build, deploy, and improve consumer-facing AI voice agents at scale. This will become one of the most important software categories of the next decade, and we intend to win it.

We are a small, AI-pilled team that likes to solve hard problems. Be it engineering, product, or GTM - we build, experiment, and move fast, while heavily leveraging the capabilities of AI models. All of us like to challenge ourselves and take real ownership over what we do, all while still staying humble and keeping a low-ego culture.

Check out our principles to see how we work.

About The Role

This is a role for a hyper-ambitious technical/product person who wants to start their own company one day. You will work directly with our CTO Seb on the most pressing day-to-day problems.

things you might do


Support everything hiring-related to get absolute A-player engineers into the door
Organize hackathons and attend events to meet great technical/product talent
Research product & customer problems to shape product roadmap


you're a fit if you


think and move fast
high agency - you just do things
can communicate precisely
can take very direct feedback
motivated by what's most important for the company instead of what you want to work on
super AI-pilled
great product taste
studied something technical at a top university

Title: Insights Analyst
Description:
The PureGym Way

The PureGym Group is a global gym business with a community of more than 700 gyms and over 2 million members. At PureGym, everything we do comes back to one simple idea: helping you feel good.

We're proud of our people and have a strong focus on internal progression. Championing diversity, we are committed to providing an excellent employee experience and workplace culture. As such, we are a Disability Confident Committed employer, and offer interviews to candidates who meet the essential criteria for a role, and opt-in to the scheme on their application form.

Our gyms are friendly, supportive, and judgement-free spaces where everybody can come in, work out and leave Feeling PureGym Good. We are proud to be certified by Top Employers Institute. See our careers page for full benefits.




We offer

Free nationwide gym membership for you + 1
Hybrid working
A truly flexible working culture
Personal private healthcare, including digital GP
Life insurance x4
Company pension contribution
25 days annual leave, plus 1 personal day
Option to purchase additional holiday (up to 5 days)
Great learning & development resources
Enhanced maternity pay, paternity and adoption leave




Location: Hybrid- London (3 days in the office)

Type: Full Time

Contract type: Permanent

Application: Closing date 22nd May 2026

We are a value business, focused on making gyms and fitness accessible to as many people as possible. Data is at the heart of how we run our business, starting from where to open our gyms, through what we offer within them, to how we engage with past, current, and future members. Combining the data from our 2.5+ million members with data about locations, operations, and performance allows us to unlock industry leading insights and drive a better member experience and commercial performance.

As an Insights Analyst you will be at the heart of solving some of the hardest data challenges in the business, turning data into insights and business value through a combination of in-depth analysis and reporting creation. You will be leveraging one of the biggest fitness data assets in the world, and working with state-of-the-art technologies, to help determine what drives member behaviour and commercial outcomes across all our operating geographies.




The Role

As a small, agile team, Insights works closely with its stakeholders, putting data at the heart of decision making across the business. While reporting to the Insights Manager, in this role you get to work closely with stakeholders at all levels across the business. You will also work in close collaboration with the wider Insights & Analytics functions, which provides ample opportunity to learn from a group of talented and experienced Data Scientists and Data Engineers.

In this role, you will have the opportunity to:

Work on business-critical analytics projects and reporting, identifying outcomes and communicating findings and recommendations to stakeholders across the business - Commercial, Finance, Operational and Marketing teams, in addition to Data Science and Data Engineering.
Build and maintain the central data product serving KPIs and insights to the entire organisation.
Work with Azure Databricks, PowerBI and dbt technology, leveraging modern cloud-native technologies and data lakehouse architecture.
Drive continuous improvement to how we enable data driven decision making in PureGym.




The Person

We would expect some or all of the following:

Experience in data analytics / insights
Proficient with SQL, Power BI, Excel and PowerPoint
Additionally, experience with dbt would be an advantage
A drive to turn insights into compelling stories for stakeholders to understand, for them to make more effective, data-led decisions
Strong commercial awareness with an interest in B2C

We are looking for an ambitious individual with a passion for leveraging analytics to drive business outcomes. The following core attributes are essential:

Excellent problem-solving: someone who enjoys applying their mind to many and varied business problems and be a driving force behind solving them
Innovative and curious: constantly seek to learn and find new and better ways of doing things; not just solving problems but seeking them out; always interested to find new and better methods and technologies
Biased towards action: take the initiative and get things done under their own steam and in collaboration with others
Collaborative: driven to engage with colleagues from across the business and support and enable your team members
A great communicator: able to translate complex concepts and analyses into digestible recommendations that can be easily interpreted and acted upon




PureGym is proud to be an equal opportunities employer. Our company mantra is ‘Everybody Welcome’ and we are dedicated to promoting a diverse and inclusive place to work. From a hiring standpoint, we welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to ensuring that our PureGym colleagues reflect the diversity of the nation as well as our millions of gym members we serve.




Please note: Qualifications and Skills represent the essential criteria for the role, which need to be met to be offered an interview under the Disability Confident Scheme. Volume of eligible applications may impact everyone being able to be offered an interview.

Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis, and the advert may be withdrawn at any time. Early application is encouraged.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst




Industry: Designer toys and collectible pieces

Location: London, Canary Wharf

Job type: Full time Permanent




About POP MART




POP MART (09992.HK) is a leading global company in the trend culture and entertainment industry. Centered around IP, POP MART has built a comprehensive platform for creative incubation and IP operation, empowering global creators while delivering exciting products, services and immersive entertainment experiences to consumers.




POP MART identifies and nurtures emerging artists and designers worldwide, creating popular character IPs through a well-established development and operation system. Our portfolio includes iconic characters such as MOLLY, SKULLPANDA, DIMOO, THE MONSTERS and Hirono. POP MART operates over 500 physical stores and more than 2,300 Robo Shops across more than thirty countries and regions, and through multiple cross border e commerce platforms reaches audiences in over ninety countries and regions, bringing joy to young consumers around the world.




POP MART has developed a comprehensive IP commercialisation ecosystem that spans multiple industries, deepening emotional bonds between fans and IPs while exploring broader opportunities for value creation. Our ventures include the world’s first immersive intellectual property themed park in the art toy industry, the expansion of its IPs into global markets through licensing and co-branding with leading global brands, and the curation of art toy expos and intellectual property themed exhibitions internationally. In Europe, POP MART has built a strong presence in key markets including the United Kingdom, France, Italy, Germany, the Netherlands and Spain, and is expanding into new high potential markets. Our expansion brings POP MART closer to European fans and offers new ways for them to connect with our IPs and stories.




For more information, please visit www.popmart.com and our LinkedIn Career page.




What to Expect




POP MART is a fast-paced company with a young and multicultural team where you can expect to:




Contribute to a fast-growing European business that combines retail, art, and creative storytelling
Work with passionate teams across different countries, all connected by a shared love for designer toys and unique customer experiences
Grow your skills through hands-on projects, collaborative learning, and opportunities to explore new ideas




Benefits




POP MART offers our employees:




Monthly appreciation gifts featuring our latest POP MART designs
A structured bonus scheme linked to both company and individual performance
25 days of annual leave, in addition to bank holidays
4x life insurance, together with group medical and travel insurance
A confidential Employee Assistance Programme, alongside access to a discount platform for selected online retailers
Training and development programmes, together with regular team events that encourage learning, collaboration, and celebration




The Role and Candidate




We are looking for a data driven and detail oriented professional to support business performance monitoring and analysis across POP MART’s European business.

This role focuses on operational reporting, performance tracking, and business insight generation across key revenue channels, including physical stores, vending machines, and online business. You will support the business by monitoring core operational indicators, identifying trends and issues through data, and helping teams better understand performance through clear, accurate, and practical analysis.

You will also work closely with teams including Finance, Retail Operations, Marketing, Merchandising, and Business Development to support regular reporting, operational reviews, and day to day data requests. In parallel, the role will contribute to improving reporting consistency, data quality, and the practical use of data in business operations.

This position would suit someone with solid analytical capability, structured thinking, and a hands on approach, who is comfortable working with detailed data while supporting wider business needs in a fast paced environment.




Responsibilities




Daily Operation Monitoring and Performance Review




Establish daily, weekly and monthly monitoring for core operational indicators, including revenue, gross profit, expense ratio, payment collection, labour efficiency and store efficiency, across all revenue channels such as physical stores, vending machines and online business
Prepare daily, weekly, monthly and quarterly operational reports in a timely manner, benchmark performance against budget, year on year and month on month data, and analyse the causes of performance gaps
Track key business indicators including sales volume, average transaction value, conversion rate and profit margin, and provide regular operational analysis to support performance improvement
Analyse the performance of different markets, stores and vending machines, identify trends and issues, and support the development of targeted actions to improve profitability
Support the Business Development team with data input for store and vending machine site selection, based on market data and operational analysis
Support monthly and quarterly operational review meetings by preparing review materials and presentations, highlighting business progress, key issues and improvement priorities, and tracking follow up actions
Work closely with the Finance team to verify operational, cost and expense data, align statistical definitions, resolve data discrepancies, and ensure management reporting is accurate and reliable




Business Insights and Reporting




Work closely with business teams on a daily basis to understand key business pain points, and support practical data solutions based on business needs and operating scenarios
Conduct data analysis across sales operations, marketing and consumer insights, identify the business logic behind the data, and provide insights to support business optimisation
Improve the quality and depth of recurring insight reports by moving beyond data listing and strengthening business focused conclusions and recommendations
Build and maintain structured data records and archives to support data traceability, consistency and reusability
Extract and process data from multiple sources, including business systems, CRM and ERP systems, and use SQL for regular queries, data cleaning, deduplication, correlation and aggregation
Build and maintain basic dashboards and automated reports to reduce manual reporting, improve data visibility, and support more efficient access to information




Cross Functional Collaboration and Data Governance




Support Sales, Marketing, Product, Supply Chain, Finance and other teams with day to day data requests, including customised queries, report exports and basic analysis support
Provide guidance to business teams on basic data viewing and analysis methods to improve the practical use of data in daily operations
Support the standardisation of data fields, statistical rules and analysis templates, and help establish data logs and archiving practices to strengthen data governance
Contribute to the development of a more consistent and practical data culture across the business




Required Qualifications




3–5 years of business analysis experience; candidates with experience in management consulting, internet business analysis, retail operations analysis, or other relevant analytical experience, or those with extensive professional experience are preferred




Excellent communication and organisational skills, capable of handling complex issues with structured thinking and analytical abilities




Proactive, hands-on, and entrepreneurial mindset with experience working in a fast-paced business environment




Outstanding problem-solving, analytical, written and verbal communication skills




Data-sensitive, able to identify business trends and issues through data analysis; proficiency in data analysis and visualisation tools such as Excel, SQL, Tableau is preferred, but strong willingness to learn is a must




Proficiency in English (listening, speaking, reading and writing), with extensive experience in cross-team project collaboration and communication







Desirable but Not Essential




Experience in the toy industry or pop culture retail sector
Familiarity with Local and European retail landscapes
Understanding of designer toy and collectible trends, particularly in the art toy market
Experience supporting operational reporting across multiple countries or regions
Understanding of retail KPIs including conversion rate, ATV, margin, labour efficiency and inventory performance
Exposure to data governance, reporting automation, or process improvement initiatives
Experience within retail, consumer goods, eCommerce, or multi-site operations environments
Experience working with ERP, CRM, POS or business intelligence systems
Familiarity with dashboard tools such as Tableau, Power BI or Looker










If you are excited to be part of POP MART’s journey and believe you have the skills and passion to contribute, click Apply Now and send us your CV.

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
Graduate / Early Career Specialty Data Analyst - Marine, Cargo and Logistics





We're looking for a curious, analytical graduate or early‑career Data Analyst to join our Marine, Cargo and Logistics Specialty team.





This is a hands‑on role where you'll learn how data is used in business decisions, working closely with brokers, data specialists, and technology teams. You'll help turn complex datasets into clear insights that support clients and business across sport, media, and entertainment. You don't need to know everything on day one. We're looking for strong foundations, curiosity, and the motivation to learn fast.





With support and mentoring from experienced analysts, you'll:


Work with brokers and business stakeholders to understand real‑world problems and translate them into data questions.


Explore and prototype data ideas, helping shape user stories and analytical approaches.


Clean, analyse and validate data from multiple sources to ensure accuracy and reliability.


Build insights, visuals and case studies that are used in client discussions and proposals.


Spot patterns and trends in data and help explain what they mean in practice.


Use modern data and analytics tools to turn raw data into clear, actionable outputs.


Collaborate with data, technology and analytics teams across the wider group.


Document your work and follow best‑practice data standards.





What we are looking for:





Essential


A degree (or equivalent) in a numerate or analytical subject (e.g. maths, statistics, data science, economics, engineering, sciences)



Strong attention to detail and logical thinking


A genuine interest in how data can influence business decisions


Confidence explaining ideas to both technical and non‑technical audiences


An inquisitive, problem‑solving mindset





Technical skills (you don't need all of these on day one):


Experience using Excel for analysis


Exposure to Python, SQL and/or R through study, projects or internships


Familiarity with data visualisation tools such as Power BI (or similar)


Interest in learning modern data platforms (Databricks)





Nice to have (but not required)


University or personal projects involving real datasets


Basic understanding of data modelling or data pipelines


Curiosity about GenAI / LLMs and how they can support analytics


An interest cargo. marine, logistics, insurance and commercial analytics.





What you'll get





A structured early‑career role with on‑the‑job learning and mentoring



Exposure to client and operations work


Training and professional qualifications (e.g. Chartered Insurance Institute)


Competitive salary, generous pension and private medical cover

You'll help turn complex datasets into clear insights that support clients and business across sport, media, and entertainment. You don't need to know everything on day one. We're looking for strong foundations, curiosity, and the motivation to learn fast.


With support and mentoring from experienced analysts, you'll:

  • Work with brokers and business stakeholders to understand real‑world problems and translate them into data questions.

  • Explore and prototype data ideas, helping shape user stories and analytical approaches.

  • Clean, analyse and validate data from multiple sources to ensure accuracy and reliability.

  • Build insights, visuals and case studies that are used in client discussions and proposals.

  • Spot patterns and trends in data and help explain what they mean in practice.

  • Use modern data and analytics tools to turn raw data into clear, actionable outputs.

  • Collaborate with data, technology and analytics teams across the wider group.

  • Document your work and follow best‑practice data standards.


What we are looking for:


Essential

  • A degree (or equivalent) in a numerate or analytical subject (e.g. maths, statistics, data science, economics, engineering, sciences)

  • Strong attention to detail and logical thinking

  • A genuine interest in how data can influence business decisions

  • Confidence explaining ideas to both technical and non‑technical audiences

  • An inquisitive, problem‑solving mindset


Technical skills (you don't need all of these on day one):

  • Experience using Excel for analysis

  • Exposure to Python, SQL and/or R through study, projects or internships

  • Familiarity with data visualisation tools such as Power BI (or similar)

  • Interest in learning modern data platforms (Databricks)


Nice to have (but not required)

  • University or personal projects involving real datasets

  • Basic understanding of data modelling or data pipelines

  • Curiosity about GenAI / LLMs and how they can support analytics

  • An interest cargo. marine, logistics, insurance and commercial analytics.


What you'll get


  • A structured early‑career role with on‑the‑job learning and mentoring

  • Exposure to client and operations work

  • Training and professional qualifications (e.g. Chartered Insurance Institute)

  • Competitive salary, generous pension and private medical cover

Title: Data Analyst
Place: City Of London, England, United Kingdom
Description:
Who are we?👋

Look at the latest headlines and you will see something Ki insures. Think space shuttles, world tours, wind farms, and even footballers' legs. 


Ki's mission is simple. Digitally disrupt and revolutionise a 335-year-old market. Working with Google and UCL, Ki has created a platform that uses algorithms, machine learning and large language models to give insurance brokers quotes in seconds, rather than days. 


Ki is proudly the biggest global algorithmic insurance carrier. It is the fastest growing syndicate in the Lloyd's of London market, and the first ever to make $100m in profit in 3 years. 


Ki's teams have varied backgrounds and work together in an agile, cross-functional way to build the very best experience for its customers. Ki has big ambitions but needs more excellent minds to challenge the status-quo and help it reach new horizons.


Where you come in?

The Data Analyst at Ki works at the intersection of analytics, engineering and product. They work within teams and across Ki's teams to support and drive design, development, and optimization of analytics, data models, reports, and other data-driven products that support strategic business goals.  


What you will be doing: 🖋️


Applied Analytics

Develop knowledge and expertise in Ki's business domains to generate insights and measurement on initiatives that drive company objectives.
Collaborate with business stakeholders to identify opportunities for leveraging data analytics to drive commercial results and improve operational efficiency.
Identify opportunities to leverage data analytics techniques to extract value from both internal and external data assets to enrich analytical capabilities.


Analytics Engineering

Develop and maintain the core data sets underlying the suite of analytical tools used by the team
Create and maintain data visualizations and dashboards using tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or similar.
Develop scalable data solutions that conform to Ki's technology and engineering principles and industry best practice.


Data Management & Governance

Preserve the integrity of data, making sure it is accurate, consistent, and reliable throughout its lifecycle.
Investigate and lead timely resolution of emerging issues with underlying data systems and models as and when these arise.
Ensure that domain data is managed and used according to Ki's data governance policy and best practice principles.


Other

Ensure all data systems and models are documented in line with Ki wide standards
Recommend ways to improve data efficiency and reliability
Increase the degree of automation within the team's data systems and tools
Investigate new tools that would help the team store, structure and analyse data.


Requirements


Proficiency in SQL and Python to manipulate and analyse datasets 
Experience with data reporting and visualisation tools (e.g. Tableau, Looker, Dash, Streamlit) 
Solid understanding of statistical techniques (e.g. regression, clustering, correlation analysis), with ability to apply these to commercial problem solving. 
Experience working in cloud native environments (especially GCP) would be plus. 
Experience with designing and implementation of data structures to ensure ease of use and accessibility by a broader audience. 
Self-organised, good at documenting and communicating findings and approach. 
Excellent communicator, able to present complex analysis to both technical and non-technical audiences. 
Experience working in a regulated industry


What to expect during the recruitment process:

Initial recruiter screening call
Interview with hiring manager
Technical Interview (this may vary depending on the role)
Values Interview

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
LOCATION: Pier Walk, London

SHIFT PATTERN: Monday - Friday, 8am - 5pm, 40 hours per week

SALARY: £38,000.00 per annum

If you require any additional support or adjustments during the recruitment process, please don't hesitate to contact our Recruitment Department at recruitment@abm.com. We're here to help!

Role Overview And Purpose

The Data Analyst role will support the TfL Mechanical & Electrical contract to support contractual performance and data consistency. The candidate will work within the performance management team working alongside a lead data analyst to ensure consistent operational performance management of our data.

Key Responsibilities


Data entry
Data processing
Data analysis and reporting.
Consolidating data.
Understand the current systems and work towards improving the data relationships between different systems.
Reviewing data collection processes and assessing the validity of the information stored
Identifying anomalous data and examining and eliminating any processes or procedures that contribute to unreliable data being collected.
Working closely with the relevant teams to ensure that the data collection and storage systems are secure and robust.
Create interactive reports & dashboards with Power BI


This is a challenging role that will require good analytical and project management skills.

Required Skills And Experience


Microsoft Excel High level of skill and experience
Collect, review, analyse and maintain contract performance data and use it to support contract delivery.
Produce dashboard reports from contract delivery data to assist contract compliance, budget and strategic reviews and trends.
Identify and implement quality and efficiency improvement initiatives. Identifying inefficiencies across the contract.
Assist in the development of the innovation plan for service delivery improvements.
Engage business leaders and senior operational managers across the business to ensure best practice and operational efficiencies are implemented.
Understand, anticipate and deliver customer (internal and external) needs while building effective relationships.
Efficiently respond to both internal and external customers through effective communication and personal accessibility.
Ensure customer satisfaction is high.
Carry out other admin tasks as directed.


Benefits

We're proud to offer a great range of benefits including:


24/7 GP: Both you and your immediate family can speak to a UK-based GP from the comfort of your own home
Mental Health support and Life Event Counseling
Get Fit Programme
Financial and legal support
Cycle to work scheme
Access Perks at Work, our innovative employee app where you can find:
Perks: discounts, gift cards, cashback, and exclusive offers
Life: Search for resources and tools on topics ranging from family and life to health, money and work
Support: Online chat or telephone service for urgent support in a crisis


For More Information About ABM's Benefits, Visit Our

About ABM:

ABM (NYSE: ABM) is one of the world's largest providers of integrated facility, engineering, and infrastructure solutions. Every day, over 100,000 team members deliver essential services that make spaces cleaner, safer, and efficient, enhancing the overall occupant experience.

ABM serves a wide range of market sectors including commercial real estate, aviation, mission critical, and manufacturing and distribution. With over $8 billion in annual revenue and a blue-chip client base, ABM delivers innovative technologies and sustainable solutions that enhance facilities and empower clients to achieve their goals. Committed to creating smarter, more connected spaces, ABM is investing in the future to meet evolving challenges and build a healthier, thriving world. ABM: Driving possibility, together.

For more information, visit .

ABM are committed to employment practices that promote diversity and inclusion in employment regardless of age, disability, gender reassignment, sex, marriage and civil partnership status, pregnancy and maternity status, race, religion or belief. We are proud members of the Armed Forces Covenant Employer Recognition Scheme.

Title: ESG Data Analyst Internship
Description:
This 3-month internship is perfect for someone who enjoys turning complex data into actionable insights and drive impactful business decisions. You will dive deep into energy, water, and waste consumption across our diverse portfolio, leveraging PowerBI, Microsoft Co-Pilot and AI tools. You will not only analyze trends but also uncover the underlying reasons for variances, considering the dynamic realities of hotel operations, such as changing opening periods, guest occupancy rates, temperature fluctuations, and renovation activities.

Primary Responsibilities:


Reviewing data across the Belmond portfolio, against recognized regional benchmarks on EarthCheck's Platform and against wider industry data sources such as World Sustainability Hospitality Alliance and Cornell University's Hotel Sustainability Benchmark tool
Working closely with the Director of Operations Support and Sustainability to identify gaps in operational data and build out a database of sustainability actions by property
Reviewing sustainability reports across the industry sector to identify the set of data points that should be included in our strategic reporting for 2026
Codifying the methodology behind creation of the quarterly insights report.
Collating comparative indicators that translate complex emissions and water consumption data into clear compelling statistics for team engagement
Analysis of environmental and social impact data, extracting key insights and recommending actions that drive swift performance improvement.
Produce high quality, engaging written and visual communications to gather information efficiently and in a timely manner from teams on properties
Prepare materials and activities for sustainability related presentations and activations with universities and schools


Your curiosity and analytical expertise will be unlock new insights to drive action and deliver greater environmental and social impact.

Requirements


Comfortable working with data and apply analytical skills to real-world business and sustainability challenges
Demonstrated experience using Excel and Power BI to support data-driven decision-making
Experience conducting analytical work
Strong interest in ESG, sustainability, and the use of data to support reporting, insights, and strategic initiatives


Benefits

What We Offer

At Belmond, we pride ourselves on fostering a culture built on kindness, active listening, and genuine connections with our colleagues and guests.

We strive to create an environment where our employees can thrive both personally and professionally. With a commitment to your ongoing development, you'll have the opportunity to learn and grow alongside some of the most talented individuals in the industry. Join us and be part of creating unforgettable moments for our guests, communities and each other.

This is your moment. Apply today!

About Us

Belmond was born from a passion for connecting discerning travellers with the world's most remarkable properties, locations and journeys. From hotels and trains to river cruises and safari lodges, Belmond's Slow Luxury invites guests to discover a new pace of travel as they savour time, engage with local culture, and connect with nature and people around them through incomparable experiences and unforgettable stories. With a legacy spanning over 45 years, since the acquisition of Hotel Cipriani in Venice in 1976, Belmond has grown into a global collection of 43 properties spread across 24 countries and territories. Exceptional destinations connected by legendary journeys are the very soul of Belmond, where the path that brings you to a place is as important as the destination itself. A pioneer of slow travel, Belmond has been operating the Venice Simplon-Orient-Express since 1982. Belmond later expanded to include pristine beach resorts, such as Maroma on the Riviera Maya, historic hideaways such as Villa San Michele in the Florentine foothills, urbane icons, such as Copacabana Palace in Rio de Janeiro, and gateways to UNESCO world heritage sites, such as Hotel das Cataratas in Brazil's Iguassu National Park. As proud custodians of storied properties, Belmond is committed to building on the past to create a new legacy: the heritage of the future. Working with communities and local talents, together with the world's most respected chefs, designers and artists, Belmond continues its purpose of perpetuating the legendary art of travel. Since 2019, Belmond has been part of the world's leading luxury group, LVMH (Moët Hennessy Louis Vuitton).

Title: Data Analyst - TikTok LIVE
Description:
Responsibilities
The Regional LIVE Operation Strategy Team leads various cross-functional projects. Our key initiatives include developing strategic plans to drive widespread adoption of LIVE at scale among creators, localizing new product features, creating user engagement strategies, ensuring a positive creator experience through education on the Community Guidelines and best practices, and providing ad hoc analysis and support to cross-functional partners.

We're looking for a Senior Data Analyst to join our dynamic LIVE team. In this role, you’ll uncover insights and deliver data-driven recommendations that directly impact how we grow and support our creator ecosystem. You'll work cross-functionally with product, strategy, and operations teams to shape our growth priorities using data and analytical thinking.

- Analyze local and global content and user trends to identify growth opportunities within the EU LIVE ecosystem.
- Create, automate, and maintain reports and dashboards to track performance, OKRs, and key strategic initiatives.
- Develop data models and perform in-depth analyses to support content strategy, creator segmentation, and user engagement.
- Translate complex data into actionable insights for regional teams and leadership.
- Collaborate closely with cross-functional teams (e.g., Creator Management, Product, Strategy) to inform decision-making.
- Present findings clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders to influence business strategy.

Examples of Problems You’ll Be Solving
- What are the best content verticals for us to focus our creator recruitment on?
- How can we build a predictive model to identify high-potential creators early?
- What are the right leading and lagging indicators to measure success against our key OKRs?
- How can we segment creators and users to better personalize support and growth strategies?
- When and how should we educate creators to maximize their long-term retention and performance?
- How can we simplify complex data sets using statistical tools to better diagnose issues and recommend scalable solutions?

Qualifications
Minimum Qualifications
- 5+ years of experience in data analysis, preferably in a high-growth or tech environment.
- Strong mathematical and analytical skills, with the ability to interpret large datasets and generate actionable insights.
- Proficiency in SQL and at least one programming language (Python preferred).
- Solid understanding of statistics and data modeling techniques.
- Experience using data visualization tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or Looker.
- High attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy.

Preferred Qualifications
- Experience working in a consumer technology, content, or creator economy business.
- Background in building predictive models or recommendation systems.
- Familiarity with A/B testing and experimental design.
- Strong communication skills, with the ability to simplify complex topics for non-technical audiences.
- Knowledge of the live streaming industry or digital content ecosystems is a plus.

About TikTok
TikTok is the leading destination for short-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo.​


Why Join Us
Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy - a mission we work towards every day.​
We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us.​
​
Diversity & Inclusion​
TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.​
