Title: Data Analyst, Business Intelligence
Description:
StackAdapt is the leading technology company that empowers marketers to reach, engage, and convert audiences with precision. With 465 billion automated optimizations per second, the AI-powered StackAdapt Marketing Platform seamlessly connects brand and performance marketing to drive measurable results across the entire customer journey. The most forward-thinking marketers choose StackAdapt to orchestrate high-impact campaigns across programmatic advertising and marketing channels.

StackAdapt is a self-serve programmatic advertising platform used by the world's top digital marketers. We are looking for a Data Analyst II to join our Business Intelligence team within Advertising Technologies. In this role, you will deliver advanced data analysis and reporting that drives strategic decision-making across product, revenue, and operations teams. You will work with complex datasets, build trusted reporting pipelines, and recommend new KPIs to improve business visibility. This is an excellent opportunity for a data professional who thrives on translating raw data into actionable insights in a fast-paced, AI-augmented environment.

This is a remote-friendly position open to candidates based in Canada.

What You'll Be Doing:
Design, build, and maintain high-quality dashboards, reports, and datasets that support business decision-making across product, revenue, and operations teams.
Write advanced SQL queries to extract, transform, and validate data from multiple sources, ensuring accuracy and reliability of reporting outputs.
Identify and recommend new KPIs and metrics that improve business visibility and stakeholder understanding of performance trends.
Manage project plans for new reporting needs, including requirements gathering, milestone tracking, and timely delivery.
Partner with cross-functional teams (Product, Revenue, Operations) to clarify data requirements, resolve data quality issues, and support evidence-based planning.
Within your first 6 months, you will own the delivery of regular BI reporting cycles, contribute to at least one new KPI framework, and actively support the team's adoption of AI-assisted workflows using Claude for reporting and coding tasks and Gemini for planning and meeting documentation.
Reporting SLA adherence: dashboards and ad hoc reports delivered within agreed timelines.
Data quality: reduction in data errors or discrepancies flagged by stakeholders.
KPI adoption: number of new KPIs recommended and accepted by business partners.
Stakeholder satisfaction: positive feedback from cross-functional partners on insight quality and responsiveness.
Project delivery: reporting project plans completed on time and within scope.
Leverage AI/LLM tools (e.g., Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini) to accelerate analysis, automate repetitive tasks, and draft documentation.
What You'll Bring to the Table
2+ years of experience in a data analytics role, with a track record of delivering actionable insights from complex datasets.
Advanced SQL proficiency, including the ability to write complex queries, troubleshoot data issues, and work with data warehouses (e.g., Redshift, Snowflake, BigQuery).
Experience building and maintaining dashboards and reports using BI tools (e.g., Looker, Tableau, Sigma, or similar).
Strong project management skills: ability to clarify requirements, set milestones, manage risks, and deliver reporting initiatives independently.
Excellent communication skills with the ability to present data findings clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Ability to write effective prompts, critically evaluate AI-generated output, and integrate AI tools into day-to-day workflows.

The compensation range listed for this role reflects the expected base salary for candidates located in Ontario, Canada. It is informed by market data and the approved budget for this position. StackAdapt maintains different compensation ranges for roles across other countries and regions, and final offers will be aligned to the candidate’s current location.This range represents base salary only. Depending on the role, candidates may also be eligible for additional compensation such as annual bonuses, commissions, equity awards, and a comprehensive benefits package.Factors Influencing Final Compensation:The final compensation offer will be determined by a variety of factors, which may include, but are not limited to: the candidate's specific experience, technical skills, knowledge, abilities, and relevant education, licensure, and certifications.Other business factors, such as organizational needs and budget alignment, may also be considered in the final offer.

Ontario Salary Band: $76,000 CAD - $90,000 CAD

StackAdapter's Enjoy:
Highly competitive salary
Retirement/ 401K/ Pension Savings globally
Competitive Paid time off packages including birthday's off!
Access to a comprehensive mental health care program
Health benefits from day one of employment
Work from home reimbursements
Optional global WeWork membership for those who want a change from their home office and hubs in London and Toronto
Robust training and onboarding program
Coverage and support of personal development initiatives (conferences, courses, books etc)
Access to StackAdapt programmatic courses and certifications to support continuous learning
An awesome parental leave program
A friendly, welcoming, and supportive culture
Our social and team events!

Please note: Benefits and perks may vary depending on your country of employment and the nature of your engagement. In locations where StackAdapt does not have a legal entity, employment and benefits are administered in accordance with local regulations and partner policies.

StackAdapt is a diverse and inclusive team of collaborative, hardworking individuals trying to make a dent in the universe. No matter who you are, where you are from, who you love, follow in faith, disability (or superpower) status, ethnicity, or the gender you identify with (if you’re comfortable, let us know your pronouns), you are welcome at StackAdapt. Title: Data Analyst, Autonomous Vehicles
Description:
At Lyft, our purpose is to serve and connect. We aim to achieve this by cultivating a work environment where all team members belong and have the opportunity to thrive.

Lyft stands at the very center of autonomous vehicle (AV) innovation by uniting the operational power of comprehensive Fleet Management with our world-class Rideshare network. Through Flexdrive, we are revolutionizing transportation by building the operational backbone for AV fleets— pioneering this new chapter with our first AV depot in Nashville, Tennessee. Lyft brings the autonomous future to life by matching our community of riders with self-driving vehicles to meet their transportation needs.

To strengthen our efforts, we are hiring a Data Analyst to help us make data driven decisions. As a Data Analyst in the autonomous space, this role will uniquely be exposed to both AV Depot Operations and the AV Rideshare network. As we build the future of mobility from the ground up, we are looking for a strong thought leader and executor who is passionate about driving data driven decisions and energized by solving complex problems. You will partner with a wide range of stakeholders – Operations, Business, Finance, Product, Data Science, Engineering and many others. This role will report into the Senior Analytics Manager within Lyft’s Global Growth Org with opportunities for senior leadership visibility.

The ideal candidate - You have strong technical skills, are team-driven and have a growth mindset. You can roll-up your sleeves to undertake large cross functional projects. You are comfortable navigating the ambiguity of innovative projects and operate effectively in fast-paced environments. The Autonomous industry excites you and you are looking for the chance to drive high impact projects in this space as Lyft engineers next-generation transportation.

Responsibilities:


Leverage technical skills to build reports, dashboards and tools for stakeholders
Develop insights through analysis to build informed narratives, deliver strategic recommendations and provide actionable guidance that optimizes business performance
Work with external partners to communicate data needs that enable success in tracking toward critical service levels and manage incoming data requests
Work with business and leadership to define critical KPIs and metrics to support cross-functional teams, manage performance, create operational rigor and proactively seek ways to improve the business
Ability to engage with FP&A partners on P&L Management and financial modeling
Bridge Operations with Tech – become an expert in the vehicle lifecycle, from the day-to-day Fleet Operations that keep AV fleets running smoothly to the tech that transports riders from point A to B
Take full ownership in solving complex problems with data and rally stakeholders to implement solutions
Define processes and streamline the operational cadence for business performance tracking
Comfortable leading meetings, packaging work and presenting in large forums


Experience:


Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field
3-5 years of experience with a mix of business analytics in a high growth or complex business, investment banking with strong analytical exposure and/or role at a top-tier consulting firm
Deep understanding of complex data models, BI methodologies, data architecture principles and metadata
Proficient in SQL and working with raw data sets. (Python or other programming languages is a huge plus.)
Proven success in driving influence within the business
Ability to operate successfully in a lean, fast-paced organization and proactively thrive in ambiguous environments
Outstanding interpersonal and stakeholder management skills with a proven ability to work cross functionally & effectively with individual contributors, management, and leadership across the organization
Operationally-focused, process-oriented, data-driven, and simply able to get stuff done
Strong communication skills with the ability to package findings & technical concepts for leadership consumption
Previous experience in P&L involvement and/or Mobility or Auto-Tech industry is a huge plus


Benefits:


Extended health and dental coverage options, along with life insurance and disability benefits
Mental health benefits
Family building benefits
Child care and pet benefits
Access to a Lyft funded Health Care Savings Account
RRSP plan with company match to help save for your future
In addition to provincial observed holidays, salaried team members are covered under Lyft's flexible paid time off policy. The policy allows team members to take off as much time as they need (with manager approval). Hourly team members get 15 days paid time off, with an additional day for each year of service
Lyft is proud to support new parents with 18 weeks of paid time off, designed as a top-up plan to complement provincial programs. Biological, adoptive, and foster parents are all eligible.
Subsidized commuter benefits and Lyft ride credits

  • Deep understanding of complex data models, BI methodologies, data architecture principles and metadata
  • Proficient in SQL and working with raw data sets. (Python or other programming languages is a huge plus.)
  • Proven success in driving influence within the business
  • Ability to operate successfully in a lean, fast-paced organization and proactively thrive in ambiguous environments
  • Outstanding interpersonal and stakeholder management skills with a proven ability to work cross functionally & effectively with individual contributors, management, and leadership across the organization
  • Operationally-focused, process-oriented, data-driven, and simply able to get stuff done
  • Strong communication skills with the ability to package findings & technical concepts for leadership consumption
  • Previous experience in P&L involvement and/or Mobility or Auto-Tech industry is a huge plus

Benefits:

  • Extended health and dental coverage options, along with life insurance and disability benefits
  • Mental health benefits
  • Family building benefits
  • Child care and pet benefits
  • Access to a Lyft funded Health Care Savings Account
  • RRSP plan with company match to help save for your future
  • In addition to provincial observed holidays, salaried team members are covered under Lyft's flexible paid time off policy. The policy allows team members to take off as much time as they need (with manager approval). Hourly team members get 15 days paid time off, with an additional day for each year of service
  • Lyft is proud to support new parents with 18 weeks of paid time off, designed as a top-up plan to complement provincial programs. Biological, adoptive, and foster parents are all eligible.
  • Subsidized commuter benefits and Lyft ride credits

Lyft is committed to creating an inclusive workforce that fosters belonging. Lyft believes that every person has a right to equal employment opportunities without discrimination because of race, ancestry, place of origin, colour, ethnic origin, citizenship, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital status, family status, disability, pardoned record of offences, or any other basis protected by applicable law or by Company policy. Lyft also strives for a healthy and safe workplace and strictly prohibits harassment of any kind. Accommodation for persons with disabilities will be provided upon request in accordance with applicable law during the application and hiring process. Please contact your recruiter if you wish to make such a request.

Lyft highly values having employees working in-office to foster a collaborative work environment and company culture. This role will be in-office on a hybrid schedule — Team Members will be expected to work in the office at least 3 days per week, including on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays. Lyft considers working in the office at least 3 days per week to be an essential function of this hybrid role. Your recruiter can share more information about the various in-office perks Lyft offers. Additionally, hybrid roles have the flexibility to work from anywhere for up to 4 weeks per year. #Hybrid

The expected base pay range for this position in the Toronto area is CAD $96,000 - CAD $120,000, not inclusive of potential equity offering, bonus or benefits. Salary ranges are dependent on a variety of factors, including qualifications, experience and geographic location. Your recruiter can share more information about the salary range specific to your working location and other factors during the hiring process.

Lyft may use artificial intelligence to screen applicants, however, Lyft employees make the ultimate selection and hiring decisions.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Overview

BET99 is Canada's Premiere Online Sportsbook and Casino. Launched in 2020, we have consistently innovated the online gaming landscape every step of the way, exponentially growing our customer base and, in turn, our internal BET99 team! We’re a fast-paced company and are looking for individuals who live and breathe our core values: challenging the status quo, trust, transparency, integrity, and collaboration.




As a Data Analyst, you will play a key role in helping teams across the business make informed decisions by translating data into clear, actionable insights. You bring strong analytical skills, curiosity to explore business problems, and the ability to communicate findings effectively to non-technical stakeholders.




In our fast-paced online sportsbook and casino environment, you’ll work closely with stakeholders in product, marketing, and operations to analyse performance, surface insights, and support data-driven decision-making. You’ll contribute to building reliable, scalable analytics and reporting while continuously developing your technical and analytical capabilities




Vacancy Status: New




Responsibilities

Partner with stakeholders to understand business questions and translate them into analytical tasks and data requirements
Analyse datasets to uncover trends, patterns, and insights related to player behaviour, marketing performance, and operational KPIs
Write clear, efficient SQL queries to extract and structure data from Snowflake and other sources
Use Python where appropriate to clean, transform, and analyse data
Build and maintain dashboards and standard reports using BI tools such as Power BI, Sigma, and others to support ongoing business visibility
Deliver campaign and product analysis, including segmentation, funnel analysis, and performance tracking
Contribute to defining, documenting, and monitoring core business metrics
Communicate insights through clear narratives, visualisations, and written summaries tailored to different audiences
Own analytical tasks end-to-end with minimal supervision, escalating ambiguity or data quality issues when appropriate
Collaborate with data engineering and analytics teammates to ensure data quality, consistency, and scalability of outputs




Requirements

3–6 years of experience in a data analyst or business intelligence role
Strong SQL skills and experience working with analytical databases (Snowflake experience is a plus)
Hands-on experience with BI tools, ideally Power BI
Working knowledge of Python for data analysis
Experience analysing product, customer, or operational data (experience in iGaming or digital products is a plus but not required)
Solid understanding of metrics, KPIs, and basic statistical concepts
Ability to communicate insights clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders
A pragmatic, detail-oriented mindset with a desire to continuously improve analytics and reporting




Nice to Have

Experience with experimentation or A/B test analysis
Exposure to dbt or analytics engineering concepts




Why Join BET99?

Come for the career… stay for the culture.

Canada-wide remote freedom - your office is anywhere you want it to be.
Time off that feels good - paid vacation, paid sick days, plus your birthday off on us.
Early access to leadership - meet the CEO in your first 30 days and see how your work truly makes an impact.
Open-door leadership style - transparency and collaboration are core to how we operate.
Premium benefits - 100% employer paid, for you and your family.
Built-in career acceleration - Mentorship Programs led by industry veterans.
A community you’ll want to be part of - book clubs, monthly employee events, cross-team fun, and more.
Bring a friend, earn a bonus - our referral program rewards great connections.
Regular recognition programs - so your great work never goes unnoticed.
Modern tools and tech - making your job smoother, not harder.
A streamlined onboarding process - hit the ground running with confidence.
A company that invests in long-term relationships, not quick wins.




Disclaimers:

This position does not use artificial intelligence during the hiring process.

Title: Azure Data Analyst
Description:
Position Description

This role is hybrid and requires you to be at our downtown Toronto and/or Client office at a minimum 2 days per week - subject to change at any time.

The Azure Data Analyst will play a critical role in designing, analyzing, and delivering enterprise‑scale data solutions within an Azure cloud ecosystem. This role requires a strong combination of data design expertise, business analysis skills, and domain knowledge in personal banking products. The candidate will collaborate closely with business stakeholders, data engineers, and agile delivery teams to translate business needs into high‑quality, scalable data models and solutions.

Your future duties and responsibilities

Data Design & Architecture


Design and maintain logical and physical data models aligned with business requirements and enterprise standards.
Define end to end data mappings from source systems to target data platforms, ensuring accuracy and completeness.
Perform data profiling to assess data quality, structure, and anomalies, and recommend remediation strategies.
Support the definition of data standards, naming conventions, and reference data.


Azure & Databricks Development Support


Collaborate with engineering teams to design data solutions using Azure services (e.g., Azure Data Lake, Synapse, Databricks).
Work closely with Databricks developers to ensure data transformations align with approved data models.
Review and validate data pipelines to ensure compliance with business rules and architectural guidelines.


Business Analysis & Banking Domain Engagement


Partner with business stakeholders to gather, analyze, and document data and reporting requirements.
Apply strong understanding of personal banking products (such as accounts, customers, transactions, lending, and payments) to data design decisions.
Translate business processes and regulatory requirements into structured data representations.


SQL & Data Validation


Write advanced SQL queries for data exploration, validation, reconciliation, and issue investigation.
Support user acceptance testing (UAT) and data validation activities by comparing source and target datasets.
Identify data quality issues and work with technical teams to resolve root causes.


Agile Delivery & Collaboration


Actively participate in Agile ceremonies (sprint planning, grooming, stand ups, reviews).
Estimate and plan data design and analysis tasks in coordination with scrum teams.
Maintain accurate documentation and user stories using Jira and Confluence.
Support continuous improvement of agile data delivery practices.


Communication & Stakeholder Management


Communicate complex data concepts clearly to technical and non technical stakeholders.
Lead data design reviews, walkthroughs, and stakeholder discussions.
Provide guidance and mentorship to junior data analysts or designers when required.


Required Qualifications To Be Successful In This Role

Core Technical Skills


Strong experience with Azure Data Platform (Azure Data Lake, Azure SQL, Synapse, or related services).
Hands on experience with Databricks for data transformations and analytics.
Advanced SQL skills for data analysis, profiling, and validation.
Proven expertise in Data Profiling, Data Mapping, and Data Modeling (logical and physical).


Business & Domain Skills


Strong Business Analysis skills, including requirements elicitation and documentation.
Solid knowledge of personal banking products and data domains.
Ability to translate business needs into scalable and reusable data designs.


Tools & Ways of Working


Experience using Jira for backlog and sprint management.
Proficient in Confluence for documentation and knowledge sharing.
Demonstrated experience working in Agile/Scrum delivery environments.


Soft Skills


Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
Strong stakeholder engagement and collaboration abilities.
Analytical mindset with strong attention to detail.
Ability to work independently while contributing effectively to team goals.


________________________________________

Nice to Have (Optional)


Experience in regulated financial services environments.
Exposure to data governance, metadata management, or data lineage tools.
Familiarity with reporting or downstream analytics use cases.


"CGI is providing a reasonable estimate of the pay range for this role. The determination of this range includes factors such as skill set level, geographic market, experience and training, and licenses and certifications. Compensation decisions depend on the facts and circumstances of each case. A reasonable estimate of the current range is $80,000–$130,000. This role is an existing vacancy."

!

Together, as owners, let’s turn meaningful insights into action.

Life at CGI is rooted in ownership, teamwork, respect and belonging. Here, you’ll reach your full potential because…

You are invited to be an owner from day 1 as we work together to bring our Dream to life. That’s why we call ourselves CGI Partners rather than employees. We benefit from our collective success and actively shape our company’s strategy and direction.

Your work creates value. You’ll develop innovative solutions and build relationships with teammates and clients while accessing global capabilities to scale your ideas, embrace new opportunities, and benefit from expansive industry and technology expertise.

You’ll shape your career by joining a company built to grow and last. You’ll be supported by leaders who care about your health and well-being and provide you with opportunities to deepen your skills and broaden your horizons.

At CGI, we value the strength that diversity brings and are committed to fostering a workplace where everyone belongs. We collaborate with our clients to build more inclusive communities and empower all CGI partners to thrive. As an equal-opportunity employer, being able to perform your best during the recruitment process is important to us. If you require an accommodation, please inform your recruiter.

To learn more about accessibility at CGI, contact us via email. Please note that this email is strictly for accessibility requests and cannot be used for application status inquiries.

Title: Analyst, Data & Analytics
Description:
Permanent Full Time

Canada Life is seeking a highly skilled Data Analyst to join the Insurance Solutions team. As a part of the Performance Management area, this role will be responsible for supporting the data management, analysis, and reporting needs of the department. The Data Analyst supports decision-making, monitors business performance, and provides actionable insights using analytics and visualizations tailored to different audiences.

The role will benefit from collaboration with subject matter experts and data scientists. If you are looking to become a subject matter expert in connected data and help guide the team in improving processes through data mining and analysis, this is the role for you.

What You Will Do


Build, implement, and maintain data models, custom reports, and dashboards, keeping in mind the needs of varied audiences
Create visually compelling presentations and use strong communication skills to present them to stakeholders (ability to build a narrative that outlines the background and highlights the impact of initiatives)
Identify opportunities to ingest data, prioritize data needs, and build data marts to drive efficiencies in reporting functions
Leverage data expertise to support new initiatives through analysis and ongoing reporting across the entire analytics lifecycle
Communicate requirements to the Technology team for development and ensure smooth deployment of data projects after performing data quality testing
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to gather requirements and deliver data solutions that meet business needs
Support existing data owners in their data management practices, ensuring a single source of truth
Work with large datasets that may be inconsistent, unclean, or not fully modeled
Ensure best practices are followed with respect to data governance and structure
Communicate effectively while demonstrating critical thinking and business acumen


What You Will Bring


2+ years of relevant experience and a university or college degree in a related field of study, or an equivalent combination of education and experience
Experience working with raw data and manipulating large datasets
Proficiency in Excel, Power BI, data modelling, and Databricks, with experience creating interactive dashboards
Ability to work with diverse data inputs and build data marts aligned with business requirements
Proven ability to learn quickly, work independently, and manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment
Ability to work with little to no documentation or instructions
Strong attention to detail, with the ability to produce high-quality outputs under tight timelines
Driven to continuously improve processes, performance, and efficiency
Knowledge of the organization, distribution channels, products, systems, and data is an asset
Ability to write complex SQL queries to extract and integrate data from multiple sources
Proficiency in scripting languages such as Python or R
Hands-on experience with Azure compute services and a good understanding of Azure Data Factory pipelines (nice to have)
Preferred: Microsoft Certified – Azure Data Analyst Associate (Power BI) certification


The base salary for this position is between $65,800 - $87,700 annually. This represents base salary only and does not represent other variable compensation components of our total compensation ( i.e. annual bonus, commission etc). If you are selected to move forward in our recruitment process, your recruiter will be able to discuss additional details of our total rewards program with you.

Career opportunities will be open a minimum of 5 business days from the date of posting, closing dates will vary depending on the search activity. All applications received will be reviewed on a rolling basis.

Grow with Canada Life

We’re united by a shared purpose: to improve the financial, physical and mental well-being of Canadians. Our company is trusted by 1 in 3 Canadians and contributes to the strength of communities across the country.

We’re looking for people who live our values everyday: we step up, we do the right thing, and we deliver – for our customers, communities and each other. Are you someone who always strives to do the right thing, who steps up for themselves and others, and who delivers with impact? Then we want to hear from you!

What we offer:

We’re committed to supporting our employees through every stage of their career. Here’s what you can expect as a full-time or part-time permanent team member:


Career Development: Opportunities for career advancement, access to industry-leading learning programs and up to $2,000 annually towards education reimbursement.
Health & Wellness: Flexible health and dental benefits, plus a $5,000 mental health benefit to support your well-being.
Time Off: In addition to regular vacation and personal days, we support community involvement with a volunteer day. 
Financial Security: Company-matching pension plan, share ownership program and additional investment options.
Rewards and Recognition: Employee recognition programs, service milestone celebrations, employee discounts and more!
Emphasis on Community: We provide a workplace where employees feel connected and supported through Employee Resource Groups (ERGs), mentorship programs, social clubs and events.


Learn more about Canada Life.

We’re committed to removing barriers and ensuring equal access to employment. Applicants requiring reasonable accommodation during the application process may contact talentacquisitioncanada@canadalife.com. All information provided will be handled in accordance with applicable laws and Canada Life policies.

Canada Life would like to thank all applicants, however only those who qualify for an interview will be contacted.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Company Overview

Brother is a leading provider of home and office equipment, sewing and crafting products, and industrial solutions. With a legacy spanning over 115 years, our brand is renowned for producing award-winning printers, sewing machines, P-touch labelers, and more. Brother Canada was established in 1960, marking over 65 years of operations. Brother Canada is headquartered in Dollard-des-Ormeaux, Quebec and is a wholly owned subsidiary of Brother International Corporation (BIC), based in the United States. BIC is in itself a subsidiary of Brother Industries Limited (BIL), which was founded in 1908 in Nagoya, Japan and operates in more than 30 countries worldwide.

Why Work at Brother?

At Brother Canada, we’re more than a team; we’re a community guided by our culture drivers of Accountability, Authenticity, Excellence, and Boldness. We foster collaboration, growth, and work-life balance through flexible hours, hybrid work options, wellness subsidies, learning and development, recognition programs, and employee discounts. With roots in Montreal since 1960, we embrace continuous improvement and a culture of “Challenging Spirit,” offering stability while delivering a superior customer experience.

Role Overview

Reporting to the Director, Analytics & Insights, the Data Analyst enables better, faster and simpler decisions through data and science. They generate valid, reliable and timely insights for business operations and strategic initiatives, drive go-to-market tactics through effective performance analysis and ensures that internal customers’ needs are met, and that operational excellence is improved and maintained.

The Data Analyst works closely with the rest of the analytics team to build & maintain tools & processes that enable the manipulation and transformation of data across multiple platforms. They have the mandate to automate processes and imbed data science principles to enhance decision making processes. They meet with stakeholders to gather business requirements, translate them into technical requirements, execute development deliverables, test functionalities, address ad-hoc requests, execute data related standard operating procedures and manage stakeholder expectations along the way. Additionally, the Data Analyst builds, maintains and enhances reports and dashboards as part of their business intelligence responsibilities. Finally, the Data Analyst commits to enhancing customer experience and identifies plans and executes continuous improvement endeavors accordingly.

Duties & Responsibilities

Analysis & reporting


Understand and capture business needs from stakeholders to analyze and track the performance of their activities.
Gather requirements and prepare formal requirements documents.
Develop statements of work to secure buy-in from stakeholders regarding analysis methods, timelines, roles & responsibilities and deliverable formats
Develop new, enhance and maintain reports and dashboards in Tableaus.
Generate valid and reliable insights by designing analysis methodologies and executing analysis projects that are repeatable and that produce statistically significant results.
Address ad-hoc reports and analysis requests.
Communicate execution progress through written status updates

Data management and quality assurance


Build processes to manipulate, transform and extract insights from datasets across various types of data sources using Python & Pandas
Troubleshoot and perform root case analysis activities to resolve technical challenges and recommend scalable solutions
Formulate, maintain, and enhance ETL pipelines
Migrate databases from local servers into cloud data warehouses (AWS, Azure Databricks)
Transform, refactor, and deploy ETL & ML models to production level in a virtual or cloud-based system (AWS, Azure Databricks)
Assemble large datasets from various data sources for prescriptive and predictive analytics
Flag data integrity issues and execute quality assurance procedures.

Experience & Qualifications


Bachelor’s Degree specializing in Business Administration, Economics, Computer Science, Analytics, Computer Engineering, Information Systems, Applied Math/Statistics, or related fields.

1-3 years of experience in analytics roles.
Has practical experience with statistics and research methods (quantitative and qualitative).
Ability to articulate results and communicate conclusions eloquently.
Can analyze large data sets from multiple sources through efficient database tools & methods
Experience in understanding departmental needs and in building effective, positive partnerships to work collaboratively with cross-functional business teams (technical and non-technical stakeholders).
Advanced proficiency with Python, SQL, etc.
Knowledge of Python ML Libraries such as Sci-kit Learn and TensorFlow
Experience with big data tools like Apache Spark, Pandas and Polars
Knowledge of cloud-based systems like AWS, Azure and development tools like Git, Bash are a plus
Advanced working knowledge with relational SQL and NoSQL databases i.e. MySQLand formulating/manipulating complex queries
Knowledge of Tableau
Is detail-oriented, self-motivated and time-efficient.
Is flexible and can adapt quickly to an ever-changing environment.
Is capable of working in high-pressure, time-constrained situations.

Title: ICQA Data Analyst
Description:
Description

At Amazon, we're working to be the most customer-centric company on earth. To get there, we need exceptionally talented, bright, and driven people. If you'd like to help us build the place to find and buy anything online, this is your chance to make history.

Amazon Canada Fulfillment Services is hiring a Data Analyst to support the Inventory Control / Quality Assurance (ICQA) team. The Data Analyst assigned to ICQA will work closely with the ICQA and operations teams in Inbound and Outbound to provide data and analytical support for ACES initiatives, root cause investigation of defects, and will provide support to ensure compliance to ICQA-related SoX requirements.

The Data Analyst will own the insights, set up analysis, and create automated dashboards and reporting. They will be responsible for identifying data sources (internal / external) to invent and simplify mechanisms and create custom reporting and analytics automation tools.

Operating in a fast-moving and sometimes ambiguous environment, you will be required to work autonomously, taking full control and responsibility for achieving business objectives. This role provides real opportunity to develop original ideas, approaches and solutions in an ever-changing and competitive business climate.

Key job responsibilities


Development of data collection processes and data management systems
Maintenance of data integrity (0% error rate)
Designing of queries, compiling of data, and generation of reports in MS Excel
Charting and graphing of data for reporting purposes
In depth research of defect trends
Data collection and entry as needed
Data mining and problem solving
Back up for Process Assistant duties


Basic Qualifications


2+ years of professional or military experience
Work 40 hours/week, and overtime as required
Work a flexible schedule/shift/work area, including weekends, nights, and/or holidays
Are 18 years of age or older
2+ years of business analyst, data analyst or an equivalent role experience
1+ years of using SQL to extract and manipulate data experience
Bachelor's degree, or High school or equivalent diploma
2+ years of professional work experience, or experience in MS Office, with an emphasis on Excel
Experience writing complex Excel VBA macros
Experience in advanced SQL, Python, Tableau
Experience demonstrating problem solving and root cause analysis
Experience with modern software development, web development frameworks, APIs
1+ years of project management experience


Preferred Qualifications


3+ years of business analyst, data analyst or similar role experience
Experience in written and verbal communication skills to communicate with technical and non-technical audiences, including senior leadership
Experience dealing well with ambiguity, prioritizing needs, and delivering measurable results in an agile environment
Experience programming with at least one software programming language, or experience with full software development life cycle, including coding standards, code reviews, source control management, build processes, testing, and operations
Knowledge of data warehousing and data modeling
Experience with data scripting languages (e.g., SQL, Python, R, or equivalent) or statistical/mathematical software (e.g., R, SAS, Matlab, or equivalent)
Bachelor's degree in Business, Engineering, Statistics, Computer Science, Mathematics, Supply Chain or related field.


Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status.

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

The base salary range for this position is listed below. As a total compensation company, Amazon's package may include other elements such as sign-on payments and restricted stock units (RSUs). Final compensation will be determined based on factors including experience, qualifications, and location. Amazon offers comprehensive benefits including health insurance (medical, dental, vision, prescription, basic life & AD&D insurance), Registered Retirement Savings Plan (RRSP), Deferred Profit Sharing Plan (DPSP), paid time off, and other resources to improve health and well-being. We thank all applicants for their interest, however only those interviewed will be advised as to hiring status.

Title: Data Modeler
Description:
The Data Modeler will support AMI 2.0 analytics program by designing and documenting clear, analytics-ready data models within IBM Netezza. The role focuses on aligning business concepts with data structures to support consistent analytics, reporting, and data sharing across teams. This is a design and enablement role, not an ETL development or platform operations role.




Key Responsibilities:

Perform data discovery on datasets available in IBM Netezza
Design and maintain logical and analytical data models to support AMI 2.0 use cases
Define and align core business entities (e.g., customer, premise, meter, usage, time, grid assets)
Work with business, analytics, and architecture teams to clarify definitions and data requirements
Create and maintain data dictionaries, model documentation, and metadata
Support downstream analytics and reporting needs (e.g., Bidgely, Facts BI) through clear data structures
Assist QA and validation teams by providing data modeling subject-matter support










Required Skills:

Experience in data modeling for analytics or data warehouse environments
Strong understanding of relational and analytical data structures
Ability to translate business requirements into clear data designs
Experience working with enterprise data platforms (IBM Netezza or similar preferred)
Strong documentation and communication skills

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
TekStaff's Client has a current vacancy for a Data Analyst

Pay Rate Range: $35-$44 per hour

Duration : This is a 4 month contract located in Toronto, ON/ Hybrid




Job Description

We are seeking a skilled Data Analyst with a strong focus on data discovery and collaboration with Subject Matter Experts (SMEs) to join our dynamic team. The ideal candidate will excel at identifying, exploring, and analyzing complex datasets to uncover actionable insights while working closely with SMEs to ensure data solutions align with business needs. This role requires strong analytical skills, effective communication, and the ability to translate technical findings into business-relevant recommendations.




Candidate Value Proposition:

Data Discovery & Exploration:

• Identify, collect, and assess relevant datasets from internal and external sources to support business objectives.

• Perform exploratory data analysis (EDA) to uncover patterns, trends, and anomalies.

• Validate data quality, integrity, and consistency, addressing issues through cleansing and preprocessing.

• SME Collaboration:

• Partner with SMEs across departments (e.g., finance, marketing, operations) to understand business requirements and domain-specific challenges.

• Facilitate workshops, interviews, and discussions to gather insights and refine data-driven solutions.

• Translate SME feedback into actionable data requirements and ensure alignment with analytical outputs.

• Data Analysis & Reporting:

• Develop and maintain dashboards, visualizations, and reports using tools like Tableau, Power BI, or similar.

• Conduct statistical analysis and modeling to derive insights that inform strategic decisions.

• Present findings to stakeholders in clear, non-technical language, tailoring insights to audience needs.

• Process Improvement:

• Identify opportunities to streamline data discovery processes and enhance data accessibility for SMEs.

• Document data sources, methodologies, and findings to support reproducibility and knowledge sharing.

• Contribute to the development of data governance and best practices for data handling.




Candidate Requirements/Must Have Skills:

• 3 - 4 years of experience in data analysis, data mapping, and data modelling.

• Proven track record of working with SMEs to deliver data-driven solutions.

• Proficiency in SQL for querying and manipulating large datasets.

• Experience with Python, R, or similar data analysis and visualization, such as BI tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI) for reporting and dashboard creation.

•Experience with cloud-based data platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure, Google Cloud).

Title: Marketing Data Analyst
Company: Mitsubishi Motor Sales of Canada, Inc.
Description:
Kindly note we are not looking for software developers or programmers.




Mitsubishi Motor Sales of Canada, Inc. is an equal opportunity employer fostering an inclusive, accessible environment. We are committed to building an environment where all employees and customers feel valued, respected, and supported. Mitsubishi Motor Sales of Canada, Inc. will support applicants requiring accommodation during the recruitment process.




All employees are required to espouse MMSCAN’s company values, MMC WAY, and conduct themselves accordingly:

Think of Our Customers First; Strengthen Trust
Enrich Society
Welcome All Facts; Share Difficult News First
Conduct and Challenge Yourself Professionally
and Respect All; Work as Part of a Broader Team




Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI)

MMSCAN does not utilize AI to review or assess applicants. All screening is conducted internally by a member of the HR team and/or the hiring manager.




Are you skilled in the art of turning data into stories? Do you thrive in transforming data into insights that drive marketing performance? If this resonates, you have discovered the role you’ve been searching for!”




PURPOSE

The Analyst, Data (Marketing) will transform complex data into actionable insights that drive business decisions and marketing performance across Mitsubishi’s digital ecosystem. This role supports internal marketing teams and external partners by collecting, cleaning, organizing, and analyzing data from multiple sources—including websites, paid and organic channels, CRM systems, and ad-hoc reports.




The ideal candidate is naturally curious, detail-oriented, and skilled at turning data into stories that inspire positive change, recommending actions that generate business outcomes.




This role reports to the Manager, Marketing Operations.




RESPONSIBILITIES:




Data Collection & Organization

Gather data from digital platforms (web, paid and organic social, CRM, media tools, ad-hoc reports).
Ensure data accuracy, integrity, and taxonomy alignment across all marketing channels.
Develop datasets and visualizations (e.g., heatmaps, attribution models) that clarify digital customer journeys.




Analysis & Insight Generation

Assist the marketing team in identifying patterns, correlations or trends, and work alongside the Communications team on data-driven recommendations that will improve the performance and efficiency of digital campaigns.
Analyze website digital journeys and campaign performance to identify insights that would maximize upper and lower funnel goals (awareness, consideration, conversion)
Evaluate A/B test performance for creative, media, and landing page assets.
Segment and assess audience clusters to enhance marketing efficiency and effectiveness.




Reporting & Data Storytelling

Produce recurring and ad-hoc reports (weekly, monthly, quarterly) summarizing key performance metrics and recommendations.
Present findings, insights, and recommendations through visual and concise reports (charts, graphs, tables)
Collaborate with internal and external partners to deliver data-driven recommendations that inform strategy.




Tool Management & Optimization

Operate analytics and reporting tools such as Google Analytics (GA4), Google Tag Manager, Campaign Manager, and Looker Studio Enterprise.
Support optimization within digital platforms (Google Ads, DV360, Meta Ads, Search Ads 360).
Maintain and improve tracking, tagging, and measurement frameworks to ensure data consistency and KPI alignment.




REQUIREMENTS:




3 years of experience in Data Analytics and Digital marketing
Proven ability to extract, interpret, and present insights across multiple data sources.
Strong knowledge of digital marketing KPIs, attribution, and optimization principles.
Proficiency in Google Analytics (GA4), Looker Enterprise (or equivalent visualization tools).
Experience with tag management systems (Google Tag Manager), and paid media platforms (Google Ads, DV360, Meta, etc).
Critical thinker with strong problem-solving skills, creativity, and a keen attention to detail.
Excellent communicator who can translate complex data into clear, actionable narratives for senior stakeholders.

Title: Data Analyst (Canada)
Description:
Jobright is a next-generation AI job search platform built to make career navigation faster, smarter, and more personal. They are looking for a Data Analyst to turn raw product and agent data into the kind of insights that shape what gets built, how agents improve, and where the team focuses next.




Why Join Us




Analyze data from AI systems that are actively changing how people find careers, with direct impact on what the product becomes
See your findings drive real decisions, from agent tuning to product roadmap, not sit unread in a dashboard somewhere
Work alongside product, engineering, and research teammates who genuinely rely on data to move forward
Join a team that invests in data infrastructure and tooling, so you spend your time on analysis instead of plumbing




Responsibilities




Dig into product, user, and agent performance data to surface patterns that inform both day-to-day decisions and longer-term strategy
Build clean, well-designed reports and dashboards that make complex findings easy for product, engineering, and external partners to act on
Turn around data requests quickly and thoughtfully, translating fuzzy questions into clear analyses and digestible narratives
Work closely with your manager and cross-functional teammates to refine how agent quality is measured and tracked over time




Qualifications




Required




Recent grad or early-career professional with 0 to 2 years of experience in data analysis, analytics, or a related technical field
Strong communicator who can present trends and tradeoffs clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences
Working understanding of statistics, experimentation, and the underlying logic of AI and machine learning systems




Preferred




Internship or project experience in a data analyst, analytics, or research role at a tech or AI-focused organization
Track record of pulling practical, decision-ready insights out of messy, large-scale datasets
Hands-on skills in SQL, Python or R, data modeling fundamentals, and visualization tools like Looker, Tableau, or Mode

Title: Data & Reporting Analyst - SQL (Power BI and Tableau)
Company: Astra-North Infoteck Inc. ~ Conquering today’s challenges, achieving tomorrow’s vision!
Description:
Data & Reporting Analyst


Location: Toronto
Work Model: Hybrid (4 Days WFO)
Duration: 12 Months
Top 3 Required Skills


SQL

Power BI

Tableau


Job Description


We are seeking a skilled Data & Reporting Analyst to join the analytics team and support data-driven decision-making across the organization. The ideal candidate will have strong SQL expertise, hands-on experience with reporting tools such as Tableau and Power BI, and the ability to build scalable reporting and analytics solutions.


The candidate will work closely with business stakeholders, data engineers, and leadership teams to deliver actionable insights, optimize reporting processes, and improve data accessibility across the enterprise.


Key Responsibilities


SQL Development & Data Analysis


Write complex and optimized SQL queries to extract, transform, and analyze data from multiple sources.

Improve query performance and ensure efficient data retrieval.

Convert repetitive manual queries into reusable stored procedures, functions, and views.


Reporting & Dashboard Development


Design, develop, and maintain interactive dashboards and reports using Tableau and Power BI.

Build scalable and user-friendly reporting solutions for business stakeholders.

Support self-service analytics initiatives through structured reporting frameworks.


Semantic Layer & Data Modeling


Design semantic layers and logical data models to support consistent reporting and analytics.

Ensure data consistency, integrity, and usability across reporting assets.


Database Migration & Optimization


Support migration of stored procedures and reporting logic across database platforms.

Validate data accuracy and minimize disruptions during migration activities.


Stakeholder Collaboration


Gather business requirements and translate them into analytical and reporting solutions.

Partner with cross-functional teams including business users, leadership, and engineering teams.


Data Quality & Governance


Maintain data accuracy, consistency, and reliability across dashboards and reporting environments.

Support data governance and reporting standards.


Required Qualifications


Strong hands-on experience with advanced SQL and query optimization.

Experience building dashboards and reports using Tableau and/or Power BI.

Good understanding of semantic layer design and data modeling concepts.

Experience with stored procedures, functions, and reusable SQL frameworks.

Knowledge of relational databases and data warehousing principles.

Experience supporting database migration or reporting migration projects.

Strong communication, analytical, and documentation skills.


Preferred Qualifications


Experience with multiple database platforms such as SQL Server, Oracle, PostgreSQL, Snowflake, or BigQuery.

Familiarity with ETL/ELT processes and data governance practices.

Exposure to Python or R scripting.

Knowledge of version control and deployment best practices.

Experience with advanced analytics or statistical methods.

Title: Canada Residents - Survey Participants - Olds, Canada
Description:
Survey Participants - Canada

About Prolific

Prolific is not just another player in the AI space – we are building the biggest pool of quality human data in the world.

Over 35,000 AI developers, researchers, and organizations use Prolific to gather data from paid study participants with a wide variety of experiences, knowledge, and skills.

The role

We are looking for Study Participants from all professional backgrounds across the Canada to take part in academic and applied research studies for global businesses and educational faculties.

Whether you are just starting your career or are a seasoned expert, your perspective is valuable. We are actively seeking a diverse range of educational backgrounds, including:


No High School Diploma / High School Graduates
Trade School & Vocational Professionals (MBO)
Undergraduate & Master's Degree Holders (HBO/WO)
PhD Candidates & Doctoral Researchers


If you have the necessary experience, we'll send you a quick 10- to 15-minute screening to assess your suitability. If successful, you'll be invited to join Prolific as a participant, where you'll get paid to contribute to a wide range of projects.

Compensation: Researchers looking for your profile tend to pay up to $18/hr per study completed. While many tasks are short, you should be prepared for studies that require up to one hour of uninterrupted work.

What you'll be doing in the role

As a participant, you will contribute to high-quality research outcomes by responding thoughtfully to various tasks, including:


AI Training & Evaluation: helping refine Large Language Models by ranking responses or providing creative prompts.
Behavioral Experiments: participating in decision-making tasks and cognitive games.
User Research: providing feedback on new software, apps, or product concepts.
Academic Studies: assisting university researchers in fields like psychology, economics, and sociology.
Interviews: engaging in one-on-one deep dives regarding your professional expertise or lived experience.
Surveys & Questionnaires: sharing your opinions on current events, consumer habits, or workplace culture.


What you'll bring


Diverse Perspective: a track record of relevant experience, knowledge, or lived context (from entry-level roles to executive or academic positions).
Attention to Detail: the ability to remain focused and provide high-quality data for the duration of a study.
Technical Setup: a reliable, fast internet connection and access to a desktop or laptop computer.
Financial Readiness: a PayPal account to receive payments and a willingness to self-declare earnings as a self-employed participant.


Why Join Prolific?

Joining our platform as a Prolific participant will give you the chance to contribute to meaningful research used by universities, researchers, and global organizations. Once you pass our screening, you can join Prolific in just 15 minutes, and start enjoying competitive pay rates, flexible hours, and the ability to work from home.

We've built a unique platform that connects researchers and organizations with a global pool of participants, enabling the collection of high-quality, ethically sourced human behavioural data and feedback. This data helps researchers answer important questions, improve products and services, and advance knowledge across disciplines.

We believe that the best research outcomes come from diverse human perspectives and experiences. By providing this crucial human data infrastructure, Prolific is positioning itself at the forefront of the next wave of research innovation – one that reflects the breadth and the best of humanity.

Links & Privacy

Website

Youtube

Privacy Statement

By submitting your application, you agree that Prolific may collect your personal data for recruiting and global organisation planning. Prolific's Candidate Privacy Notice explains what personal information Prolific may process, where Prolific may process your personal information, its purposes for processing your personal information, and the rights you can exercise over Prolific's use of your personal information.

Joining our platform as a Prolific participant will give you the chance to contribute to meaningful research used by universities, researchers, and global organizations. Once you pass our screening, you can join Prolific in just 15 minutes, and start enjoying competitive pay rates, flexible hours, and the ability to work from home.

We've built a unique platform that connects researchers and organizations with a global pool of participants, enabling the collection of high-quality, ethically sourced human behavioural data and feedback. This data helps researchers answer important questions, improve products and services, and advance knowledge across disciplines.

We believe that the best research outcomes come from diverse human perspectives and experiences. By providing this crucial human data infrastructure, Prolific is positioning itself at the forefront of the next wave of research innovation – one that reflects the breadth and the best of humanity.

Website

Youtube

Privacy Statement

By submitting your application, you agree that Prolific may collect your personal data for recruiting and global organisation planning. Prolific's Candidate Privacy Notice explains what personal information Prolific may process, where Prolific may process your personal information, its purposes for processing your personal information, and the rights you can exercise over Prolific's use of your personal information.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst

Reports To: Data Science & Analytics Manager

Location: Montreal, QC (Hybrid)




About Us

Broadsign is a growing software company with a mission to make buying, selling, and delivering out-of-home media easier than ever. Our software is operated by some of the most successful out-of-home businesses and powers impactful, compelling campaigns seen across the world.




About the Role

As a Data Analyst at Broadsign, you will join our Data Science & Analytics team to solve real-world problems and generate significant business impact. You will play a pivotal role in supporting our data-driven product portfolio, working closely with commercial and engineering teams to ensure our clients succeed in the rapidly growing Digital Out of Home (OOH) media channel.




What to Expect

Data Operations & Product Performance: You will support daily and monthly data operations while helping to build monitoring and QA processes. This includes learning the methodologies behind our data products and ensuring high performance through detailed reporting.
Commercial Support & Problem Solving: Working with commercial team members, you will diagnose and address client-facing data issues. You will be a key collaborator for engineers, data scientists, and business leaders to drive data-driven decision-making.
Innovation & Independent Analysis: You will take ownership of independent coding and analysis projects to explore new methodologies. We expect you to contribute creative ideas for new innovations that will help shape our future product roadmap.




What you need to perform in this job

Technical Proficiency: Strong experience with SQL and business intelligence tools like Looker or Tableau. You should be comfortable coding in Python and have experience with Snowflake and AWS platforms. Experience with software engineering best practices (CI/CD) and GIS data and systems is a plus, as is experience with web languages/standards.
Analytical Mindset: Formal training in data science, including statistical analysis, visualization, and machine learning. Strong problem-solving and critical-thinking skills are critical for this role, as you will be asked to tackle problems that have not been encountered before.
Soft Skills: A natural curiosity to learn how things work and quickly embrace new technologies and tools. You must be able to communicate clearly on technical matters and be able to work effectively within a team while taking independent ownership of your tasks.




What we bring to the table

Comprehensive Benefits: Complete company insurance plan (health, dental, vision, travel) effective from day one, 100% employer-paid. This includes a $500 annual Health Care Savings Account (HCSA) and unlimited access to the Telus Health virtual platform.
Wellness & Fitness: A $500 annual Wellness fund to support your mental/physical health and office-related expenses.
Paid Time Off: 3 weeks vacation, 5 floater days, 5 sick/personal days, and 2 volunteer days.
Retirement Savings: Group RRSP with 50% employer matching up to 4% of your salary.
Financial Perks: Transportation reimbursement for travel to a Broadsign office.
Family Support: Parental leave salary supplement, including a 23-week maternity top-up.
Growth Opportunities: A dedicated yearly budget for training and professional development.




At Broadsign, we value the varied social identities that make up our community. We recognize talent comes in different forms and encourage applications that reflect different backgrounds and experiences. Our promise is to be an inclusive employer and partner, open to learning, with thoughtful strategies and practices that amplify the different voices of our industry.




Knowledge of French is required for positions permanently located in Quebec so incumbents can communicate with their colleagues and partners in Quebec as necessary. French-language training is offered to all incumbents in permanent positions in Quebec who do not have a good knowledge of French. Fluent English is required for this position in order to communicate with colleagues, clients and partners (or suppliers) located outside Quebec and to understand the technical and scientific documentation used in our industry.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
LayerZero

The Future is Omnichain.

Founded in 2021, LayerZero’s vision is to create a community of cross-chain developers, building dApps that are no longer constrained by individual blockchain capabilities. With LayerZero's simple, generic messaging protocol, builders will develop cross-chain dApps designed to unify the power of individual blockchains.

We are funded by the best investors in the world including:

a16z, Sequoia, PayPal, Binance Ventures, Coinbase Ventures, Uniswap Labs, Circle Ventures, Delphi Digital, and many more.

About The Role

As we continue to grow at LayerZero, we're faced with an exciting set of challenges: more data, more developers, more teams, more projects, and more opportunities for innovation. To tackle these problems head-on, we're expanding our data team with a dedicated Data Analyst.

As a Data Analyst at LayerZero, you'll be at the forefront of shaping a rich data foundation for a company that's making a real impact in the web3 space. You'll work closely with teams and leaders to uncover insights, drive decision-making, and fuel our next-generation products and services.

What You'll Do

As a key member of the data team at LayerZero, you'll be diving headfirst into the world of crypto data - exploring on-chain wallets and contracts, block and transaction data, insights from in-house systems, and third-party intelligence. Your mission will be to combine these diverse datasets into rich, actionable data products for a broad group of stakeholders.


Leveraging and expanding our ever-growing Kimball dimensional model.
Writing SQL to create and expand insights in our in-house reporting solutions.
Collaborating with stakeholders across the organization to conduct ad-hoc explorations and analytics - from engineering questions to help understand production issues to product insights that illuminate the broader crypto landscape.
Be a key owner of data quality, building out insights that serve the data team itself to help guide our data efforts towards cleaner and richer data.
Compose pipelines by writing SQL code to clean, combine, refine, and aggregate data into the insights the organization needs.
Collaborating on new datasets to ingest into our Snowflake data warehouse, working closely with data engineers on your team.
Not afraid of pushing code that supports tens of billions of dollars in daily transaction volume.


Who You Are

We are looking for someone with previous data analyst experience. Likely with a bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Physics or related field, but we also consider and highly value equivalent practical experience.

Required


Strong SQL knowledge and experience
Proven track record in data modeling, statistics, and analytics
Experience working with a broad range of stakeholders
Strong convictions weakly held.
You enjoy working in a fast-paced start-up environment, where you'll need to take initiative and solve problems independently.
You understand the importance of data quality through incremental data development.


Nice to have


Experience with general programming, we use Python and Go on the data stack
Experience with Snowflake
Experience building DAG based data pipelines
Experience with streaming real-time data pipelines
Previous experience with blockchain technologies, smart contracts, and decentralized finance
Experience with Kimball dimensional modeling
Working on a mid-to-large scale data stacks


Equal Opportunity Employer

LayerZero Labs is committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace. LayerZero Labs is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, national origin, religion, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, marital status, protected veteran status, disability, age, or any other legally protected status.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Department: Technology

Employment Type: Permanent

Location: Greater Montreal Metropolitan Area, Canada

Reporting To: Software Engineering Manager

Description

Le Data Analyst sera responsable de la conception, de la création et de la maintenance de la couche analytique ainsi que des rapports destinés aux clients, reposant sur la plateforme Compliance Pro.

Au sein de l’équipe Reporting, ce rôle consiste à traduire des exigences complexes en matière de conformité et de sécurité en tableaux de bord robustes et en rapports tabulaires qui répondent aux besoins des utilisateurs finaux. Le Data Analyst sera responsable de la livraison de bout en bout : modélisation des données, création et mise à jour des tableaux de bord et des widgets, définition des meilleures représentations des données, ainsi que garantie de l’exactitude, de la performance et de la cohérence des rapports avec les données affichées dans CPro.

Ce rôle est fortement orienté client : une grande partie du travail consiste à créer et mettre à jour des rapports personnalisés pour des opérateurs spécifiques, à résoudre des problèmes en production et à s’assurer que les rapports critiques pour l’entreprise sont fiables et à jour.

Construire des solutions innovantes ; permettre des lieux de travail plus sûrs pour tous.

Nous allons créer un monde du travail plus sûr, en développant des logiciels pour soutenir un réseau mondial d'acheteurs, de fournisseurs et de partenaires responsables.

Aujourd'hui, chez Veriforce, nous soulageons plus de 50 000 organisations à travers le monde des contraintes de conformité, en les aidant à protéger leurs employés, leurs activités et la planète.

Nous veillons à ce que notre réseau de clients, de fournisseurs et de sous-traitants respecte les normes les plus importantes, qu'il s'agisse de santé, de sécurité, de développement durable ou de comportement éthique, en élaborant les meilleures solutions du marché.

La technologie que nous construisons aujourd'hui créera un avenir meilleur.

{Insérer une déclaration d'impact tirée du dossier d'appel d'offres.}

Anglais De Niveau Avancé

Cette exigence est requise puisque dans le cadre de cette fonction, vous aurez à intervenir auprès des clients, partenaires et/ou nos filiales internationaux sur une base récurrente tant à l’oral qu’à l’écrit.

Dans le présent document, le genre masculin est utilisé dans le seul but d'alléger le texte

Qu'est-ce Que Cela Signifie Au Jour Le Jour


Créer et améliorer des tableaux de bord et des rapports spécifiques aux clients
Assurer la mise en valeur des données (« data storytelling ») afin que celles-ci soient pertinentes et exploitables pour la prise de décision
Veiller à ce que les rapports Sisense reflètent fidèlement les données de l’application en production, tant en termes de valeurs que de mise en forme
Diagnostiquer et résoudre les incidents en production
Collaborer étroitement avec les Product Owners, les équipes QA, les parties prenantes internes et les équipes en contact avec les clients afin d’affiner les critères d’acceptation, clarifier la logique des données et valider les rapports nouveaux ou mis à jour
Mettre en place et maintenir des standards de reporting cohérents à travers l’environnement Sisense et les produits de reporting
Collaborer avec les équipes d’ingénierie et de données pour identifier et modéliser de nouvelles sources de données lorsque nécessaire
Maintenir une documentation technique claire afin de faciliter la maintenance et l’intégration des nouveaux collaborateurs
Participer aux rituels agiles, utiliser Jira pour gérer les tâches et fournir des estimations, des mises à jour d’avancement et des livrables clairs pour le QAT


Ce Dont Vous Aurez Besoin Pour Réussir


Expérience pratique dans le développement de solutions analytiques avec Sisense (ou une plateforme BI similaire), incluant la création de tableaux de bord complexes et de rapports tabulaires avec filtres, widgets et champs personnalisés
Solides compétences en SQL et expérience en modélisation de données pour le reporting (jointures, agrégations, champs calculés, conversions de types de données, optimisation des performances)
Expérience démontrée dans la gestion de rapports critiques en environnement de production, incluant :
L’analyse des écarts entre les systèmes sources et les données produites
La correction des problèmes de données et de mise en forme
La maintenance de rapports opérationnels à fort enjeu
Capacité à interpréter des spécifications métiers détaillées et à les traduire en solutions de reporting précises
Excellentes compétences en « data storytelling » et solide compréhension des bonnes pratiques en reporting
Expérience dans l’analyse et l’amélioration de rapports Sisense existants
À l’aise pour collaborer étroitement avec les Product Owners, le QAT et les équipes en contact avec les clients, notamment pour valider le comportement des tableaux de bord, des filtres et des résultats
Grande attention aux détails et souci de la qualité
Excellentes capacités d’analyse et de résolution de problèmes, avec aptitude à identifier des enjeux à travers plusieurs couches de données


Ce Que Vous Obtiendrez En Retour

Nous voulons que vous puissiez donner le meilleur de vous-même. Nous mettons l'accent sur les moyens de soutenir notre équipe pour qu'elle donne le meilleur d'elle-même. Voici quelques-uns des avantages que nous offrons :

Santé et bien-être personnel / Avantages

>🧠 Soutien intégré à la santé mentale et au bien-être

>🌴 Vacances - à partir de 3 semaines

>💟 Journées du bien-être et journée annuelle de dons - une occasion supplémentaire de se rendre service ou de rendre service à la communauté

>🩺 Couverture médicale et dentaire complète

>😴 Fin de l'année, fermeture de l'entreprise pour vous permettre de vous détendre et de vous ressourcer

Planification future

>🏫 Licence d'apprentissage LinkedIn pour la mise à niveau et le développement

Vous êtes intéressé(e) mais ne pensez pas répondre à toutes les exigences ?

Notre équipe de recrutement évalue et examine toutes les candidatures en fonction du poste et des besoins de l'entreprise. Nous croyons que les gens ont des compétences transférables et non techniques et nous voulons que vous sachiez que nous prenons en considération les cas où une personne ne répond pas à tous les critères, mais possède néanmoins les aptitudes et les capacités nécessaires. Notre priorité est de veiller à ce que les candidats soient en mesure de réussir. Nous prendrons une décision finale en déterminant si nous pouvons offrir le soutien nécessaire pour améliorer les compétences ou fournir le soutien au développement dont vous avez besoin pour tirer le meilleur parti de cette opportunité avec nous !

Apportez votre entièreté au travail.

Veriforce est fière d'être un employeur qui respecte l'égalité des chances. Nous nous engageons à ce qu'aucun candidat ne soit discriminé en raison de son identité et de son expression sexuelles, de sa race, de son handicap, de son ethnicité, de son orientation sexuelle, de son âge, de sa couleur, de sa région, de sa croyance, de son origine nationale ou de son sexe. Nous sommes déterminés à développer une équipe diversifiée tout en continuant à créer un environnement inclusif où chacun se sent en sécurité et a la possibilité d'être lui-même.

Ce à quoi vous pouvez vous attendre si vous postulez :


Une réponse à votre candidature dans les 15 jours ouvrables
Un processus d'entretien comprenant
Un appel initial de découverte avec le recruteur
Un premier entretien via Microsoft Teams
Un entretien supplémentaire (probablement en face à face) avec les parties prenantes avec lesquelles vous travaillerez en étroite collaboration dans le cadre de votre fonction.

Nous tenons à ce que notre processus de recrutement vous permette de donner le meilleur de vous-même. Si vous souhaitez que nous procédions à des ajustements, n'hésitez pas à nous en faire part.
