Title: Business Data Analyst - Sales & Marketing
Description:
Job Description

Business Data Analyst – Sales & Marketing

About Toyota Financial Services

Toyota Financial Services (TFS) provides retail, leasing and dealer financing services to Toyota and Lexus dealerships and customers across Canada. TFS is a member of Toyota Financial Services Corporation (TFSC), a wholly owned subsidiary of Toyota Motor Corporation in Japan with the Canadian operation headquarters in Markham, Ontario

What Sets Us Apart?

At Toyota Financial Services (TFS), you will help create best in class customer and dealer experiences in a collaborative and innovative environment. TFS is a key part of the Toyota family, a global company known for its technological leadership, high quality products, continuous improvement, and environmental responsibility.

TFS currently has an exciting permanent opportunity as a Business Data Analyst – Sales & Marketing in the Business Analytics Department. This role reports to the Business Analytics Supervisor and resides in our Markham Head Office.

This role will provide dedicated analytics support to our Sales & Marketing division, helping to drive data-driven decisions that improve sales performance, marketing campaign effectiveness, customer engagement, and dealer performance. The successful candidate will collaborate closely with Sales & Marketing leaders and teams, to deliver timely insights and advanced reporting solutions and ensure alignment with enterprise data standards and strategy.

What You’ll Be Doing


Sales & Marketing Performance Analysis
Manage the full spectrum of marketing analytics, including campaign tracking, performance dashboards, and funnel analysis.
Lead efforts to maintain a comprehensive and accurate view of marketing performance across demand generation segments and channels, focusing on identifying actionable or predictive insights to optimize campaigns and achieve growth targets.
Design and analyze A/B tests to support data-driven experimentation and optimization.
Customer & Dealer Insights
Leverage data to uncover trends in customer behavior, dealer performance, product penetration, and regional sales trends.
Translate these insights into recommendations for targeted marketing strategies, improved customer retention, and dealer network development.
Dashboard & Reporting Development
Design, build, and maintain advanced Tableau dashboards and reports for the Sales & Marketing department, using data from the Enterprise Data Platform (Snowflake) as the single source of truth. Example outputs include campaign ROI dashboards, sales and market share analyses, sales performance reports, and dealer scorecards to support daily decision-making by Sales & Marketing leaders.
Ensure that reports are visually clear, accurate, and aligned with corporate data definitions and KPIs.
Present clear, actionable performance insights to leadership through compelling data storytelling.
Ad-Hoc Data Support
Serve as the go-to analytics resource for the Sales & Marketing team.
Respond promptly to ad-hoc requests for data or analysis (e.g., identifying which customer segments responded best to a recent promotion, or providing a quick sales forecast for a new product launch).
Deliver timely, well-explained insights to support business decisions, product launches, promotions, and campaign adjustments.
Cross-Functional Collaboration
Collaborate with the central Business Analytics team and other functions as needed.
Work under the mentorship of the Senior Data & Insights Analyst to incorporate advanced models or new data sources for complex analyses (for example, feeding Sales & Marketing requirements into enterprise predictive models for customer lifetime value or sales forecasting).
Ensure that all analyses and metrics for Sales & Marketing are consistent with enterprise-wide standards.
Coordinate with Finance (for alignment on incentive budgets and profitability targets) and with other departments (e.g. Risk or Business Operations) when Sales & Marketing data intersects with broader company initiatives.
Training & Data Enablement
Help foster a data-driven culture across the organization.
Help leaders and colleagues in Sales & Marketing understand and utilize the available reports and data tools.
Train team members to interpret metrics and leverage self-service analytics (e.g., basic Tableau functions or SQL queries) for their daily management needs.
Promote a culture of continuous improvement and data-driven decision-making in Sales & Marketing teams by making data accessible and understandable.
Cross‑Functional Coverage & Business Continuity
Provide backup analytical support and coverage for Business Operations and other departments as needed, ensuring continuity of reporting, insights, and decision support during peak periods, absences, or shifting priorities.


What You’ll Bring


Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business, Data Analytics, or a related field; strong blend of business acumen and analytical training.
3-5 years of experience in business analytics, BI, or data analysis roles, supporting Sales, Marketing, Pricing, or customer analytics functions.
Experience in the financial services or captive finance industry (especially automotive finance) is preferred.
Proven track record of exceptional problem-solving skills and the ability to synthesize complex data into actionable insights that inform business decisions, including analysis of marketing campaigns, sales performance, pricing effectiveness, and customer trends.
Strong SQL proficiency and experience working in modern data warehouse environments (e.g., Snowflake or similar).
Advanced Excel skills for analysis and ad‑hoc reporting.
Hands‑on experience building reports and interactive dashboards using Tableau (preferred), with solid understanding of data visualization best practices and data storytelling.
Strong understanding of sales and marketing concepts and KPIs (e.g., sales, market share, retention, incentives, ROI), with the ability to link insights to revenue growth and market share.
Excellent communication skills with experience working cross‑functionally (Finance, Risk, IT, Credit, Compliance), presenting insights clearly to both technical and non‑technical stakeholders in fast‑paced environments.
Proactive, team‑oriented problem solver capable of managing multiple priorities, working independently when needed, and continuously learning new tools and analytical techniques.


What We’ll Bring


A work environment built on teamwork, flexibility, and respect
Competitive salary including bonus
Health and dental benefits effective immediately
Company pension plan with matched contribution
Associate vehicle discount program
Professional growth and development programs to help advance your career, as well as tuition reimbursement
Fitness reimbursement
Competitive time off benefits
Great company culture-Respect for people and continuous improvement is at our core
Hybrid work environment (based on role requirements)


What You Should Know

Our success begins and ends with our people. We embrace diverse perspectives and value unique human experiences. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer that celebrates the diversity of the communities where we live and do business. Applicants for our positions are considered without regard to race, ethnicity, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, disability, religion, or any other characteristics protected by law.

Title: Data Quality Analyst
Description:
Job Description

What is the opportunity?

In the increasingly complex world of financial services, accurate and complete data is critical for risk management and regulatory compliance. Ensuring data integrity begins with an understanding of financial services data, data quality controls and identifying and resolving data issues.

As a Data Quality Analyst focusing on Data Quality with the Shared Data Management Office (SDMO), you will analyze the critical data, build and deploy data quality checks for business partners across the organization including Finance, Group Risk Management, Personal & Commercial Banking, Capital Markets, and Wealth Management.

What will you do?


Perform deep data analysis to identify trends and patterns in the data
Design controls to detect data quality issues
Work using Agile methodology to automate the controls using various tools/technologies
Profile business, financial and reporting data through use of cutting edge data/analytic tools.
Collaborate with various stakeholders to understand business needs, and create user stories and business requirements


What do you need to succeed?

Must-have


2+ years of professional work experience, with 2 years of analysis experience working in a data-rich industry (such as Banking, Insurance, Capital Markets, Retail etc.)
Education in Science, Engineering, Business, Finance or any other analytical discipline or equivalent work experience
Intermediate to advanced knowledge of data analysis methods and the ability to analyze and resolve complex data related issues.
SQL skills (or other data analysis tools/packages) to extract data and perform various types of functions/operations to gain insight
Excellent communication - strong verbal, written and presentation skills
Attention to detail and ability to work independently and within a team


Nice-to-have


Understanding of Data Quality concepts and processes
Understanding of basic Banking/Finance terms and processes
Understanding of data flows and processing
Experience using IBM’s DataStage or Information Analyzer software


If your specific skills don't necessarily align with the description and you feel your background, experience and passion are close to what we're looking for we would love for you to apply.

What’s in it for you?

We thrive on the challenge to be our best, progressive thinking to keep growing, and working together to deliver trusted advice to help our clients thrive and communities prosper. We care about each other, reaching our potential, making a difference to our communities, and achieving success that is mutual.


A comprehensive Total Rewards Program including bonuses and flexible benefits and competitive compensation
Leaders who support your development through coaching and managing opportunities
Work in a dynamic, collaborative, progressive, and high-performing team
Opportunities to do challenging work


Job Skills

Critical Thinking, Data Administration, Database Structures, Data Modeling, Data Movement, Detail-Oriented, Group Problem Solving, Information Capture, Standard Operating Procedure (SOP), Teamwork

Title: PepsiCo Canada: Fall 2026 Data Science Co-op
Description:
Overview

PepsiCo Canada: Fall 2026 Data Science Co-op

Co-op Work Term Duration: September to December 2026

Start Date: September 8, 2026

Job Location: Mississauga Headquarters

Compensation: $25 to $37.50 per hour

Overview:

At PepsiCo Canada, we have great brands, great distribution systems and great quality products, all of which are led by great people! To ensure we stay on the path of delivering great results, it is important that we continue to develop a deep bench of talented leaders. Our ability to grow year after year is driven by our ability to attract, develop, and retain world class people who will thrive in the PepsiCo environment.

Our people are our greatest asset. By supporting their ability to work effectively together and providing them with the tools they need to succeed, we are ensuring that PepsiCo Canada is the kind of company where talented people of all backgrounds want to work. The PepsiCo Canada co-op program will provide candidates with a unique opportunity to build their experience and leadership skills.

Are you excited about analytics and passionate about problem solving? Then come join our Data Science team at PepsiCo Foods Canada! We are focused on accelerating consumer demand, strengthening customer partnership, and driving end to end productivity through data science and advanced analytics. We work with Shopper Insights, Shopper Marketing, Category Leadership, Supply Chain, and IT resources to unlock opportunities. Being embedded in the business and linked to the top strategic priorities allows us to build best-in-class capabilities, develop deep functional expertise, and offer focused products in an agile responsive manner.

Help us solve new and exciting business problems with data analysis, advanced analytics, and a love of problem solving. Success in this role will require exceptional data analysis skills, an analytical mindset, intellectual curiosity, and strong appreciation for delivering business results. Strong communication and collaboration skills are required.

Responsibilities


Translate Business needs into technical requirements
Collaborating with business stakeholders and technical teams to deliver a variety of projects
Deliver advanced analytics and data science projects from end-to-end
Effectively communicating insights to project stakeholders
Complete proof of concepts
Support the adoption of solutions into the business process


Qualifications


Working towards bachelor’s degree in Data/Computer Science, Mathematics/Statistics, an engineering discipline, or equivalent.
Familiarity with statistical modeling, machine learning and/or deep learning modeling
Knowledge of statistical analysis tools like R or Python
Knowledge of Databases and SQL programming skills
Ability to translate business and technical requirements into non-technical terms
Strong communication and collaboration skills
Analytical and conceptual thinking skills
Demonstrated ability to thrive in fast paced environment
Ability to work independently with minimal supervision


The Company

PepsiCo is making a difference on a global scale, and so can you! Competing in a dynamic, fast-paced industry, our ability to grow year after year is driven by our ability to attract, develop, and retain world class people who will thrive at PepsiCo. Follow the link below to find out more about our culture and commitment to Performance with Purpose. We believe that delivering value for our consumers and customers, protecting our environment, sourcing with integrity, and investing in our employees are not simply good things to do, but that these actions fuel our returns and position PepsiCo for long-term, sustainable growth.

Grow. Belong. Smile.

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Job Summary

The Business Intelligence (BI) Analyst for the Specialty Reporting Services team will turn data into information, information into insight and insight into business decisions. The team member will have the ability to develop complex and appealing visualizations using a variety of BI Tools based on tightly established client turnaround times.

Duties And Responsibilities


Implementation of new reporting processes and procedures as required such as moving to automated reporting methods
Analyze reports for trends and variances across the programs and communicate findings
Design and prepare monthly, bimonthly or quarterly business reports including metrics/volumes and significant case summaries to clients using a variety of tools, including; Excel, Power BI, SSRS, PowerPoint
Convert data to usable information to drive growth, individual contract KPI’s and increased productivity
Act as the subject matter expert for specific Bayshore Specialty Rx Ltd data sets and act as a liaison with internal management and clients as required
Test and debug reporting and analysis using standard tools including but not limited to Excel,


Access, CRM and other database management tools/systems


Interpret data and develop actionable recommendations for business leaders
Identify, analyze, and interpret trends or patterns in complex data sets
Locate and define new process improvement opportunities
Ability to respond to customer inquiries and reporting needs efficiently as well as adapt to rapidly evolving client requirements
Knowledge of SQL queries to acquire data from primary and secondary data sources is desirable
Formulating and communicating meaningful and actionable process plans
Participate in ongoing internal and/or external continuing education activities
Adhere to Bayshore Policies and Procedure
Participate in quality activities and continuous improvement initiatives in keeping with the company’s Quality Management System
Participates in proactive Health & Safety activities while performing all duties. Is responsible to notify immediate Supervisor of any health and safety risks or concerns. Responsible for completing Accident Reports for all direct reports who injure themselves on the job, within 24 hours of the incident.
Maintains confidentiality of client and corporate information and discusses same only with appropriate Bayshore personnel


Qualifications

EDUCATION


Bachelor’s degree in information technology, statistics or data analysis


Experience


Expert level Power BI, Excel, and Dashboarding Skills
Minimum 2 years of experience working with complex data sets
Minimum 2 years of experience in analyzing and presenting results to stakeholders and clients

Title: Business Data Analyst - Artificial Intelligence (AI/ML)
Company: Astra-North Infoteck Inc. ~ Conquering today’s challenges, achieving tomorrow’s vision!
Description:
Job Title: Senior Business Analyst – Artificial Intelligence (AIML)
Location: Toronto, ON
Work Style: Hybrid (2 days onsite preferred)

Skills: Business Analysis, Digital : Machine Learning, Digital : Artificial Intelligence(AI)
Experience: 8-10 Years

We are seeking a highly experienced AI-focused Business Analyst to drive the identification, analysis, and implementation of Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning solutions across the organization. This role bridges the gap between business objectives and data science/engineering teams, ensuring AI initiatives deliver measurable business value, ethical compliance, and scalability.

Key Responsibilities
1. Business Strategy & AI Opportunity Identification
Identify and evaluate AI/ML use cases aligned with business goals (e.g., predictive analytics, NLP, computer vision).
Collaborate with leadership to define AI strategy and roadmap.
Conduct feasibility analysis including cost-benefit, ROI, and risk assessment.
Recommend AI-driven solutions to improve efficiency, decision-making, and customer experience.

2. Requirements Engineering for AI Solutions
Elicit and document AI-specific functional and non-functional requirements.
Translate business problems into data science problems and hypotheses.
Define:
Model inputs/outputs
Training data requirements
Performance metrics (accuracy, precision, recall, etc.)
Develop user stories, epics, and AI use case documentation.

3. Data Analysis & Management
Analyze data sources to assess:
Data availability, quality, and readiness
Data governance and compliance requirements
Collaborate with data engineers to define:
Data pipelines
Data cleaning and transformation needs
Support creation of data dictionaries and metadata documentation.

4. Collaboration with AI/ML Teams
Work closely with:
Data Scientists
Machine Learning Engineers
Data Engineers
Facilitate understanding of business context for model development.
Ensure models align with business needs and operational constraints.
Participate in model evaluation, validation, and performance monitoring discussions.

5. AI Model Lifecycle Support
Support end-to-end lifecycle:
Problem definition
Model development
Testing & validation
Deployment & monitoring
Define acceptance criteria for AI models.
Ensure continuous improvement using feedback loops and retraining strategies.

6. AI Governance, Ethics & Compliance
Ensure AI solutions comply with:
Data privacy regulations (GDPR, CCPA)
Ethical AI principles (fairness, bias mitigation, transparency)
Assist in defining model explainability and auditability requirements.
Support risk management for AI-based decisions.

7. Process Integration & Automation
Integrate AI models into business workflows and applications.
Define target operating models (TOM) for AI adoption.
Work with product

Title: Analyst, Data Sourcing
Description:
Job Description

WHAT IS THE OPPORTUNITY?


The SOR/APP declaration process as laid out in the data sourcing guideline is a structured and repeatable approach designed to efficiently identify and manage Systems of Record (SORs) and Authorized Provisioning Points (APPs) in accordance with the Enterprise Information Management Risk Policy
The Analyst, Data Sourcing is responsible for assisting with the SOR/APP declaration process to identify and declare SORs/APPs for fully managed domains and providing high level advisory support to self-managed domains to assist them in completing the end-to-end SOR/APP declaration process.
Role will maintain relationships with various business & IT stakeholders to understand & communicate details of the SOR/APP declaration process


What Will You Do


Assisting SOR/APP Declaration Process:
Analyze data lineage to identify and validate Systems of Record (SOR) and Authorized Point of Publication (APP) for Critical Data Elements (CDEs).
Collaborate with lineage analysts, stakeholders, and committees to review, present, and finalize declarations.
Document and maintain evidence of approvals in Collibra and SharePoint, ensuring compliance with RBC standards.
Provide Advisory Support:
Guide domains through SOR/APP self-management, including enablement training and process clarification.
Address ad-hoc queries and escalate complex issues as needed.
Foster Cross-Functional Relationships:
Engage with Data Management Offices, Control Owners, and System Owners across Lines of Business (LOBs) to align on data sourcing requirements.
Present Data Sourcing Guidelines and process details to stakeholders to ensure consistent adoption.
Drive Process Optimization:
Identify opportunities to streamline SOR/APP declarations by leveraging existing artifacts and reducing duplication.
Propose enhancements to improve efficiency, consistency, and documentation practices.
Support Team & Delivery:
Assist with ad-hoc requests and contribute to broader Data Sourcing team initiatives.
Develop expertise to become a trusted resource, advancing team capabilities and deliverables.

WHAT DO YOU NEED TO SUCCEED?

Must have:


Prior experience in identifying and managing SORs/APPs or equivalent experience working with data.
Experience handling and deriving information from multiple sources of data, and large data sets.
Strong written and verbal communication skills coupled with the ability to communicate effectively with both business and technology staff at different levels of seniority.
Understanding of retail and commercial banking policies, processes and procedures.
Strong ability to utilize the Microsoft Suite of tools (Outlook, PowerPoint, Word, Excel, Visio)


Nice-to-have:


Familiarity with data governance and metadata management tools such as Collibra.
Familiarity with agile methodology and agility tools such as Jira.


WHAT’S IN IT FOR YOU?

We thrive on the challenge to be our best, progressive thinking to keep growing, and working together to deliver trusted advice to help our clients thrive and communities prosper. We care about each other, reaching our potential, making a difference to our communities, and achieving success that is mutual.


A comprehensive Total Rewards Program including bonuses and flexible benefits, competitive compensation, commissions, and stock where applicable
Leaders who support your development through coaching and managing opportunities
Ability to make a difference and lasting impact
Work in a dynamic, collaborative, progressive, and high-performing team
A world-class training program in financial services
Flexible work/life balance options
Opportunities to do challenging work


Job Skills

Critical Thinking, Data Administration, Database Structures, Data Modeling, Data Movement, Detail-Oriented, Group Problem Solving, Information Capture, Standard Operating Procedure (SOP), Teamwork

Title: Data Solutions Analyst
Description:
About Citylitics Inc.

Citylitics delivers predictive intelligence on where, how, and why infrastructure investments will occur across North America.

Infrastructure includes the foundational systems that shape our quality of life: water, transportation, transit, roads and highways, broadband, and power. It represents a multi-trillion-dollar market and a 20+ year megatrend in North America.

Citylitics transforms millions of documents across more than 40,000 cities, utilities, and public agencies into real-time market signals and high-value sales intelligence. Leading infrastructure vendors, engineering firms, and construction companies use the platform to identify and pre-position for major infrastructure opportunities earlier, helping them build relationships, shape project priorities, and influence scope before projects move to formal procurement.

Citylitics combines a network of AI agents, fine-tuned models, a proprietary infrastructure signals graph, and daily human-in-the-loop quality assurance workflows to deliver highly nuanced and reliable sales intelligence. The platform integrates seamlessly with CRMs, ERPs, and data lakes, embedding actionable insights directly into sales workflows so teams can prioritize the right pursuits and build stronger pipelines.

Our mission is to improve how infrastructure investments get made so critical needs can be addressed earlier and communities benefit from stronger, more resilient infrastructure.

Join Citylitics to work in a meaningful industry that will matter for decades to come while helping grow a company at the forefront of applied AI. We operate with a Results-Driven Mentality and a commitment to Raise The Bar, in a collaborative environment where Teamwork Makes the Dream Work as we tackle big challenges and celebrate wins together.

Who Are We Looking For?

Citylitics is looking for a Data Solutions Analyst to support our sales process by helping prospects understand how Citylitics' proprietary data, market signals, platform workflows, integrations, and purpose-built AI can help them pre-position earlier for future infrastructure projects, unlock business development capacity, and strengthen pricing workflows.

You will partner with Account Executives during active sales cycles to support sales intelligence workshops, answer data and methodology questions, scope and present data integration plans, configure trial accounts, train trial users, manage demo and trial environments, and create data-backed collateral.

You will also support data roadmap discovery and own proof-of-concepts for new data areas that align with customer needs, prospect requests, and future infrastructure verticals.

This role is ideal for someone who is analytical, customer-facing, commercially curious, and comfortable translating data into practical business value. You do not need to be a quota-carrying seller, but you should be comfortable working directly with prospects and customers during the sales process.

This is a hybrid role based out of Citylitics' Toronto office and requires 3+ days in-office per week.

What Will You Accomplish? / How You Will Make An Immediate Impact:

Support Sales Intelligence Workshops and Technical Discovery

You will support Account Executives with the data and integration expertise needed throughout the sales process.

Responsibilities include:


Support AEs with data and integration expertise during sales intelligence workshops and pre-implementation syncs
Answer prospect questions about data coverage, signal types, methodology, platform configuration, workflows, and use-case fit to strengthen data trust
Advise prospects on relevant signals, indicators, data packages, product add-ons, filters, dashboards, feeds, alerts, and data methodology
Conduct deeper data and technical discovery during sales intelligence workshops to understand prospective customers' systems, data environments, workflows, and integration needs
Support AE-owned demos by ensuring demo environments, data feeds configuration, methodology explanations, and supporting materials are accurate, relevant, and ready for prospect conversations


Create Sales and Marketing Collateral that Strengthen Data Trust

You will create data-backed materials that support Citylitics' sales and marketing efforts while helping prospects understand the quality, relevance, and differentiation of Citylitics' data.

Responsibilities include:


Create market analytics, sample data, data stories, and proof points that support sales opportunities, executive conversations, trials, and marketing campaigns
Help quantify opportunity volume, account coverage, signal activity, and potential pipeline relevance for prospects
Support outbound and inbound marketing campaigns with relevant sample data, market examples, data stories, and other collateral needs
Build and maintain reusable assets, including sample reports, vertical-specific examples, sales intelligence workshop materials, trial templates, and AI prompts
Help prospects understand how Citylitics identifies nuanced signals often missed by generic search tools, broad data providers, bid platforms, or general AI tools
Create and maintain methodology collateral, FAQs, slides, talk tracks, and examples that support sales intelligence workshops, trials, and customer conversations


Configure Trials and Manage Demo Environments

You will help prospects experience a relevant, well-configured version of Citylitics during demos and trials, while ensuring demo environments are ready for AE-owned demos.

Responsibilities include:


Set up and configure trial accounts based on prospect needs, target markets, user roles, geographies, use cases, and signal preferences
Manage demo environments to ensure they remain accurate, current, and ready for AE-owned demos
Conduct 1:1 training calls to help trial users navigate the platform, interpret signals, use dashboards, and apply insights to their workflow
Monitor trial activity and support AEs in interpreting user engagement, adoption, feedback, workflow fit, and demonstrated value
Identify opportunities to improve trial configuration, demo environment readiness, training, and support processes


Scope Data Integration and Implementation Plans

You will help prospects understand how Citylitics can fit into their existing GTM systems, data environments, and workflows.

Responsibilities include:


Scope and present implementation and data integration plans based on customer needs, system requirements, data availability, workflow complexity, and existing GTM systems

Support pre-implementation syncs by documenting prospect needs, configured workflows, signal preferences, data integration considerations, and success criteria

You will help Citylitics evaluate and deliver new data capabilities that align with customer and prospect needs.

Responsibilities include:


Own assigned data roadmap tickets tied to customer needs, prospect requests, emerging use cases, and future infrastructure verticals
Assess data feasibility for new markets, sectors, signal types, workflows, and customer use cases
Research potential data sources, collection approaches, classification methods, enrichment opportunities, and QA requirements for new data areas
Build and manage proof-of-concepts for new data areas that Citylitics is exploring or branching into
Document learnings from proof-of-concepts, including data availability, quality, coverage, repeatability, operational effort, and commercial relevance


Support Procurement-Stage Handoff

You will help ensure a clean transition from the sales process into implementation and long-term customer success.

Responsibilities include:


Document prospect needs, use cases, configured workflows, signal preferences, trial learnings, user feedback, success criteria, and data integration considerations
Support the handoff from Sales to Customer Success and Implementation when the deal reaches procurement stage
Share relevant context, configuration notes, trial engagement, value proof points, and implementation risks to reduce loss of context between sales and onboarding


Requirements

Must-Have Experience


Working experience with AI agents for research, data analysis, workflow configuration, documentation, collateral creation, or customer-facing deliverables is critical
Keen interest in infrastructure industries and data products and services
1-3 years of experience in data analysis, solutions consulting, implementation, customer success operations, product operations, market research, business analysis, or a similar role
Strong ability to analyze data and translate findings into clear business insights
Experience working directly with customers, prospects, or internal commercial teams
Comfort leading customer-facing conversations, data workshops, training sessions, or executive-level presentations
Strong communication skills, including the ability to explain data, methodology, data integration considerations, and workflow recommendations clearly
Strong comfort working with dashboards, reports, data exports, and analytical tools
Experience working with customer data environments and following data security and integration best practices
Ability to create clear collateral, summaries, and presentations using data
Strong organizational skills and ability to manage multiple sales cycles, trials, roadmap tickets, and internal requests at once


Nice-to-Have Experience


Experience in a data, SaaS, AI, market intelligence, sales intelligence, business intelligence, or research platform company
Experience supporting pre-sales, pilot, or trial processes
Experience conducting user training or customer enablement sessions
Experience creating market analytics, opportunity analyses, methodology collateral, or sales collateral
Experience scoping data integrations, implementation plans, data workflows, CRM workflows, or customer-specific configuration requirements
Experience with CRM integrations and data security is a major advantage
Experience supporting early-stage data product discovery, feasibility analysis, proof-of-concept development, or new-market data exploration


Benefits

What's in it for you?


Make real-world impact: Influence positive change across one of the biggest societal challenges of our generation: building and maintaining sustainable public infrastructure
Sell a genuinely differentiated platform: You'll represent a disruptive, data-driven solution with a compelling value proposition in a market that is eager to engage
Operate at the intersection of infrastructure, scale-up, and data science: We live where infrastructure meets AI and advanced data. There is no other team like ours in Toronto
Move fast, learn faster: There's no corporate bureaucracy here. You'll accomplish more in a few months than you would in years at a large, entrenched technology company
Be empowered by data and AI: We believe Data and AI will play an outsized role in the future of sales and decision-making. Every CityZen has access to generative AI tools and our full data universe to enhance productivity and encourage experimentation
Grow with intention: We invest deeply in our people through in-role coaching, skill-based development, an internal mentorship program, and clear internal promotion pathways
Win as a team: We work hard, we support one another, and we celebrate progress along the way. We're serious about solving infrastructure and just as serious about enjoying the journey together


Citylitics is an equal opportunity employer. We are passionate about providing a safe workplace where everyone is accepted and has the opportunity to grow with us. We are committed to making diversity and inclusivity part of our culture!

About Citylitics Inc.

Citylitics delivers predictive intelligence on where, how, and why infrastructure investments will occur across North America.

Infrastructure includes the foundational systems that shape our quality of life: water, transportation, transit, roads and highways, broadband, and power. It represents a multi-trillion-dollar market and a 20+ year megatrend in North America.

Citylitics transforms millions of documents across more than 40,000 cities, utilities, and public agencies into real-time market signals and high-value sales intelligence. Leading infrastructure vendors, engineering firms, and construction companies use the platform to identify and pre-position for major infrastructure opportunities earlier, helping them build relationships, shape project priorities, and influence scope before projects move to formal procurement.

Citylitics combines a network of AI agents, fine-tuned models, a proprietary infrastructure signals graph, and daily human-in-the-loop quality assurance workflows to deliver highly nuanced and reliable sales intelligence. The platform integrates seamlessly with CRMs, ERPs, and data lakes, embedding actionable insights directly into sales workflows so teams can prioritize the right pursuits and build stronger pipelines.

Our mission is to improve how infrastructure investments get made so critical needs can be addressed earlier and communities benefit from stronger, more resilient infrastructure.

Join Citylitics to work in a meaningful industry that will matter for decades to come while helping grow a company at the forefront of applied AI. We operate with a Results-Driven Mentality and a commitment to Raise The Bar, in a collaborative environment where Teamwork Makes the Dream Work as we tackle big challenges and celebrate wins together.

Who Are We Looking For?

Citylitics is looking for a Data Solutions Analyst to support our sales process by helping prospects understand how Citylitics' proprietary data, market signals, platform workflows, integrations, and purpose-built AI can help them pre-position earlier for future infrastructure projects, unlock business development capacity, and strengthen pricing workflows.

You will partner with Account Executives during active sales cycles to support sales intelligence workshops, answer data and methodology questions, scope and present data integration plans, configure trial accounts, train trial users, manage demo and trial environments, and create data-backed collateral.

You will also support data roadmap discovery and own proof-of-concepts for new data areas that align with customer needs, prospect requests, and future infrastructure verticals.

This role is ideal for someone who is analytical, customer-facing, commercially curious, and comfortable translating data into practical business value. You do not need to be a quota-carrying seller, but you should be comfortable working directly with prospects and customers during the sales process.

This is a hybrid role based out of Citylitics' Toronto office and requires 3+ days in-office per week.

What Will You Accomplish? / How You Will Make An Immediate Impact:

Support Sales Intelligence Workshops and Technical Discovery

You will support Account Executives with the data and integration expertise needed throughout the sales process.

Responsibilities include:

  • Support AEs with data and integration expertise during sales intelligence workshops and pre-implementation syncs

  • Advise prospects on relevant signals, indicators, data packages, product add-ons, filters, dashboards, feeds, alerts, and data methodology
  • Conduct deeper data and technical discovery during sales intelligence workshops to understand prospective customers' systems, data environments, workflows, and integration needs
  • Support AE-owned demos by ensuring demo environments, data feeds configuration, methodology explanations, and supporting materials are accurate, relevant, and ready for prospect conversations

Create Sales and Marketing Collateral that Strengthen Data Trust

You will create data-backed materials that support Citylitics' sales and marketing efforts while helping prospects understand the quality, relevance, and differentiation of Citylitics' data.

Responsibilities include:

  • Create market analytics, sample data, data stories, and proof points that support sales opportunities, executive conversations, trials, and marketing campaigns
  • Help quantify opportunity volume, account coverage, signal activity, and potential pipeline relevance for prospects
  • Support outbound and inbound marketing campaigns with relevant sample data, market examples, data stories, and other collateral needs
  • Build and maintain reusable assets, including sample reports, vertical-specific examples, sales intelligence workshop materials, trial templates, and AI prompts
  • Help prospects understand how Citylitics identifies nuanced signals often missed by generic search tools, broad data providers, bid platforms, or general AI tools
  • Create and maintain methodology collateral, FAQs, slides, talk tracks, and examples that support sales intelligence workshops, trials, and customer conversations

Configure Trials and Manage Demo Environments

You will help prospects experience a relevant, well-configured version of Citylitics during demos and trials, while ensuring demo environments are ready for AE-owned demos.

Responsibilities include:

  • Set up and configure trial accounts based on prospect needs, target markets, user roles, geographies, use cases, and signal preferences
  • Manage demo environments to ensure they remain accurate, current, and ready for AE-owned demos
  • Conduct 1:1 training calls to help trial users navigate the platform, interpret signals, use dashboards, and apply insights to their workflow
  • Monitor trial activity and support AEs in interpreting user engagement, adoption, feedback, workflow fit, and demonstrated value
  • Identify opportunities to improve trial configuration, demo environment readiness, training, and support processes

Title: Business Systems Analyst
Description:
Requisition ID: 260370

Join a purpose driven winning team, committed to results, in an inclusive and high-performing culture.

The Business Systems Analyst (BSA) for the Forms Free Teller (FFT) application plays a critical role in enabling seamless, paperless branch operations by translating business needs into functional and technical requirements for the FFT platform. The BSA acts as the key liaison between Branch Operations, Technology Delivery, Architecture, and downstream applications to ensure FFT supports efficient, compliant, and user‑friendly teller workflows.

Key Responsibilities


Requirements Gathering & Business Analysis
Engage with Branch Operations, Product Owners, and Customer Experience teams to understand branch workflows and identify opportunities for digitization within FFT.
Document business processes, usability needs, teller pain points, and regulatory requirements.
Create user stories, acceptance criteria, use cases, process flows, and FFT‑specific functional specifications.
Analyze the impact of changes on teller workflows, downstream systems, audit logging, and customer experience
Solution Design Support
Work with architecture and development teams to define the optimal technical solution that aligns with business objectives.
Participate in design discussions, impact assessments, and system integration planning.
Support API requirements, data mapping, and downstream/upstream system alignment.
Stakeholder Communication
Act as the primary liaison between business partners and IT teams, ensuring consistent communication and shared understanding.
Manage requirement walkthroughs, approvals, and sign-offs.
Testing & Validation
Develop test scenarios and support QA in validating the solution.
Perform UAT coordination, defect triage, and requirement traceability.
Ensure delivered features meet the accepted criteria and business objectives.
Project Execution & Support
Support project planning, sprint activities, backlog grooming, and change management.
Assist in readiness activities, documentation, training materials, and post‑implementation support.


Skills & Competencies


Deep understanding of branch banking workflows, teller operations, and regulatory requirements.
Strong skills in workflow mapping, process analysis, and user‑centered design.
Experience with API requirements, integration patterns, and multi‑system interactions.
Excellent communication and stakeholder‑management skills across business and technical teams.
Proficiency with Agile practices and tools like Azure DevOps / Jira.


Typical Deliverables


Business requirements documents (BRD)
Functional specifications / system requirement documents (FRD/SRD)
User stories and acceptance criteria
Process flows, data mapping, and sequence diagrams
Test scenarios, traceability matrices, and UAT support material


Location(s): Canada : Ontario : Toronto

Scotiabank is a leading bank in the Americas. Guided by our purpose: "for every future", we help our customers, their families and their communities achieve success through a broad range of advice, products and services, including personal and commercial banking, wealth management and private banking, corporate and investment banking, and capital markets.

At Scotiabank, we value the unique skills and experiences each individual brings to the Bank, and are committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive and accessible environment for everyone. If you require accommodation (including, but not limited to, an accessible interview site, alternate format documents, ASL Interpreter, or Assistive Technology) during the recruitment and selection process, please let our Recruitment team know. If you require technical assistance, please click here. Candidates must apply directly online to be considered for this role. We thank all applicants for their interest in a career at Scotiabank; however, only those candidates who are selected for an interview will be contacted.

Title: Data Analyst - Claims
Description:
Company: CGIC

Department: Claims

Employment Type: Regular Full-Time

Work Model: Hybrid (2 days in office)

Language: Bilingualism in English and French is an asset.

The Opportunity

We are a leading Canadian financial services co-operative committed to being a catalyst for a sustainable and resilient society and our team is essential to deliver on this strategy. That’s why we prioritize our people, to ensure we provide a strong culture and development opportunities which enables our team to thrive and to live our purpose. The best part is that you will work with people that care passionately about you, our clients and our communities.

Our Claims team aspires to create peace of mind for our clients and our communities. Our national team of knowledgeable and trusted professionals serve our clients with compassion. We are passionate about continuous improvement and operate with high-integrity, motivated by our desire to do the right thing for our clients.

As Data Analyst, you actively contribute to various initiatives by analyzing data to solve business problems and providing valuable insights. You understand the insurance business domain and ensure your own tasks contribute to meeting the business objectives by applying appropriate methods and critical thinking and performing analysis.

How You Will Create Impact


Perform descriptive and diagnostic analysis using recommended methods (ex: regression, cohort analysis, cluster analysis, A/B testing, factor analysis).
Design and develop metrics to enable descriptive analysis, monitoring and diagnostics for GenAI tools implemented within Claims.
Design proof of concept and deliver with guidance a prototype analytical solution to enable effective storytelling and descriptive/diagnostic analysis.
Work on Data querying (T-SQL, SQL, MySQL, etc.)
Extract, clean, reconcile and prepare the data required for the analysis.
Communicate findings to colleagues and your manager in an effective manner.



To Join Our Team


You have 1- 3 years of relevant experience in Business Intelligence or other relevant domain.
You have a University Degree in Business Intelligence, Computer Science, Actuarial Science, Mathematics, Statistics, or equivalent technical experience.
ACAS or FCAS designations are considered an asset.
You have experience solving standard problems by analyzing possible solutions by using our experience, judgment and precedent & challenging status quo.
You have good knowledge and understanding of data analysis, programing, modeling techniques, data warehouse and data management, research, architecture and design.
Experience with the following technologies is required: [TBD during Recruitment Discussion]
Experience with the following technologies is an asset: [TBD during Recruitment Discussion]



How You Will Succeed


You influence change and are committed to continuous improvement, in order to exceed client expectations.
You leverage critical thinking skills to identify problems and proactively propose solutions.
Your strong communication skills allow you to clearly convey messages.
You’re an effective team player who shares knowledge to support our peers.



What You Need To Know


You will be subject to a Background check as a condition of employment, in the event you are the successful candidate.



What’s in it for you?


Training and development opportunities to grow your career.
Flexible work options and paid time off to support your personal and family needs.
A holistic approach to your well-being, with physical and mental health programs and a supportive workplace culture.
Paid volunteer days to give back to your community.
In addition to our competitive salary and incentive programs, eligible employees also benefit from a comprehensive total rewards package including group retirement savings plans, pension and benefits (e.g., health and wellness, dental, disability and life coverage), mental health support and an employee assistance program.



Salary Information

Expected Salary range : CAD $60,574.00 to $100,956.00 CAD

The salary amount for the successful candidate is determined by Co-operators in its discretion and will vary depending on several criteria including but not limited to: local market conditions, geography and relevant job-related factors such as knowledge, skills, qualification, experience and education.

Employees may also have the opportunity to participate in incentive programs and earn additional compensation tied to individual and/or business performance, or other business metrics.

Title: Data Specialist, Assessment
Description:
Job description:



A day in the life of a Data Specialist at Esri Canada:

Design, develop, test, and maintain scalable, reliable data pipelines and data integration processes to support analytics, modeling, and product features.
Build and optimize data models, data stores, and transformation workflows to ensure data performance, accuracy, and maintainability.
Collect, ingest, clean, and integrate large volumes of structured and unstructured data from multiple internal and external sources, ensuring data quality, integrity, and analytical readiness.
Collaborate with software developers and platform teams to integrate data pipelines and analytics capabilities into production systems and enterprise software solutions.
Monitor, troubleshoot, and improve data pipeline performance, reliability, and scalability, contributing to operational stability and continuous improvement.
Develop, evaluate, and refine descriptive, predictive, and diagnostic models using statistical and machine learning techniques such as regression, classification, clustering, and time series analysis.

Title: Excel Expert
Description:
Excel Expert — Refactor & Fix 13‑Sheet Master Costing Workbook (NDA Required)




Project summary

Seeking an experienced Excel specialist to refactor, validate, and document a 13‑sheet Master Costing workbook. Work includes updating row/line‑item descriptions, adding rows, repairing and re-linking formulas across sheets, resolving errors, improving performance, and ensuring all totals/math reconcile. This is a confidential project — NDA required.




Key responsibilities

Review supplied sanitized workbook and identify structural/formula issues.
Revise row/line item descriptions and add rows where required, ensuring dependent formulas update correctly.
Repair, simplify, and re-link formulas across sheets so all calculations tabulate correctly (no #REF/#VALUE errors or unintended circular references).
Convert ranges to structured Tables and/or named ranges where appropriate to improve reliability.
Improve performance and robustness (use helper columns, INDEX/MATCH, dynamic arrays, Power Query where appropriate).
Provide change log and short user guide (1–2 pages) describing key changes and maintenance notes.
Deliver final, tested workbook and a brief acceptance checklist/scripts to verify totals.




Required skills & experience

Advanced Excel (formulas, array formulas, INDEX/MATCH, LOOKUPs, logical functions)
Experience with large multi‑sheet workbooks and formula auditing
Power Query, Power Pivot, VBA/Macros (preferred but not mandatory)
Familiarity with structured Tables, named ranges, dynamic arrays
Strong attention to detail and communication skills
Prior examples of similar workbook refactors (portfolio or case studies)




Deliverables

Updated workbook (sanitized or original per NDA) with all fixes implemented
Change log listing edits, added rows, and formula changes
Short user guide and testing/acceptance steps




Process & milestones

Test task (short) — correct and document fixes on single sheet excerpt (30–60 min).
Full scoping and fixed-price estimate or hourly proposal after test task.
Delivery milestone(s): core fixes → full workbook → QA & handover.

Timeline




Test task start immediately
Full project completion target 3days.




Budget

Provide hourly rate or fixed‑price estimate.




Security & confidentiality

Non Disclosure Agreemnt is mandatory.
Sensitive data will be sanitized for test; live file access handled via secure transfer or temporary restricted SharePoint/OneDrive link.




How to apply

Send:

Short cover message describing relevant experience.
Provide examples of similar Excel projects.
Hourly rate or fixed fee estimate and earliest start date.
Confirmation you will sign an NDA.
Paste your application or questions here or message me on LinkedIn. I’ll reply to qualified candidates.

Title: Information Technology Application Analyst
Description:
TMS Application Analyst – Brokerage Systems




Why you’ll love Canada Cartage




An iconic Canadian company with over 100 years of helping companies grow and succeed in Canada, we provide national dedicated fleet solutions, general freight services, managed transportation, fulfillment & distribution, and home delivery. With a national network of terminals, cross-docks, and distribution centers, we are one of Canada’s largest and most trusted supply chain service providers. Driven to exceed customer expectations, and supported by technology and innovation, we bring simplicity to complex logistics challenges.




We work with intelligent and focused leaders who are driven to create an outstanding customer experience in partnership with Canadian leading brands. Canada Cartage is deep rooted in family values with an entrepreneurial culture where ideas are welcomed, growth is fostered and high performance is championed. A recognized thought leader and founded in 1914, Canada Cartage dominates the industry by delivering innovative fleet solutions.




Position Summary




Reporting to the Manager, Transport Systems, Information Technology, the individual in this position will reside at one of our main hubs within Mississauga and will play a key role in supporting Canada Cartage’s applications and technology. The portfolio encompasses all application systems that support the various transportation businesses. The applications are a mix of 3rd party systems with some customizations and internally built applications.

The individual will administer the portfolio of transportation applications and support day-to-day issues in a timely manner. This is a key role in ensuring the ongoing smooth operation of the business and becomes the natural go-to person for both resources within the technical team and the business community. They will act as a Subject Matter Expert for internal initiatives relating to these applications and participate in projects involving the terminals.




Location: 6767 Davand Drive, Mississauga, Ontario




Schedule: Mon-Fri, 40 hours - Hybrid

Weekend and after hours support is required based on production issues and project timelines




What you will do

Provide Tier 2/3 Application Support by logging, prioritizing, and troubleshooting incidents via the ticketing system, collaborating with internal teams, and performing root cause analysis to ensure timely resolution and continuous improvement.
Oversee Application Health & Maintenance, coordinating with Infrastructure, Security, and vendor teams to ensure system updates, optimal performance, and compliance with IT standards.
Contribute to Project Delivery by deﬁning scope, gathering requirements, analyzing processes, managing project timelines, and adapting to changes in deliverables or priorities.
Act as a Subjec Matter Expert (SME) on supported applications, staying currenton product roadmaps, conﬁguring systems, and leading system testing, training, and SOP development.







Support Business Process Optimization through process mapping, documentation, system conﬁguration, and mentoring super users and L1 staff to ensure consistent application usage.
Travel as Needed to provide on-site support and user training at terminal locations across Canada during key project phases or go-lives.
Build and Maintain Relationships with business users, leadership, and vendors, communicating effectively across audiences and acting as a key liaison to drive adoption and resolve issues.




What you will Bring

5+ years of experience in Application Support, Business Analysis, or similar roles with strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Background in Brokerage systems and Supply Chain operations, with a solid understanding of Transportation Management Systems and related technologies.
Strong communicator (written, verbal, presentation) who thrives in cross-functional teams and works well with minimal supervision.
Technical knowledge of APIs, EDI, system integrations, and proﬁciency in Microsoft Office

tools (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio, Project).

Proactive learner and change leader with the ability to juggle support and project work while managing shifting priorities.
Educational background in a relevant post-secondary program (Engineering, Sciences, or IT-focused Business), with bonus points for Lean Six Sigma, SQL, BI tools like Power BI, and work management systems like Wrike.




Why people love working for Canada Cartage




Cohesive team and a positive, hard-working atmosphere.
Meaningful opportunities for career growth.
Company wide recognition initiatives designed to recognize employees when they demonstrate outstanding customer service, teamwork and our core values.
Perks - discounted hotels, car rentals, entertainment, gym memberships, phone plans, electronics, to name a few
Fun events throughout the year, like summer barbeques and driver-appreciation week that regularly bring us together.
We give back to the communities in which we operate by volunteering and donating to local charities.
We are a strong company that weathers the storm through pandemics and recessionary periods, so that we can benefit from good economic environments.




We are a top employer!




Canada Cartage has been recognized on the Forbes list of Canada’s Best Employers for six straight years in a row.




Trucking HR has announced that Canada Cartage has again been selected for the 8th year in a row, as one of Canada’s Top Fleet Employers. In addition, we have been recognized as an Employer of Distinction for the 4th year in a row. This serves to reinforce Canada Cartage’s reputation as an employer of high quality.







Come work with an industry leader!




Canada Cartage is committed to adhering to the objectives and requirements outlined in the Accessible Canada Act (ACA). We are determined in meeting the accessibility needs of persons with disabilities in a timely manner, through the implementation of the requirements of the ACA and its applicable regulations. If you require accommodation under the ACA at any point in your application or employment duration, please let us know.




We would like to thank all those expressing an interest in this position; however only the candidates selected for an interview will be contacted.

Title: Data Analyst Co-op - Enterprise Technology & Business Management
Description:
We’re building a relationship-oriented bank for the modern world. We need talented, passionate professionals who are dedicated to doing what’s right for our clients.

At CIBC, we embrace your strengths and your ambitions, so you are empowered at work. Our team members have what they need to make a meaningful impact and are truly valued for who they are and what they contribute.

To learn more about CIBC, please visit CIBC.com

What You’ll Be Doing

As a Data Analyst Co-op you will apply your knowledge and experience of data modelling to solve challenging business problems and bring innovative solutions. You’ll work closely with senior team members regarding BI and data analytics platforms for the organization. You’ll work with real world structured and unstructured datasets to produce end-to-end solutions, taking models from experiment to deployment. Additionally, you'll be exposed to various groups across the organization through collaboration and coordination on projects.

At CIBC we enable the work environment most optimal for you to thrive in your role. You’ll have the flexibility to manage your work activities within a hybrid work arrangement where you’ll spend 1-3 days per week on-site, while other days will be remote.

Important information

Please note, we have multiple positions available under this posting and you may be considered by more than one hiring team.


We may ask you to complete an attribute-based assessment and other skills tests (such as simulation, coding, French proficiency, MS Office). Our goal for the application process is to get to know more about you, all that you have to offer, and give you the opportunity to learn more about us.
You must be currently enrolled in post-secondary education and returning to full-time studies to be eligible. However, you do not need to be in a registered Co-Op program to be considered for a role.
Recruitment timeline: Applications review – May/June; Interviews – May/June; Offers - July.
Please include your resume (no more than 2 pages), a cover letter (no more than 1 page), and your most recent unofficial transcript with your application. These should all be uploaded into the Resume section of the application as one pdf document.
4 and 8 month opportunities available.


How You'll Succeed


Communication - You’ll use your excellent verbal and writing skills to effectively articulate ideas and opinions that involve evaluation, interpretation, and assessment.
Continuous learning - You actively seek new knowledge and you know that there is no limit to what you can achieve.
Accuracy - You’ll review processes and ensuring that the identified activities are followed in an accurate and precise manner.
Problem solving - You’ll apply business-relevant statistical, machine learning, or deep learning approaches to solve business challenges.
Data - You’ll extract, explore, and wrangle data from various sources/formats to appropriately prepare it for further study.


Who You Are


You're motivated by collective success. You know that teamwork can transform a good idea into a great one. You know that an inclusive team that enjoys working together can bring a vision to life.
You know that details matter. You notice things that others don't. Your critical thinking skills help to inform your decision making.
You give meaning to data. You enjoy investigating complex problems and making sense of information. You communicate detailed information in a meaningful way.
You're digitally savvy. You seek out innovative solutions and embrace evolving technologies. You can easily adapt to new tools and trends.
You act like an owner. You thrive when you're empowered to take initiative, go above and beyond, and deliver results.
Values matter to you. You bring your real self to work and you live our values - trust, teamwork, and accountability.


What CIBC Offers

At CIBC, your goals are a priority. We start with your strengths and ambitions as an employee and strive to create opportunities to tap into your potential.


We work to recognize you in meaningful, personalized ways including a competitive compensation, a banking benefit*, wellbeing support, and additional offers such as employee and family assistance programs and MomentMakers, our social, points-based recognition program.
Our spaces and technological toolkit will make it simple to bring together great minds to create innovative solutions that make a difference for our clients.
Subject to program terms and conditions


What You Need To Know


CIBC is committed to creating an inclusive environment where all team members and clients feel like they belong. We seek applicants with a wide range of abilities and we provide an accessible candidate experience. If you need accommodation, please contact Mailbox.careers-carrieres@cibc.com
CIBC is committed to clarity in our hiring process. All roles posted are opportunities we’re actively recruiting for, unless stated otherwise.
You need to be legally eligible to work at the location(s) specified above and, where applicable, must have a valid work or study permit.
We may ask you to complete an attribute-based assessment and other skills test (such as simulation, coding, French proficiency).
We use artificial intelligence tools during the recruitment process. Our goal for the application process is to get to know more about you, all that you have to offer, and give you the opportunity to learn more about us.


Job Location

Toronto-81 Bay, 27th Floor

Employment Type

Temporary (Fixed Term)

Weekly Hours

37.5

Skills

Analytical Thinking, Problem Solving

Title: Data Product Analyst
Description:
Overview

Adastra is seeking a driven and curious Data Product Analyst who is passionate about turning data into meaningful insights that shape business and product strategy. In this role, you will sit at the intersection of data, product, and business, helping teams unlock the full value of their data through scalable solutions, actionable analytics, and thoughtful storytelling.

You will have the opportunity to work on high-impact digital initiatives—analyzing user behavior, optimizing engagement and monetization strategies, and influencing decisions that directly impact customer experiences. This role is ideal for someone who thrives in a fast-paced environment, enjoys solving complex problems, and is excited to lead multiple analytics initiatives end-to-end, particularly within digital or sports & entertainment contexts.

Primary Location: Across Canada or USA

Work Model: Fully Remote

Employment Type: Contract

Vacancy Status: New

Responsibilities


Translate business requirements into data models, KPIs, and scalable data solutions
Build and maintain dashboards, reporting frameworks, and analytics tools
Perform deep-dive analysis on large datasets to support product and business decisions
Develop tracking strategies to improve user engagement, monetization, and retention
Partner with Product, Engineering, Marketing, and Business teams as a trusted advisor
Communicate complex insights clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Manage stakeholder expectations and drive alignment on data-driven initiatives
Lead and deliver multiple analytics projects simultaneously
Own end-to-end delivery from requirements gathering to insights delivery
Continuously improve data processes, workflows, and reporting capabilities
Analyze digital interactions across web, mobile, streaming, and ticketing platforms
Provide insights on user behavior, content performance, churn, and conversion trends
Support analysis of live events, campaigns, and seasonal trends



Qualifications, Skills & Experience


Bachelor’s degree in Analytics, Computer Science, Business, Statistics, or a related field
5+ years of experience in data analytics or product analytics
Experience working in digital and/or sports & entertainment environments
Advanced SQL expertise, including strong proficiency in window functions such as RANK, DENSE_RANK, ROW_NUMBER, LEAD, and LAG
Working knowledge of Python for data analysis
Experience with AWS data ecosystem, including AWS Glue and Amazon Redshift
Strong stakeholder management and cross-functional collaboration skills
Excellent written and verbal communication skills
Proven ability to manage and deliver multiple concurrent initiatives



NICE TO HAVE


Experience with dbt or other data transformation tools
Familiarity with A/B testing and experimentation frameworks
Exposure to modern data stack architecture and ELT pipelines



About Adastra

Adastra is a global leader in AI and data-driven transformation, helping organizations lead with artificial intelligence—responsibly, strategically, and at scale. With over 25 years of experience, Adastra empowers enterprise clients to unlock business value through data innovation, operational excellence, and smart customer engagement.

Trusted by some of the world’s most prominent brands, Adastra delivers end-to-end solutions grounded in thoughtful strategy, robust governance, and deep technical expertise. From defining vision to ensuring execution, Adastra guides organizations through every stage of their AI, data and cloud journey—building future-ready capabilities and delivering measurable, lasting impact.

Adastra serves clients across key industries including financial services, automotive, manufacturing, technology, media and telecom (TMT), healthcare, retail, and professional services. The company employs more than 2,000 professionals across North America, Europe, and Asia.

What We Offer


Opportunity for advancement and career progression
Competitive compensation package
Comprehensive benefits plan
Successful referral program
The opportunity to work with one of Canada’s 50 Best Managed Companies
Satisfaction of working for a reputable company
A flexible, dynamic, and diverse workplace



EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER

In our commitment to promote fair and equitable treatment of all employees and applicants, Adastra Corporation provides equal employment opportunities for all individuals regardless of age, sex, disability, race, ethnic origin, citizenship, creed, sexual orientation, marital status, or any other ground as described in the Ontario Human Rights Code. In addition, accommodation will be provided during the hiring process. Adastra Corporation’s implementation and support of employment initiatives, encourage diversified labour force participation and equal access to opportunities based on merit and performance.

AI Usage - Our hiring process includes the use of AI-enabled tools to screen applications (e.g., keyword matching, qualification ranking). A human recruiter reviews all AI-generated shortlists to make informed hiring decisions.

There has been an increased instance of fraudulent job offers coming from people posing as Adastra HR employees. Please note that Adastra will never request fees as part of our recruiting process and any emails sent to you that are not from ‘@adastragrp.com’ or ‘@talent.icims.com’ are fraudulent. All employment offers are sent via DocuSign and if you receive an employment offer from a suspicious email and not via DocuSign, it is fraudulent.

Contact careers@adastragrp.com to inquire about jobs at Adastra, to report a suspicious request for money or personal information from external websites or suspicious employment offers.

Title: Product Support Analyst
Description:
Founded in 1974, CMiC today delivers comprehensive and advanced enterprise and field operations solutions, purpose-built for construction and capital projects companies. CMiC's powerful software transforms how firms optimize productivity, minimize risk and drive growth by planning and managing all financials, projects, resources, and content assets - all from a single database platform.

In the past several years, the construction industry has experienced unprecedented changes driven by new technologies - including integration with multi-dimensional modeling, an explosion of cloud-based offerings and the demand for robust mobile capabilities. CMiC has kept pace by constantly upgrading and enhancing our advanced platform to reflect the changing needs of the industry, leading to significant growth as a company.

Position Scope

We are seeking enthusiastic, talented people to act as the first point of contact when our customers have inquiries or encounter issues as they use our software. Product Analysts receive tickets and analyse screenshots and details of customer issues, to replicate the problem while proactively communicating with CMiC teams to guide issues to resolution. In addition to providing a very high level of client service, you will develop strong relationships with clients and staff and provide feedback on documentation. Individuals applying for this position must be able to multi-task, have a strong attention to detail, professional demeanor, and be able to adapt into a growing and changing environment.

Important Information:


The overall hiring salary range for this position is $45,000 to $55,000 annually
This position is for an existing vacancy
We do not use AI to screen, assess, or select applicants


Duties & Responsibilities


Answer client queries and provide basic direction and training on common issues
Triage product queues and prioritize issues according to their severity
Conduct web meetings with customers to gather information for investigative purposes or to provide assistance
Test and duplicate issues in order to document and demonstrate the problem to other teams such as programming
Participate in project and team meetings; interacting and collaborating with team members
Leverage and contribute to the Knowledge Base to assist with the resolution of issues
Relay feedback to Product Managers so that the product can be improved
Provide feedback to supervisors and managers regarding processes that are unclear and need to be changed


This role is for you if:


You would rather spend most of your time on challenging issues rather than handling simple support requests
You want to engage in a collaborative environment with helpful co-workers and accessible leaders and managers
You prefer to work for a company that listens to its staff and continues to work toward being a Great Place To Work


Requirements


College/University Degree
Proficiency in spoken and written French
Excellent communication skills, both verbal & written
Exceptional analytical and problem-solving skills
A passion for exploring new software, ability to grasp new concepts and learn quickly
Extremely detail oriented with the ability to manage and prioritize workflow
Facilitate communication of information between team members and Product Groups
Previous technical support experience or customer service experience
Able to operate in a fast-paced environment
Team-player, strong work ethic and a positive attitude


Benefits


Competitive benefits Package (including Health & Dental benefits)
Paid vacation and personal days
Townhall meetings where all employees are encouraged to participate in open discussions
Located on York University's campus, easily accessible by transit (TTC, GO, etc.), walking distance to shopping and restaurants
Outdoor lunch space, including picnic tables
An active Social Events Committee (past events include annual seasonal parties, pool and bowling tournaments, karaoke nights, Game nights, BBQ's, and more)
Health and Wellness focus including virtual yoga classes and wellness webinars
RRSP Matching Program after 2 years of employment
Experience in a rapidly growing, socially responsible corporation


CMiC is an Equal Opportunity Employer. In accordance with the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act, 2005 and the Ontario Human Rights Code, CMiC will provide accommodation to applicants with disabilities throughout the recruitment, selection and/or assessment process. If selected to participate in the recruitment, selection and/or assessment process, please inform Human Resources staff of the nature of any accommodation(s) that you may require.
